types.name: Update
types.doc: This object represents an incoming update.At most one of the optional fields can be present in any given update.

fields.name: UpdateID
fields.doc: The update's unique identifier. Update identifiers start from a certain positive number and increase sequentially. This identifier becomes especially handy if you're using webhooks, since it allows you to ignore repeated updates or to restore the correct update sequence, should they get out of order. If there are no new updates for at least a week, then identifier of the next update will be chosen randomly instead of sequentially.

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Optional. New incoming message of any kind - text, photo, sticker, etc.

fields.name: EditedMessage
fields.doc: Optional. New version of a message that is known to the bot and was edited. This update may at times be triggered by changes to message fields that are either unavailable or not actively used by your bot.

fields.name: ChannelPost
fields.doc: Optional. New incoming channel post of any kind - text, photo, sticker, etc.

fields.name: EditedChannelPost
fields.doc: Optional. New version of a channel post that is known to the bot and was edited. This update may at times be triggered by changes to message fields that are either unavailable or not actively used by your bot.

fields.name: BusinessConnection
fields.doc: Optional. The bot was connected to or disconnected from a business account, or a user edited an existing connection with the bot

fields.name: BusinessMessage
fields.doc: Optional. New message from a connected business account

fields.name: EditedBusinessMessage
fields.doc: Optional. New version of a message from a connected business account

fields.name: DeletedBusinessMessages
fields.doc: Optional. Messages were deleted from a connected business account

fields.name: GuestMessage
fields.doc: Optional. New guest message. The bot can use the field Message.guest_query_id and the method answerGuestQuery to send a message in response.

fields.name: MessageReaction
fields.doc: Optional. A reaction to a message was changed by a user. The bot must be an administrator in the chat and must explicitly specify "message_reaction" in the list of allowed_updates to receive these updates. The update isn't received for reactions set by bots.

fields.name: MessageReactionCount
fields.doc: Optional. Reactions to a message with anonymous reactions were changed. The bot must be an administrator in the chat and must explicitly specify "message_reaction_count" in the list of allowed_updates to receive these updates. The updates are grouped and can be sent with delay up to a few minutes.

fields.name: InlineQuery
fields.doc: Optional. New incoming inline query

fields.name: ChosenInlineResult
fields.doc: Optional. The result of an inline query that was chosen by a user and sent to their chat partner. Please see our documentation on the feedback collecting for details on how to enable these updates for your bot.

fields.name: CallbackQuery
fields.doc: Optional. New incoming callback query

fields.name: ShippingQuery
fields.doc: Optional. New incoming shipping query. Only for invoices with flexible price.

fields.name: PreCheckoutQuery
fields.doc: Optional. New incoming pre-checkout query. Contains full information about checkout.

fields.name: PurchasedPaidMedia
fields.doc: Optional. A user purchased paid media with a non-empty payload sent by the bot in a non-channel chat

fields.name: Poll
fields.doc: Optional. New poll state. Bots receive only updates about manually stopped polls and polls, which are sent by the bot.

fields.name: PollAnswer
fields.doc: Optional. A user changed their answer in a non-anonymous poll. Bots receive new votes only in polls that were sent by the bot itself.

fields.name: MyChatMember
fields.doc: Optional. The bot's chat member status was updated in a chat. For private chats, this update is received only when the bot is blocked or unblocked by the user.

fields.name: ChatMember
fields.doc: Optional. A chat member's status was updated in a chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat and must explicitly specify "chat_member" in the list of allowed_updates to receive these updates.

fields.name: ChatJoinRequest
fields.doc: Optional. A request to join the chat has been sent. The bot must have the can_invite_users administrator right in the chat to receive these updates.

fields.name: ChatBoost
fields.doc: Optional. A chat boost was added or changed. The bot must be an administrator in the chat to receive these updates.

fields.name: RemovedChatBoost
fields.doc: Optional. A boost was removed from a chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat to receive these updates.

fields.name: ManagedBot
fields.doc: Optional. A new bot was created to be managed by the bot, or token or owner of a managed bot was changed

types.name: WebhookInfo
types.doc: Describes the current status of a webhook.

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: Webhook URL, may be empty if webhook is not set up

fields.name: HasCustomCertificate
fields.doc: True, if a custom certificate was provided for webhook certificate checks

fields.name: PendingUpdateCount
fields.doc: Number of updates awaiting delivery

fields.name: IPAddress
fields.doc: Optional. Currently used webhook IP address

fields.name: LastErrorDate
fields.doc: Optional. Unix time for the most recent error that happened when trying to deliver an update via webhook

fields.name: LastErrorMessage
fields.doc: Optional. Error message in human-readable format for the most recent error that happened when trying to deliver an update via webhook

fields.name: LastSynchronizationErrorDate
fields.doc: Optional. Unix time of the most recent error that happened when trying to synchronize available updates with Telegram datacenters

fields.name: MaxConnections
fields.doc: Optional. The maximum allowed number of simultaneous HTTPS connections to the webhook for update delivery

fields.name: AllowedUpdates
fields.doc: Optional. A list of update types the bot is subscribed to. Defaults to all update types except chat_member, message_reaction, and message_reaction_count.

types.name: User
types.doc: This object represents a Telegram user or bot.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this user or bot. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier.

fields.name: IsBot
fields.doc: True, if this user is a bot

fields.name: FirstName
fields.doc: User's or bot's first name

fields.name: LastName
fields.doc: Optional. User's or bot's last name

fields.name: Username
fields.doc: Optional. User's or bot's username

fields.name: LanguageCode
fields.doc: Optional. IETF language tag of the user's language

fields.name: IsPremium
fields.doc: Optional. True, if this user is a Telegram Premium user

fields.name: AddedToAttachmentMenu
fields.doc: Optional. True, if this user added the bot to the attachment menu

fields.name: CanJoinGroups
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can be invited to groups. Returned only in getMe.

fields.name: CanReadAllGroupMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if privacy mode is disabled for the bot. Returned only in getMe.

fields.name: SupportsGuestQueries
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot supports guest queries from chats it is not a member of. Returned only in getMe.

fields.name: SupportsInlineQueries
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot supports inline queries. Returned only in getMe.

fields.name: CanConnectToBusiness
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can be connected to a user account to manage it. Returned only in getMe.

fields.name: HasMainWebApp
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot has a main Web App. Returned only in getMe.

fields.name: HasTopicsEnabled
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot has forum topic mode enabled in private chats. Returned only in getMe.

fields.name: AllowsUsersToCreateTopics
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot allows users to create and delete topics in private chats. Returned only in getMe.

fields.name: CanManageBots
fields.doc: Optional. True, if other bots can be created to be controlled by the bot. Returned only in getMe.

types.name: Chat
types.doc: This object represents a chat.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this chat. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the chat, can be either “private”, “group”, “supergroup” or “channel”

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Optional. Title, for supergroups, channels and group chats

fields.name: Username
fields.doc: Optional. Username, for private chats, supergroups and channels if available

fields.name: FirstName
fields.doc: Optional. First name of the other party in a private chat

fields.name: LastName
fields.doc: Optional. Last name of the other party in a private chat

fields.name: IsForum
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the supergroup chat is a forum (has topics enabled)

fields.name: IsDirectMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the chat is the direct messages chat of a channel

types.name: ChatFullInfo
types.doc: This object contains full information about a chat.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this chat. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the chat, can be either “private”, “group”, “supergroup” or “channel”

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Optional. Title, for supergroups, channels and group chats

fields.name: Username
fields.doc: Optional. Username, for private chats, supergroups and channels if available

fields.name: FirstName
fields.doc: Optional. First name of the other party in a private chat

fields.name: LastName
fields.doc: Optional. Last name of the other party in a private chat

fields.name: IsForum
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the supergroup chat is a forum (has topics enabled)

fields.name: IsDirectMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the chat is the direct messages chat of a channel

fields.name: AccentColorID
fields.doc: Identifier of the accent color for the chat name and backgrounds of the chat photo, reply header, and link preview. See accent colors for more details.

fields.name: MaxReactionCount
fields.doc: The maximum number of reactions that can be set on a message in the chat

fields.name: Photo
fields.doc: Optional. Chat photo

fields.name: ActiveUsernames
fields.doc: Optional. If non-empty, the list of all active chat usernames; for private chats, supergroups and channels

fields.name: Birthdate
fields.doc: Optional. For private chats, the date of birth of the user

fields.name: BusinessIntro
fields.doc: Optional. For private chats with business accounts, the intro of the business

fields.name: BusinessLocation
fields.doc: Optional. For private chats with business accounts, the location of the business

fields.name: BusinessOpeningHours
fields.doc: Optional. For private chats with business accounts, the opening hours of the business

fields.name: PersonalChat
fields.doc: Optional. For private chats, the personal channel of the user

fields.name: ParentChat
fields.doc: Optional. Information about the corresponding channel chat; for direct messages chats only

fields.name: AvailableReactions
fields.doc: Optional. List of available reactions allowed in the chat. If omitted, then all emoji reactions are allowed.

fields.name: BackgroundCustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Optional. Custom emoji identifier of the emoji chosen by the chat for the reply header and link preview background

fields.name: ProfileAccentColorID
fields.doc: Optional. Identifier of the accent color for the chat's profile background. See profile accent colors for more details.

fields.name: ProfileBackgroundCustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Optional. Custom emoji identifier of the emoji chosen by the chat for its profile background

fields.name: EmojiStatusCustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Optional. Custom emoji identifier of the emoji status of the chat or the other party in a private chat

fields.name: EmojiStatusExpirationDate
fields.doc: Optional. Expiration date of the emoji status of the chat or the other party in a private chat, in Unix time, if any

fields.name: Bio
fields.doc: Optional. Bio of the other party in a private chat

fields.name: HasPrivateForwards
fields.doc: Optional. True, if privacy settings of the other party in the private chat allows to use tg://user?id=<user_id> links only in chats with the user

fields.name: HasRestrictedVoiceAndVideoMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the privacy settings of the other party restrict sending voice and video note messages in the private chat

fields.name: JoinToSendMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if users need to join the supergroup before they can send messages

fields.name: JoinByRequest
fields.doc: Optional. True, if all users directly joining the supergroup without using an invite link need to be approved by supergroup administrators

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: Optional. Description, for groups, supergroups and channel chats

fields.name: InviteLink
fields.doc: Optional. Primary invite link, for groups, supergroups and channel chats

fields.name: PinnedMessage
fields.doc: Optional. The most recent pinned message (by sending date)

fields.name: Permissions
fields.doc: Optional. Default chat member permissions, for groups and supergroups

fields.name: AcceptedGiftTypes
fields.doc: Information about types of gifts that are accepted by the chat or by the corresponding user for private chats

fields.name: CanSendPaidMedia
fields.doc: Optional. True, if paid media messages can be sent or forwarded to the channel chat. The field is available only for channel chats.

fields.name: SlowModeDelay
fields.doc: Optional. For supergroups, the minimum allowed delay between consecutive messages sent by each unprivileged user; in seconds

fields.name: UnrestrictBoostCount
fields.doc: Optional. For supergroups, the minimum number of boosts that a non-administrator user needs to add in order to ignore slow mode and chat permissions

fields.name: MessageAutoDeleteTime
fields.doc: Optional. The time after which all messages sent to the chat will be automatically deleted; in seconds

fields.name: HasAggressiveAntiSpamEnabled
fields.doc: Optional. True, if aggressive anti-spam checks are enabled in the supergroup. The field is only available to chat administrators.

fields.name: HasHiddenMembers
fields.doc: Optional. True, if non-administrators can only get the list of bots and administrators in the chat

fields.name: HasProtectedContent
fields.doc: Optional. True, if messages from the chat can't be forwarded to other chats

fields.name: HasVisibleHistory
fields.doc: Optional. True, if new chat members will have access to old messages; available only to chat administrators

fields.name: StickerSetName
fields.doc: Optional. For supergroups, name of the group sticker set

fields.name: CanSetStickerSet
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can change the group sticker set

fields.name: CustomEmojiStickerSetName
fields.doc: Optional. For supergroups, the name of the group's custom emoji sticker set. Custom emoji from this set can be used by all users and bots in the group.

fields.name: LinkedChatID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier for the linked chat, i.e. the discussion group identifier for a channel and vice versa; for supergroups and channel chats. This identifier may be greater than 32 bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it is smaller than 52 bits, so a signed 64 bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier.

fields.name: Location
fields.doc: Optional. For supergroups, the location to which the supergroup is connected

fields.name: Rating
fields.doc: Optional. For private chats, the rating of the user if any

fields.name: FirstProfileAudio
fields.doc: Optional. For private chats, the first audio added to the profile of the user

fields.name: UniqueGiftColors
fields.doc: Optional. The color scheme based on a unique gift that must be used for the chat's name, message replies and link previews

fields.name: PaidMessageStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of Telegram Stars a general user has to pay to send a message to the chat

types.name: Message
types.doc: This object represents a message.

fields.name: MessageID
fields.doc: Unique message identifier inside this chat. In specific instances (e.g., message containing a video sent to a big chat), the server might automatically schedule a message instead of sending it immediately. In such cases, this field will be 0 and the relevant message will be unusable until it is actually sent.

fields.name: MessageThreadID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier of a message thread or forum topic to which the message belongs; for supergroups and private chats only

fields.name: DirectMessagesTopic
fields.doc: Optional. Information about the direct messages chat topic that contains the message

fields.name: From
fields.doc: Optional. Sender of the message; may be empty for messages sent to channels. For backward compatibility, if the message was sent on behalf of a chat, the field contains a fake sender user in non-channel chats.

fields.name: SenderChat
fields.doc: Optional. Sender of the message when sent on behalf of a chat. For example, the supergroup itself for messages sent by its anonymous administrators or a linked channel for messages automatically forwarded to the channel's discussion group. For backward compatibility, if the message was sent on behalf of a chat, the field from contains a fake sender user in non-channel chats.

fields.name: SenderBoostCount
fields.doc: Optional. If the sender of the message boosted the chat, the number of boosts added by the user

fields.name: SenderBusinessBot
fields.doc: Optional. The bot that actually sent the message on behalf of the business account. Available only for outgoing messages sent on behalf of the connected business account.

fields.name: SenderTag
fields.doc: Optional. Tag or custom title of the sender of the message; for supergroups only

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date the message was sent in Unix time. It is always a positive number, representing a valid date.

fields.name: GuestQueryID
fields.doc: Optional. The unique identifier for the guest query. Use this identifier with the method answerGuestQuery to send a response message. If non-empty, the message belongs to the chat where the guest bot was summoned, which may not coincide with other existing bot chats sharing the same identifier.

fields.name: BusinessConnectionID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier of the business connection from which the message was received. If non-empty, the message belongs to a chat of the corresponding business account that is independent from any potential bot chat which might share the same identifier.

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: Chat the message belongs to

fields.name: ForwardOrigin
fields.doc: Optional. Information about the original message for forwarded messages

fields.name: IsTopicMessage
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the message is sent to a topic in a forum supergroup or a private chat with the bot

fields.name: IsAutomaticForward
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the message is a channel post that was automatically forwarded to the connected discussion group

fields.name: ReplyToMessage
fields.doc: Optional. For replies in the same chat and message thread, the original message. Note that the Message object in this field will not contain further reply_to_message fields even if it itself is a reply.

fields.name: ExternalReply
fields.doc: Optional. Information about the message that is being replied to, which may come from another chat or forum topic

fields.name: Quote
fields.doc: Optional. For replies that quote part of the original message, the quoted part of the message

fields.name: ReplyToStory
fields.doc: Optional. For replies to a story, the original story

fields.name: ReplyToChecklistTaskID
fields.doc: Optional. Identifier of the specific checklist task that is being replied to

fields.name: ReplyToPollOptionID
fields.doc: Optional. Persistent identifier of the specific poll option that is being replied to

fields.name: ViaBot
fields.doc: Optional. Bot through which the message was sent

fields.name: GuestBotCallerUser
fields.doc: Optional. For a message sent by a guest bot, this is the user whose original message triggered the bot's response

fields.name: GuestBotCallerChat
fields.doc: Optional. For a message sent by a guest bot, this is the chat whose original message triggered the bot's response

fields.name: EditDate
fields.doc: Optional. Date the message was last edited in Unix time

fields.name: HasProtectedContent
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the message can't be forwarded

fields.name: IsFromOffline
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the message was sent by an implicit action, for example, as an away or a greeting business message, or as a scheduled message

fields.name: IsPaidPost
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the message is a paid post. Note that such posts must not be deleted for 24 hours to receive the payment and can't be edited.

fields.name: MediaGroupID
fields.doc: Optional. The unique identifier inside this chat of a media message group this message belongs to

fields.name: AuthorSignature
fields.doc: Optional. Signature of the post author for messages in channels, or the custom title of an anonymous group administrator

fields.name: PaidStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of Telegram Stars that were paid by the sender of the message to send it

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Optional. For text messages, the actual UTF-8 text of the message

fields.name: Entities
fields.doc: Optional. For text messages, special entities like usernames, URLs, bot commands, etc. that appear in the text

fields.name: LinkPreviewOptions
fields.doc: Optional. Options used for link preview generation for the message, if it is a text message and link preview options were changed

fields.name: SuggestedPostInfo
fields.doc: Optional. Information about suggested post parameters if the message is a suggested post in a channel direct messages chat. If the message is an approved or declined suggested post, then it can't be edited.

fields.name: EffectID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier of the message effect added to the message

fields.name: Animation
fields.doc: Optional. Message is an animation, information about the animation. For backward compatibility, when this field is set, the document field will also be set.

fields.name: Audio
fields.doc: Optional. Message is an audio file, information about the file

fields.name: Document
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a general file, information about the file

fields.name: LivePhoto
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a live photo, information about the live photo. For backward compatibility, when this field is set, the photo field will also be set.

fields.name: PaidMedia
fields.doc: Optional. Message contains paid media; information about the paid media

fields.name: Photo
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a photo, available sizes of the photo

fields.name: Sticker
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a sticker, information about the sticker

fields.name: Story
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a forwarded story

fields.name: Video
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a video, information about the video

fields.name: VideoNote
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a video note, information about the video message

fields.name: Voice
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a voice message, information about the file

fields.name: Caption
fields.doc: Optional. Caption for the animation, audio, document, paid media, photo, video or voice

fields.name: CaptionEntities
fields.doc: Optional. For messages with a caption, special entities like usernames, URLs, bot commands, etc. that appear in the caption

fields.name: ShowCaptionAboveMedia
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the caption must be shown above the message media

fields.name: HasMediaSpoiler
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the message media is covered by a spoiler animation

fields.name: Checklist
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a checklist

fields.name: Contact
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a shared contact, information about the contact

fields.name: Dice
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a dice with random value

fields.name: Game
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a game, information about the game. More about games »

fields.name: Poll
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a native poll, information about the poll

fields.name: Venue
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a venue, information about the venue. For backward compatibility, when this field is set, the location field will also be set.

fields.name: Location
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a shared location, information about the location

fields.name: NewChatMembers
fields.doc: Optional. New members that were added to the group or supergroup and information about them (the bot itself may be one of these members)

fields.name: LeftChatMember
fields.doc: Optional. A member was removed from the group, information about them (this member may be the bot itself)

fields.name: ChatOwnerLeft
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: chat owner has left

fields.name: ChatOwnerChanged
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: chat owner has changed

fields.name: NewChatTitle
fields.doc: Optional. A chat title was changed to this value

fields.name: NewChatPhoto
fields.doc: Optional. A chat photo was change to this value

fields.name: DeleteChatPhoto
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: the chat photo was deleted

fields.name: GroupChatCreated
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: the group has been created

fields.name: SupergroupChatCreated
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: the supergroup has been created. This field can't be received in a message coming through updates, because bot can't be a member of a supergroup when it is created. It can only be found in reply_to_message if someone replies to a very first message in a directly created supergroup.

fields.name: ChannelChatCreated
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: the channel has been created. This field can't be received in a message coming through updates, because bot can't be a member of a channel when it is created. It can only be found in reply_to_message if someone replies to a very first message in a channel.

fields.name: MessageAutoDeleteTimerChanged
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: auto-delete timer settings changed in the chat

fields.name: MigrateToChatID
fields.doc: Optional. The group has been migrated to a supergroup with the specified identifier. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier.

fields.name: MigrateFromChatID
fields.doc: Optional. The supergroup has been migrated from a group with the specified identifier. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier.

fields.name: PinnedMessage
fields.doc: Optional. Specified message was pinned. Note that the Message object in this field will not contain further reply_to_message fields even if it itself is a reply.

fields.name: Invoice
fields.doc: Optional. Message is an invoice for a payment, information about the invoice. More about payments »

fields.name: SuccessfulPayment
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a service message about a successful payment, information about the payment. More about payments »

fields.name: RefundedPayment
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a service message about a refunded payment, information about the payment. More about payments »

fields.name: UsersShared
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: users were shared with the bot

fields.name: ChatShared
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: a chat was shared with the bot

fields.name: Gift
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: a regular gift was sent or received

fields.name: UniqueGift
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: a unique gift was sent or received

fields.name: GiftUpgradeSent
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: upgrade of a gift was purchased after the gift was sent

fields.name: ConnectedWebsite
fields.doc: Optional. The domain name of the website on which the user has logged in. More about Telegram Login »

fields.name: WriteAccessAllowed
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: the user allowed the bot to write messages after adding it to the attachment or side menu, launching a Web App from a link, or accepting an explicit request from a Web App sent by the method requestWriteAccess

fields.name: PassportData
fields.doc: Optional. Telegram Passport data

fields.name: ProximityAlertTriggered
fields.doc: Optional. Service message. A user in the chat triggered another user's proximity alert while sharing Live Location.

fields.name: BoostAdded
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: user boosted the chat

fields.name: ChatBackgroundSet
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: chat background set

fields.name: ChecklistTasksDone
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: some tasks in a checklist were marked as done or not done

fields.name: ChecklistTasksAdded
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: tasks were added to a checklist

fields.name: DirectMessagePriceChanged
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: the price for paid messages in the corresponding direct messages chat of a channel has changed

fields.name: ForumTopicCreated
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: forum topic created

fields.name: ForumTopicEdited
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: forum topic edited

fields.name: ForumTopicClosed
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: forum topic closed

fields.name: ForumTopicReopened
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: forum topic reopened

fields.name: GeneralForumTopicHidden
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: the 'General' forum topic hidden

fields.name: GeneralForumTopicUnhidden
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: the 'General' forum topic unhidden

fields.name: GiveawayCreated
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: a scheduled giveaway was created

fields.name: Giveaway
fields.doc: Optional. The message is a scheduled giveaway message

fields.name: GiveawayWinners
fields.doc: Optional. A giveaway with public winners was completed

fields.name: GiveawayCompleted
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: a giveaway without public winners was completed

fields.name: ManagedBotCreated
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: user created a bot that will be managed by the current bot

fields.name: PaidMessagePriceChanged
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: the price for paid messages has changed in the chat

fields.name: PollOptionAdded
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: answer option was added to a poll

fields.name: PollOptionDeleted
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: answer option was deleted from a poll

fields.name: SuggestedPostApproved
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: a suggested post was approved

fields.name: SuggestedPostApprovalFailed
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: approval of a suggested post has failed

fields.name: SuggestedPostDeclined
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: a suggested post was declined

fields.name: SuggestedPostPaid
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: payment for a suggested post was received

fields.name: SuggestedPostRefunded
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: payment for a suggested post was refunded

fields.name: VideoChatScheduled
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: video chat scheduled

fields.name: VideoChatStarted
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: video chat started

fields.name: VideoChatEnded
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: video chat ended

fields.name: VideoChatParticipantsInvited
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: new participants invited to a video chat

fields.name: WebAppData
fields.doc: Optional. Service message: data sent by a Web App

fields.name: ReplyMarkup
fields.doc: Optional. Inline keyboard attached to the message. login_url buttons are represented as ordinary url buttons.

types.name: MessageId
types.doc: This object represents a unique message identifier.

fields.name: MessageID
fields.doc: Unique message identifier. In specific instances (e.g., message containing a video sent to a big chat), the server might automatically schedule a message instead of sending it immediately. In such cases, this field will be 0 and the relevant message will be unusable until it is actually sent.

types.name: InaccessibleMessage
types.doc: This object describes a message that was deleted or is otherwise inaccessible to the bot.

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: Chat the message belonged to

fields.name: MessageID
fields.doc: Unique message identifier inside the chat

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Always 0. The field can be used to differentiate regular and inaccessible messages.

types.name: MaybeInaccessibleMessage
types.doc: This object describes a message that can be inaccessible to the bot. It can be one of

types.name: MessageEntity
types.doc: This object represents one special entity in a text message. For example, hashtags, usernames, URLs, etc.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the entity. Currently, can be “mention” (@username), “hashtag” (#hashtag or #hashtag@chatusername), “cashtag” ($USD or $USD@chatusername), “bot_command” (/start@jobs_bot), “url” (https://telegram.org), “email” (do-not-reply@telegram.org), “phone_number” (+1-212-555-0123), “bold” (bold text), “italic” (italic text), “underline” (underlined text), “strikethrough” (strikethrough text), “spoiler” (spoiler message), “blockquote” (block quotation), “expandable_blockquote” (collapsed-by-default block quotation), “code” (monowidth string), “pre” (monowidth block), “text_link” (for clickable text URLs), “text_mention” (for users without usernames), “custom_emoji” (for inline custom emoji stickers), or “date_time” (for formatted date and time).

fields.name: Offset
fields.doc: Offset in UTF-16 code units to the start of the entity

fields.name: Length
fields.doc: Length of the entity in UTF-16 code units

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: Optional. For “text_link” only, URL that will be opened after user taps on the text

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Optional. For “text_mention” only, the mentioned user

fields.name: Language
fields.doc: Optional. For “pre” only, the programming language of the entity text

fields.name: CustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Optional. For “custom_emoji” only, unique identifier of the custom emoji. Use getCustomEmojiStickers to get full information about the sticker.

fields.name: UnixTime
fields.doc: Optional. For “date_time” only, the Unix time associated with the entity

fields.name: DateTimeFormat
fields.doc: Optional. For “date_time” only, the string that defines the formatting of the date and time. See date-time entity formatting for more details.

types.name: TextQuote
types.doc: This object contains information about the quoted part of a message that is replied to by the given message.

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Text of the quoted part of a message that is replied to by the given message

fields.name: Entities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities that appear in the quote. Currently, only bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, spoiler, custom_emoji, and date_time entities are kept in quotes.

fields.name: Position
fields.doc: Approximate quote position in the original message in UTF-16 code units as specified by the sender

fields.name: IsManual
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the quote was chosen manually by the message sender. Otherwise, the quote was added automatically by the server.

types.name: ExternalReplyInfo
types.doc: This object contains information about a message that is being replied to, which may come from another chat or forum topic.

fields.name: Origin
fields.doc: Origin of the message replied to by the given message

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: Optional. Chat the original message belongs to. Available only if the chat is a supergroup or a channel.

fields.name: MessageID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique message identifier inside the original chat. Available only if the original chat is a supergroup or a channel.

fields.name: LinkPreviewOptions
fields.doc: Optional. Options used for link preview generation for the original message, if it is a text message

fields.name: Animation
fields.doc: Optional. Message is an animation, information about the animation

fields.name: Audio
fields.doc: Optional. Message is an audio file, information about the file

fields.name: Document
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a general file, information about the file

fields.name: LivePhoto
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a live photo, information about the live photo

fields.name: PaidMedia
fields.doc: Optional. Message contains paid media; information about the paid media

fields.name: Photo
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a photo, available sizes of the photo

fields.name: Sticker
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a sticker, information about the sticker

fields.name: Story
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a forwarded story

fields.name: Video
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a video, information about the video

fields.name: VideoNote
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a video note, information about the video message

fields.name: Voice
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a voice message, information about the file

fields.name: HasMediaSpoiler
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the message media is covered by a spoiler animation

fields.name: Checklist
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a checklist

fields.name: Contact
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a shared contact, information about the contact

fields.name: Dice
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a dice with random value

fields.name: Game
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a game, information about the game. More about games »

fields.name: Giveaway
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a scheduled giveaway, information about the giveaway

fields.name: GiveawayWinners
fields.doc: Optional. A giveaway with public winners was completed

fields.name: Invoice
fields.doc: Optional. Message is an invoice for a payment, information about the invoice. More about payments »

fields.name: Location
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a shared location, information about the location

fields.name: Poll
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a native poll, information about the poll

fields.name: Venue
fields.doc: Optional. Message is a venue, information about the venue

types.name: ReplyParameters
types.doc: Describes reply parameters for the message that is being sent.

fields.name: MessageID
fields.doc: Identifier of the message that will be replied to in the current chat, or in the chat chat_id if it is specified

fields.name: ChatID
fields.doc: Optional. If the message to be replied to is from a different chat, unique identifier for the chat or username of the bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username. Not supported for messages sent on behalf of a business account and messages from channel direct messages chats.

fields.name: AllowSendingWithoutReply
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the message should be sent even if the specified message to be replied to is not found. Always False for replies in another chat or forum topic. Always True for messages sent on behalf of a business account.

fields.name: Quote
fields.doc: Optional. Quoted part of the message to be replied to; 0-1024 characters after entities parsing. The quote must be an exact substring of the message to be replied to, including bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, spoiler, custom_emoji, and date_time entities. The message will fail to send if the quote isn't found in the original message.

fields.name: QuoteParseMode
fields.doc: Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the quote. See formatting options for more details.

fields.name: QuoteEntities
fields.doc: Optional. A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the quote. It can be specified instead of quote_parse_mode.

fields.name: QuotePosition
fields.doc: Optional. Position of the quote in the original message in UTF-16 code units

fields.name: ChecklistTaskID
fields.doc: Optional. Identifier of the specific checklist task to be replied to

fields.name: PollOptionID
fields.doc: Optional. Persistent identifier of the specific poll option to be replied to

types.name: MessageOrigin
types.doc: This object describes the origin of a message. It can be one of

types.name: MessageOriginUser
types.doc: The message was originally sent by a known user.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the message origin, always “user”

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date the message was sent originally in Unix time

fields.name: SenderUser
fields.doc: User that sent the message originally

types.name: MessageOriginHiddenUser
types.doc: The message was originally sent by an unknown user.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the message origin, always “hidden_user”

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date the message was sent originally in Unix time

fields.name: SenderUserName
fields.doc: Name of the user that sent the message originally

types.name: MessageOriginChat
types.doc: The message was originally sent on behalf of a chat to a group chat.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the message origin, always “chat”

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date the message was sent originally in Unix time

fields.name: SenderChat
fields.doc: Chat that sent the message originally

fields.name: AuthorSignature
fields.doc: Optional. For messages originally sent by an anonymous chat administrator, original message author signature

types.name: MessageOriginChannel
types.doc: The message was originally sent to a channel chat.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the message origin, always “channel”

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date the message was sent originally in Unix time

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: Channel chat to which the message was originally sent

fields.name: MessageID
fields.doc: Unique message identifier inside the chat

fields.name: AuthorSignature
fields.doc: Optional. Signature of the original post author

types.name: PhotoSize
types.doc: This object represents one size of a photo or a file / sticker thumbnail.

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: Width
fields.doc: Photo width

fields.name: Height
fields.doc: Photo height

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: Optional. File size in bytes

types.name: Animation
types.doc: This object represents an animation file (GIF or H.264/MPEG-4 AVC video without sound).

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: Width
fields.doc: Video width as defined by the sender

fields.name: Height
fields.doc: Video height as defined by the sender

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Duration of the video in seconds as defined by the sender

fields.name: Thumbnail
fields.doc: Optional. Animation thumbnail as defined by the sender

fields.name: FileName
fields.doc: Optional. Original animation filename as defined by the sender

fields.name: MimeType
fields.doc: Optional. MIME type of the file as defined by the sender

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: Optional. File size in bytes. It can be bigger than 2^31 and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this value.

types.name: Audio
types.doc: This object represents an audio file to be treated as music by the Telegram clients.

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Duration of the audio in seconds as defined by the sender

fields.name: Performer
fields.doc: Optional. Performer of the audio as defined by the sender or by audio tags

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Optional. Title of the audio as defined by the sender or by audio tags

fields.name: FileName
fields.doc: Optional. Original filename as defined by the sender

fields.name: MimeType
fields.doc: Optional. MIME type of the file as defined by the sender

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: Optional. File size in bytes. It can be bigger than 2^31 and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this value.

fields.name: Thumbnail
fields.doc: Optional. Thumbnail of the album cover to which the music file belongs

types.name: Document
types.doc: This object represents a general file (as opposed to photos, voice messages and audio files).

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: Thumbnail
fields.doc: Optional. Document thumbnail as defined by the sender

fields.name: FileName
fields.doc: Optional. Original filename as defined by the sender

fields.name: MimeType
fields.doc: Optional. MIME type of the file as defined by the sender

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: Optional. File size in bytes. It can be bigger than 2^31 and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this value.

types.name: LivePhoto
types.doc: This object represents a live photo.

fields.name: Photo
fields.doc: Optional. Available sizes of the corresponding static photo

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for the video file which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for the video file which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: Width
fields.doc: Video width as defined by the sender

fields.name: Height
fields.doc: Video height as defined by the sender

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Duration of the video in seconds as defined by the sender

fields.name: MimeType
fields.doc: Optional. MIME type of the file as defined by the sender

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: Optional. File size in bytes. It can be bigger than 2^31 and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this value.

types.name: Story
types.doc: This object represents a story.

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: Chat that posted the story

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for the story in the chat

types.name: VideoQuality
types.doc: This object represents a video file of a specific quality.

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: Width
fields.doc: Video width

fields.name: Height
fields.doc: Video height

fields.name: Codec
fields.doc: Codec that was used to encode the video, for example, “h264”, “h265”, or “av01”

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: Optional. File size in bytes. It can be bigger than 2^31 and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this value.

types.name: Video
types.doc: This object represents a video file.

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: Width
fields.doc: Video width as defined by the sender

fields.name: Height
fields.doc: Video height as defined by the sender

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Duration of the video in seconds as defined by the sender

fields.name: Thumbnail
fields.doc: Optional. Video thumbnail

fields.name: Cover
fields.doc: Optional. Available sizes of the cover of the video in the message

fields.name: StartTimestamp
fields.doc: Optional. Timestamp in seconds from which the video will play in the message

fields.name: Qualities
fields.doc: Optional. List of available qualities of the video

fields.name: FileName
fields.doc: Optional. Original filename as defined by the sender

fields.name: MimeType
fields.doc: Optional. MIME type of the file as defined by the sender

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: Optional. File size in bytes. It can be bigger than 2^31 and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this value.

types.name: VideoNote
types.doc: This object represents a video message (available in Telegram apps as of v.4.0).

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: Length
fields.doc: Video width and height (diameter of the video message) as defined by the sender

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Duration of the video in seconds as defined by the sender

fields.name: Thumbnail
fields.doc: Optional. Video thumbnail

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: Optional. File size in bytes

types.name: Voice

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Duration of the audio in seconds as defined by the sender

fields.name: MimeType
fields.doc: Optional. MIME type of the file as defined by the sender

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: Optional. File size in bytes. It can be bigger than 2^31 and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this value.

types.name: PaidMediaInfo
types.doc: Describes the paid media added to a message.

fields.name: StarCount
fields.doc: The number of Telegram Stars that must be paid to buy access to the media

fields.name: PaidMedia
fields.doc: Information about the paid media

types.name: PaidMedia
types.doc: This object describes paid media. Currently, it can be one of

types.name: PaidMediaLivePhoto
types.doc: The paid media is a live photo.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the paid media, always “live_photo”

fields.name: LivePhoto
fields.doc: The photo

types.name: PaidMediaPhoto
types.doc: The paid media is a photo.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the paid media, always “photo”

fields.name: Photo
fields.doc: The photo

types.name: PaidMediaPreview
types.doc: The paid media isn't available before the payment.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the paid media, always “preview”

fields.name: Width
fields.doc: Optional. Media width as defined by the sender

fields.name: Height
fields.doc: Optional. Media height as defined by the sender

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Optional. Duration of the media in seconds as defined by the sender

types.name: PaidMediaVideo
types.doc: The paid media is a video.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the paid media, always “video”

fields.name: Video
fields.doc: The video

types.name: Contact
types.doc: This object represents a phone contact.

fields.name: PhoneNumber
fields.doc: Contact's phone number

fields.name: FirstName
fields.doc: Contact's first name

fields.name: LastName
fields.doc: Optional. Contact's last name

fields.name: UserID
fields.doc: Optional. Contact's user identifier in Telegram. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier.

fields.name: Vcard
fields.doc: Optional. Additional data about the contact in the form of a vCard

types.name: Dice
types.doc: This object represents an animated emoji that displays a random value.

fields.name: Emoji
fields.doc: Emoji on which the dice throw animation is based

fields.name: Value
fields.doc: Value of the dice, 1-6 for “”, “” and “” base emoji, 1-5 for “” and “” base emoji, 1-64 for “” base emoji

types.name: PollMedia
types.doc: At most one of the optional fields can be present in any given object.

fields.name: Animation
fields.doc: Optional. Media is an animation, information about the animation

fields.name: Audio
fields.doc: Optional. Media is an audio file, information about the file; currently, can't be received in a poll option

fields.name: Document
fields.doc: Optional. Media is a general file, information about the file; currently, can't be received in a poll option

fields.name: LivePhoto
fields.doc: Optional. Media is a live photo, information about the live photo

fields.name: Location
fields.doc: Optional. Media is a shared location, information about the location

fields.name: Photo
fields.doc: Optional. Media is a photo, available sizes of the photo

fields.name: Sticker
fields.doc: Optional. Media is a sticker, information about the sticker; currently, for poll options only

fields.name: Venue
fields.doc: Optional. Media is a venue, information about the venue

fields.name: Video
fields.doc: Optional. Media is a video, information about the video

types.name: InputPollMedia
types.doc: This object represents the content of a poll description or a quiz explanation to be sent. It should be one of

types.name: InputPollOptionMedia
types.doc: This object represents the content of a poll option to be sent. It should be one of

types.name: PollOption
types.doc: This object contains information about one answer option in a poll.

fields.name: PersistentID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the option, persistent on option addition and deletion

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Option text, 1-100 characters

fields.name: TextEntities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities that appear in the option text. Currently, only custom emoji entities are allowed in poll option texts

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: Optional. Media added to the poll option

fields.name: VoterCount
fields.doc: Number of users who voted for this option; may be 0 if unknown

fields.name: AddedByUser
fields.doc: Optional. User who added the option; omitted if the option wasn't added by a user after poll creation

fields.name: AddedByChat
fields.doc: Optional. Chat that added the option; omitted if the option wasn't added by a chat after poll creation

fields.name: AdditionDate
fields.doc: Optional. Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the option was added; omitted if the option existed in the original poll

types.name: InputPollOption
types.doc: This object contains information about one answer option in a poll to be sent.

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Option text, 1-100 characters

fields.name: TextParseMode
fields.doc: Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the text. See formatting options for more details. Currently, only custom emoji entities are allowed.

fields.name: TextEntities
fields.doc: Optional. A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the poll option text. It can be specified instead of text_parse_mode.

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: Optional. Media added to the poll option

types.name: PollAnswer
types.doc: This object represents an answer of a user in a non-anonymous poll.

fields.name: PollID
fields.doc: Unique poll identifier

fields.name: VoterChat
fields.doc: Optional. The chat that changed the answer to the poll, if the voter is anonymous

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Optional. The user that changed the answer to the poll, if the voter isn't anonymous

fields.name: OptionIds
fields.doc: 0-based identifiers of chosen answer options. May be empty if the vote was retracted.

fields.name: OptionPersistentIds
fields.doc: Persistent identifiers of the chosen answer options. May be empty if the vote was retracted.

types.name: Poll
types.doc: This object contains information about a poll.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique poll identifier

fields.name: Question
fields.doc: Poll question, 1-300 characters

fields.name: QuestionEntities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities that appear in the question. Currently, only custom emoji entities are allowed in poll questions

fields.name: Options
fields.doc: List of poll options

fields.name: TotalVoterCount
fields.doc: Total number of users that voted in the poll

fields.name: IsClosed
fields.doc: True, if the poll is closed

fields.name: IsAnonymous
fields.doc: True, if the poll is anonymous

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Poll type, currently can be “regular” or “quiz”

fields.name: AllowsMultipleAnswers
fields.doc: True, if the poll allows multiple answers

fields.name: AllowsRevoting
fields.doc: True, if the poll allows to change the chosen answer options

fields.name: MembersOnly
fields.doc: True if voting is limited to users who have been members of the chat where the poll was originally sent for more than 24 hours

fields.name: CountryCodes
fields.doc: Optional. A list of two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes indicating the countries from which users can vote in the poll. The country code “FT” is used for users with anonymous numbers. If omitted, then users from any country can participate in the poll.

fields.name: CorrectOptionIds
fields.doc: Optional. Array of 0-based identifiers of the correct answer options. Available only for polls in quiz mode which are closed or were sent (not forwarded) by the bot or to the private chat with the bot.

fields.name: Explanation
fields.doc: Optional. Text that is shown when a user chooses an incorrect answer or taps on the lamp icon in a quiz-style poll, 0-200 characters

fields.name: ExplanationEntities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities like usernames, URLs, bot commands, etc. that appear in the explanation

fields.name: ExplanationMedia
fields.doc: Optional. Media added to the quiz explanation

fields.name: OpenPeriod
fields.doc: Optional. Amount of time in seconds the poll will be active after creation

fields.name: CloseDate
fields.doc: Optional. Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the poll will be automatically closed

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: Optional. Description of the poll; for polls inside the Message object only

fields.name: DescriptionEntities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities like usernames, URLs, bot commands, etc. that appear in the description

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: Optional. Media added to the poll description; for polls inside the Message object only

types.name: ChecklistTask
types.doc: Describes a task in a checklist.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the task

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Text of the task

fields.name: TextEntities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities that appear in the task text

fields.name: CompletedByUser
fields.doc: Optional. User that completed the task; omitted if the task wasn't completed by a user

fields.name: CompletedByChat
fields.doc: Optional. Chat that completed the task; omitted if the task wasn't completed by a chat

fields.name: CompletionDate
fields.doc: Optional. Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the task was completed; 0 if the task wasn't completed

types.name: Checklist
types.doc: Describes a checklist.

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Title of the checklist

fields.name: TitleEntities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities that appear in the checklist title

fields.name: Tasks
fields.doc: List of tasks in the checklist

fields.name: OthersCanAddTasks
fields.doc: Optional. True, if users other than the creator of the list can add tasks to the list

fields.name: OthersCanMarkTasksAsDone
fields.doc: Optional. True, if users other than the creator of the list can mark tasks as done or not done

types.name: InputChecklistTask
types.doc: Describes a task to add to a checklist.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the task; must be positive and unique among all task identifiers currently present in the checklist

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Text of the task; 1-100 characters after entities parsing

fields.name: ParseMode
fields.doc: Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the text. See formatting options for more details.

fields.name: TextEntities
fields.doc: Optional. List of special entities that appear in the text, which can be specified instead of parse_mode. Currently, only bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, spoiler, custom_emoji, and date_time entities are allowed.

types.name: InputChecklist
types.doc: Describes a checklist to create.

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Title of the checklist; 1-255 characters after entities parsing

fields.name: ParseMode
fields.doc: Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the title. See formatting options for more details.

fields.name: TitleEntities
fields.doc: Optional. List of special entities that appear in the title, which can be specified instead of parse_mode. Currently, only bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, spoiler, custom_emoji, and date_time entities are allowed.

fields.name: Tasks
fields.doc: List of 1-30 tasks in the checklist

fields.name: OthersCanAddTasks
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if other users can add tasks to the checklist

fields.name: OthersCanMarkTasksAsDone
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if other users can mark tasks as done or not done in the checklist

types.name: ChecklistTasksDone
types.doc: Describes a service message about checklist tasks marked as done or not done.

fields.name: ChecklistMessage
fields.doc: Optional. Message containing the checklist whose tasks were marked as done or not done. Note that the Message object in this field will not contain the reply_to_message field even if it itself is a reply.

fields.name: MarkedAsDoneTaskIds
fields.doc: Optional. Identifiers of the tasks that were marked as done

fields.name: MarkedAsNotDoneTaskIds
fields.doc: Optional. Identifiers of the tasks that were marked as not done

types.name: ChecklistTasksAdded
types.doc: Describes a service message about tasks added to a checklist.

fields.name: ChecklistMessage
fields.doc: Optional. Message containing the checklist to which the tasks were added. Note that the Message object in this field will not contain the reply_to_message field even if it itself is a reply.

fields.name: Tasks
fields.doc: List of tasks added to the checklist

types.name: Location
types.doc: This object represents a point on the map.

fields.name: Latitude
fields.doc: Latitude as defined by the sender

fields.name: Longitude
fields.doc: Longitude as defined by the sender

fields.name: HorizontalAccuracy
fields.doc: Optional. The radius of uncertainty for the location, measured in meters; 0-1500

fields.name: LivePeriod
fields.doc: Optional. Time relative to the message sending date, during which the location can be updated; in seconds. For active live locations only.

fields.name: Heading
fields.doc: Optional. The direction in which user is moving, in degrees; 1-360. For active live locations only.

fields.name: ProximityAlertRadius
fields.doc: Optional. The maximum distance for proximity alerts about approaching another chat member, in meters. For sent live locations only.

types.name: Venue
types.doc: This object represents a venue.

fields.name: Location
fields.doc: Venue location. Can't be a live location.

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Name of the venue

fields.name: Address
fields.doc: Address of the venue

fields.name: FoursquareID
fields.doc: Optional. Foursquare identifier of the venue

fields.name: FoursquareType
fields.doc: Optional. Foursquare type of the venue. (For example, “arts_entertainment/default”, “arts_entertainment/aquarium” or “food/icecream”.)

fields.name: GooglePlaceID
fields.doc: Optional. Google Places identifier of the venue

fields.name: GooglePlaceType
fields.doc: Optional. Google Places type of the venue. (See supported types.)

types.name: WebAppData
types.doc: Describes data sent from a Web App to the bot.

fields.name: Data
fields.doc: The data. Be aware that a bad client can send arbitrary data in this field.

fields.name: ButtonText
fields.doc: Text of the web_app keyboard button from which the Web App was opened. Be aware that a bad client can send arbitrary data in this field.

types.name: ProximityAlertTriggered
types.doc: This object represents the content of a service message, sent whenever a user in the chat triggers a proximity alert set by another user.

fields.name: Traveler
fields.doc: User that triggered the alert

fields.name: Watcher
fields.doc: User that set the alert

fields.name: Distance
fields.doc: The distance between the users

types.name: MessageAutoDeleteTimerChanged
types.doc: This object represents a service message about a change in auto-delete timer settings.

fields.name: MessageAutoDeleteTime
fields.doc: New auto-delete time for messages in the chat; in seconds

types.name: ManagedBotCreated
types.doc: This object contains information about the bot that was created to be managed by the current bot.

fields.name: Bot
fields.doc: Information about the bot. The bot's token can be fetched using the method getManagedBotToken.

types.name: ManagedBotUpdated
types.doc: This object contains information about the creation, token update, or owner update of a bot that is managed by the current bot.

fields.name: User
fields.doc: User that created the bot

fields.name: Bot
fields.doc: Information about the bot. Token of the bot can be fetched using the method getManagedBotToken.

types.name: PollOptionAdded
types.doc: Describes a service message about an option added to a poll.

fields.name: PollMessage
fields.doc: Optional. Message containing the poll to which the option was added, if known. Note that the Message object in this field will not contain the reply_to_message field even if it itself is a reply.

fields.name: OptionPersistentID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the added option

fields.name: OptionText
fields.doc: Option text

fields.name: OptionTextEntities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities that appear in the option_text

types.name: PollOptionDeleted
types.doc: Describes a service message about an option deleted from a poll.

fields.name: PollMessage
fields.doc: Optional. Message containing the poll from which the option was deleted, if known. Note that the Message object in this field will not contain the reply_to_message field even if it itself is a reply.

fields.name: OptionPersistentID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the deleted option

fields.name: OptionText
fields.doc: Option text

fields.name: OptionTextEntities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities that appear in the option_text

types.name: ChatBoostAdded
types.doc: This object represents a service message about a user boosting a chat.

fields.name: BoostCount
fields.doc: Number of boosts added by the user

types.name: BackgroundFill
types.doc: This object describes the way a background is filled based on the selected colors. Currently, it can be one of

types.name: BackgroundFillSolid
types.doc: The background is filled using the selected color.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the background fill, always “solid”

fields.name: Color
fields.doc: The color of the background fill in the RGB24 format

types.name: BackgroundFillGradient
types.doc: The background is a gradient fill.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the background fill, always “gradient”

fields.name: TopColor
fields.doc: Top color of the gradient in the RGB24 format

fields.name: BottomColor
fields.doc: Bottom color of the gradient in the RGB24 format

fields.name: RotationAngle
fields.doc: Clockwise rotation angle of the background fill in degrees; 0-359

types.name: BackgroundFillFreeformGradient
types.doc: The background is a freeform gradient that rotates after every message in the chat.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the background fill, always “freeform_gradient”

fields.name: Colors
fields.doc: A list of the 3 or 4 base colors that are used to generate the freeform gradient in the RGB24 format

types.name: BackgroundType
types.doc: This object describes the type of a background. Currently, it can be one of

types.name: BackgroundTypeFill
types.doc: The background is automatically filled based on the selected colors.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the background, always “fill”

fields.name: Fill
fields.doc: The background fill

fields.name: DarkThemeDimming
fields.doc: Dimming of the background in dark themes, as a percentage; 0-100

types.name: BackgroundTypeWallpaper
types.doc: The background is a wallpaper in the JPEG format.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the background, always “wallpaper”

fields.name: Document
fields.doc: Document with the wallpaper

fields.name: DarkThemeDimming
fields.doc: Dimming of the background in dark themes, as a percentage; 0-100

fields.name: IsBlurred
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the wallpaper is downscaled to fit in a 450x450 square and then box-blurred with radius 12

fields.name: IsMoving
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the background moves slightly when the device is tilted

types.name: BackgroundTypePattern
types.doc: The background is a .PNG or .TGV (gzipped subset of SVG with MIME type “application/x-tgwallpattern”) pattern to be combined with the background fill chosen by the user.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the background, always “pattern”

fields.name: Document
fields.doc: Document with the pattern

fields.name: Fill
fields.doc: The background fill that is combined with the pattern

fields.name: Intensity
fields.doc: Intensity of the pattern when it is shown above the filled background; 0-100

fields.name: IsInverted
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the background fill must be applied only to the pattern itself. All other pixels are black in this case. For dark themes only.

fields.name: IsMoving
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the background moves slightly when the device is tilted

types.name: BackgroundTypeChatTheme
types.doc: The background is taken directly from a built-in chat theme.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the background, always “chat_theme”

fields.name: ThemeName
fields.doc: Name of the chat theme, which is usually an emoji

types.name: ChatBackground
types.doc: This object represents a chat background.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the background

types.name: ForumTopicCreated
types.doc: This object represents a service message about a new forum topic created in the chat.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name of the topic

fields.name: IconColor
fields.doc: Color of the topic icon in RGB format

fields.name: IconCustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier of the custom emoji shown as the topic icon

fields.name: IsNameImplicit
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the name of the topic wasn't specified explicitly by its creator and likely needs to be changed by the bot

types.name: ForumTopicClosed
types.doc: This object represents a service message about a forum topic closed in the chat. Currently holds no information.

types.name: ForumTopicEdited
types.doc: This object represents a service message about an edited forum topic.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Optional. New name of the topic, if it was edited

fields.name: IconCustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Optional. New identifier of the custom emoji shown as the topic icon, if it was edited; an empty string if the icon was removed

types.name: ForumTopicReopened
types.doc: This object represents a service message about a forum topic reopened in the chat. Currently holds no information.

types.name: GeneralForumTopicHidden
types.doc: This object represents a service message about General forum topic hidden in the chat. Currently holds no information.

types.name: GeneralForumTopicUnhidden
types.doc: This object represents a service message about General forum topic unhidden in the chat. Currently holds no information.

types.name: SharedUser
types.doc: This object contains information about a user that was shared with the bot using a KeyboardButtonRequestUsers button.

fields.name: UserID
fields.doc: Identifier of the shared user. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so 64-bit integers or double-precision float types are safe for storing these identifiers. The bot may not have access to the user and could be unable to use this identifier, unless the user is already known to the bot by some other means.

fields.name: FirstName
fields.doc: Optional. First name of the user, if the name was requested by the bot

fields.name: LastName
fields.doc: Optional. Last name of the user, if the name was requested by the bot

fields.name: Username
fields.doc: Optional. Username of the user, if the username was requested by the bot

fields.name: Photo
fields.doc: Optional. Available sizes of the chat photo, if the photo was requested by the bot

types.name: UsersShared
types.doc: This object contains information about the users whose identifiers were shared with the bot using a KeyboardButtonRequestUsers button.

fields.name: RequestID
fields.doc: Identifier of the request

fields.name: Users
fields.doc: Information about users shared with the bot

types.name: ChatShared
types.doc: This object contains information about a chat that was shared with the bot using a KeyboardButtonRequestChat button.

fields.name: RequestID
fields.doc: Identifier of the request

fields.name: ChatID
fields.doc: Identifier of the shared chat. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier. The bot may not have access to the chat and could be unable to use this identifier, unless the chat is already known to the bot by some other means.

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Optional. Title of the chat, if the title was requested by the bot

fields.name: Username
fields.doc: Optional. Username of the chat, if the username was requested by the bot and available

fields.name: Photo
fields.doc: Optional. Available sizes of the chat photo, if the photo was requested by the bot

types.name: WriteAccessAllowed
types.doc: This object represents a service message about a user allowing a bot to write messages after adding it to the attachment menu, launching a Web App from a link, or accepting an explicit request from a Web App sent by the method requestWriteAccess.

fields.name: FromRequest
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the access was granted after the user accepted an explicit request from a Web App sent by the method requestWriteAccess

fields.name: WebAppName
fields.doc: Optional. Name of the Web App, if the access was granted when the Web App was launched from a link

fields.name: FromAttachmentMenu
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the access was granted when the bot was added to the attachment or side menu

types.name: VideoChatScheduled
types.doc: This object represents a service message about a video chat scheduled in the chat.

fields.name: StartDate
fields.doc: Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the video chat is supposed to be started by a chat administrator

types.name: VideoChatStarted
types.doc: This object represents a service message about a video chat started in the chat. Currently holds no information.

types.name: VideoChatEnded
types.doc: This object represents a service message about a video chat ended in the chat.

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Video chat duration in seconds

types.name: VideoChatParticipantsInvited
types.doc: This object represents a service message about new members invited to a video chat.

fields.name: Users
fields.doc: New members that were invited to the video chat

types.name: PaidMessagePriceChanged
types.doc: Describes a service message about a change in the price of paid messages within a chat.

fields.name: PaidMessageStarCount
fields.doc: The new number of Telegram Stars that must be paid by non-administrator users of the supergroup chat for each sent message

types.name: DirectMessagePriceChanged
types.doc: Describes a service message about a change in the price of direct messages sent to a channel chat.

fields.name: AreDirectMessagesEnabled
fields.doc: True, if direct messages are enabled for the channel chat; false otherwise

fields.name: DirectMessageStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. The new number of Telegram Stars that must be paid by users for each direct message sent to the channel. Does not apply to users who have been exempted by administrators. Defaults to 0.

types.name: SuggestedPostApproved
types.doc: Describes a service message about the approval of a suggested post.

fields.name: SuggestedPostMessage
fields.doc: Optional. Message containing the suggested post. Note that the Message object in this field will not contain the reply_to_message field even if it itself is a reply.

fields.name: Price
fields.doc: Optional. Amount paid for the post

fields.name: SendDate
fields.doc: Date when the post will be published

types.name: SuggestedPostApprovalFailed
types.doc: Describes a service message about the failed approval of a suggested post. Currently, only caused by insufficient user funds at the time of approval.

fields.name: SuggestedPostMessage
