fields.doc: Optional. Message containing the suggested post whose approval has failed. Note that the Message object in this field will not contain the reply_to_message field even if it itself is a reply.

fields.name: Price
fields.doc: Expected price of the post

types.name: SuggestedPostDeclined
types.doc: Describes a service message about the rejection of a suggested post.

fields.name: SuggestedPostMessage
fields.doc: Optional. Message containing the suggested post. Note that the Message object in this field will not contain the reply_to_message field even if it itself is a reply.

fields.name: Comment
fields.doc: Optional. Comment with which the post was declined

types.name: SuggestedPostPaid
types.doc: Describes a service message about a successful payment for a suggested post.

fields.name: SuggestedPostMessage
fields.doc: Optional. Message containing the suggested post. Note that the Message object in this field will not contain the reply_to_message field even if it itself is a reply.

fields.name: Currency
fields.doc: Currency in which the payment was made. Currently, one of “XTR” for Telegram Stars or “TON” for toncoins.

fields.name: Amount
fields.doc: Optional. The amount of the currency that was received by the channel in nanotoncoins; for payments in toncoins only

fields.name: StarAmount
fields.doc: Optional. The amount of Telegram Stars that was received by the channel; for payments in Telegram Stars only

types.name: SuggestedPostRefunded
types.doc: Describes a service message about a payment refund for a suggested post.

fields.name: SuggestedPostMessage
fields.doc: Optional. Message containing the suggested post. Note that the Message object in this field will not contain the reply_to_message field even if it itself is a reply.

fields.name: Reason
fields.doc: Reason for the refund. Currently, one of “post_deleted” if the post was deleted within 24 hours of being posted or removed from scheduled messages without being posted, or “payment_refunded” if the payer refunded their payment.

types.name: GiveawayCreated
types.doc: This object represents a service message about the creation of a scheduled giveaway.

fields.name: PrizeStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of Telegram Stars to be split between giveaway winners; for Telegram Star giveaways only

types.name: Giveaway
types.doc: This object represents a message about a scheduled giveaway.

fields.name: Chats
fields.doc: The list of chats which the user must join to participate in the giveaway

fields.name: WinnersSelectionDate
fields.doc: Point in time (Unix timestamp) when winners of the giveaway will be selected

fields.name: WinnerCount
fields.doc: The number of users which are supposed to be selected as winners of the giveaway

fields.name: OnlyNewMembers
fields.doc: Optional. True, if only users who join the chats after the giveaway started should be eligible to win

fields.name: HasPublicWinners
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the list of giveaway winners will be visible to everyone

fields.name: PrizeDescription
fields.doc: Optional. Description of additional giveaway prize

fields.name: CountryCodes
fields.doc: Optional. A list of two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes indicating the countries from which eligible users for the giveaway must come. If empty, then all users can participate in the giveaway. Users with a phone number that was bought on Fragment can always participate in giveaways.

fields.name: PrizeStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of Telegram Stars to be split between giveaway winners; for Telegram Star giveaways only

fields.name: PremiumSubscriptionMonthCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of months the Telegram Premium subscription won from the giveaway will be active for; for Telegram Premium giveaways only

types.name: GiveawayWinners
types.doc: This object represents a message about the completion of a giveaway with public winners.

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: The chat that created the giveaway

fields.name: GiveawayMessageID
fields.doc: Identifier of the message with the giveaway in the chat

fields.name: WinnersSelectionDate
fields.doc: Point in time (Unix timestamp) when winners of the giveaway were selected

fields.name: WinnerCount
fields.doc: Total number of winners in the giveaway

fields.name: Winners
fields.doc: List of up to 100 winners of the giveaway

fields.name: AdditionalChatCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of other chats the user had to join in order to be eligible for the giveaway

fields.name: PrizeStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of Telegram Stars that were split between giveaway winners; for Telegram Star giveaways only

fields.name: PremiumSubscriptionMonthCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of months the Telegram Premium subscription won from the giveaway will be active for; for Telegram Premium giveaways only

fields.name: UnclaimedPrizeCount
fields.doc: Optional. Number of undistributed prizes

fields.name: OnlyNewMembers
fields.doc: Optional. True, if only users who had joined the chats after the giveaway started were eligible to win

fields.name: WasRefunded
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the giveaway was canceled because the payment for it was refunded

fields.name: PrizeDescription
fields.doc: Optional. Description of additional giveaway prize

types.name: GiveawayCompleted
types.doc: This object represents a service message about the completion of a giveaway without public winners.

fields.name: WinnerCount
fields.doc: Number of winners in the giveaway

fields.name: UnclaimedPrizeCount
fields.doc: Optional. Number of undistributed prizes

fields.name: GiveawayMessage
fields.doc: Optional. Message with the giveaway that was completed, if it wasn't deleted

fields.name: IsStarGiveaway
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the giveaway is a Telegram Star giveaway. Otherwise, currently, the giveaway is a Telegram Premium giveaway.

types.name: LinkPreviewOptions
types.doc: Describes the options used for link preview generation.

fields.name: IsDisabled
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the link preview is disabled

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: Optional. URL to use for the link preview. If empty, then the first URL found in the message text will be used.

fields.name: PreferSmallMedia
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the media in the link preview is supposed to be shrunk; ignored if the URL isn't explicitly specified or media size change isn't supported for the preview

fields.name: PreferLargeMedia
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the media in the link preview is supposed to be enlarged; ignored if the URL isn't explicitly specified or media size change isn't supported for the preview

fields.name: ShowAboveText
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the link preview must be shown above the message text; otherwise, the link preview will be shown below the message text

types.name: SuggestedPostPrice
types.doc: Describes the price of a suggested post.

fields.name: Currency
fields.doc: Currency in which the post will be paid. Currently, must be one of “XTR” for Telegram Stars or “TON” for toncoins.

fields.name: Amount
fields.doc: The amount of the currency that will be paid for the post in the smallest units of the currency, i.e. Telegram Stars or nanotoncoins. Currently, price in Telegram Stars must be between 5 and 100000, and price in nanotoncoins must be between 10000000 and 10000000000000.

types.name: SuggestedPostInfo
types.doc: Contains information about a suggested post.

fields.name: State
fields.doc: State of the suggested post. Currently, it can be one of “pending”, “approved”, “declined”.

fields.name: Price
fields.doc: Optional. Proposed price of the post. If the field is omitted, then the post is unpaid.

fields.name: SendDate
fields.doc: Optional. Proposed send date of the post. If the field is omitted, then the post can be published at any time within 30 days at the sole discretion of the user or administrator who approves it.

types.name: SuggestedPostParameters
types.doc: Contains parameters of a post that is being suggested by the bot.

fields.name: Price
fields.doc: Optional. Proposed price for the post. If the field is omitted, then the post is unpaid.

fields.name: SendDate
fields.doc: Optional. Proposed send date of the post. If specified, then the date must be between 300 second and 2678400 seconds (30 days) in the future. If the field is omitted, then the post can be published at any time within 30 days at the sole discretion of the user who approves it.

types.name: DirectMessagesTopic
types.doc: Describes a topic of a direct messages chat.

fields.name: TopicID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the topic. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier.

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Optional. Information about the user that created the topic. Currently, it is always present.

types.name: UserProfilePhotos
types.doc: This object represent a user's profile pictures.

fields.name: TotalCount
fields.doc: Total number of profile pictures the target user has

fields.name: Photos
fields.doc: Requested profile pictures (in up to 4 sizes each)

types.name: UserProfileAudios
types.doc: This object represents the audios displayed on a user's profile.

fields.name: TotalCount
fields.doc: Total number of profile audios for the target user

fields.name: Audios
fields.doc: Requested profile audios

types.name: File
types.doc:
This object represents a file ready to be downloaded. The file can be downloaded via the link https://api.telegram.org/file/bot<token>/<file_path>. It is guaranteed that the link will be valid for at least 1 hour. When the link expires, a new one can be requested by calling getFile.
The maximum file size to download is 20 MB

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: Optional. File size in bytes. It can be bigger than 2^31 and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this value.

fields.name: FilePath
fields.doc: Optional. File path. Use https://api.telegram.org/file/bot<token>/<file_path> to get the file.

types.name: WebAppInfo
types.doc: Describes a Web App.

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: An HTTPS URL of a Web App to be opened with additional data as specified in Initializing Web Apps

types.name: ReplyKeyboardMarkup
types.doc: This object represents a custom keyboard with reply options (see Introduction to bots for details and examples). Not supported in channels and for messages sent on behalf of a business account.

fields.name: Keyboard
fields.doc: Array of button rows, each represented by an Array of KeyboardButton objects

fields.name: IsPersistent
fields.doc: Optional. Requests clients to always show the keyboard when the regular keyboard is hidden. Defaults to false, in which case the custom keyboard can be hidden and opened with a keyboard icon.

fields.name: ResizeKeyboard
fields.doc: Optional. Requests clients to resize the keyboard vertically for optimal fit (e.g., make the keyboard smaller if there are just two rows of buttons). Defaults to false, in which case the custom keyboard is always of the same height as the app's standard keyboard.

fields.name: OneTimeKeyboard
fields.doc: Optional. Requests clients to hide the keyboard as soon as it's been used. The keyboard will still be available, but clients will automatically display the usual letter-keyboard in the chat - the user can press a special button in the input field to see the custom keyboard again. Defaults to false.

fields.name: InputFieldPlaceholder
fields.doc: Optional. The placeholder to be shown in the input field when the keyboard is active; 1-64 characters

fields.name: Selective
fields.doc: Optional. Use this parameter if you want to show the keyboard to specific users only. Targets: 1) users that are @mentioned in the text of the Message object; 2) if the bot's message is a reply to a message in the same chat and forum topic, sender of the original message.Example: A user requests to change the bot's language, bot replies to the request with a keyboard to select the new language. Other users in the group don't see the keyboard.

types.name: KeyboardButton
types.doc: This object represents one button of the reply keyboard. At most one of the fields other than text, icon_custom_emoji_id, and style must be used to specify the type of the button. For simple text buttons, String can be used instead of this object to specify the button text.

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Text of the button. If none of the fields other than text, icon_custom_emoji_id, and style are used, it will be sent as a message when the button is pressed.

fields.name: IconCustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier of the custom emoji shown before the text of the button. Can only be used by bots that purchased additional usernames on Fragment or in the messages directly sent by the bot to private, group and supergroup chats if the owner of the bot has a Telegram Premium subscription.

fields.name: Style
fields.doc: Optional. Style of the button. Must be one of “danger” (red), “success” (green) or “primary” (blue). If omitted, then an app-specific style is used.

fields.name: RequestUsers
fields.doc: Optional. If specified, pressing the button will open a list of suitable users. Identifiers of selected users will be sent to the bot in a “users_shared” service message. Available in private chats only.

fields.name: RequestChat
fields.doc: Optional. If specified, pressing the button will open a list of suitable chats. Tapping on a chat will send its identifier to the bot in a “chat_shared” service message. Available in private chats only.

fields.name: RequestManagedBot
fields.doc: Optional. If specified, pressing the button will ask the user to create and share a bot that will be managed by the current bot. Available for bots that enabled management of other bots in the @BotFather Mini App. Available in private chats only.

fields.name: RequestContact
fields.doc: Optional. If True, the user's phone number will be sent as a contact when the button is pressed. Available in private chats only.

fields.name: RequestLocation
fields.doc: Optional. If True, the user's current location will be sent when the button is pressed. Available in private chats only.

fields.name: RequestPoll
fields.doc: Optional. If specified, the user will be asked to create a poll and send it to the bot when the button is pressed. Available in private chats only.

fields.name: WebApp
fields.doc: Optional. If specified, the described Web App will be launched when the button is pressed. The Web App will be able to send a “web_app_data” service message. Available in private chats only.

types.name: KeyboardButtonRequestUsers
types.doc: This object defines the criteria used to request suitable users. Information about the selected users will be shared with the bot when the corresponding button is pressed. More about requesting users »

fields.name: RequestID
fields.doc: Signed 32-bit identifier of the request that will be received back in the UsersShared object. Must be unique within the message.

fields.name: UserIsBot
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request bots, pass False to request regular users. If not specified, no additional restrictions are applied.

fields.name: UserIsPremium
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request premium users, pass False to request non-premium users. If not specified, no additional restrictions are applied.

fields.name: MaxQuantity
fields.doc: Optional. The maximum number of users to be selected; 1-10. Defaults to 1.

fields.name: RequestName
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request the users' first and last names

fields.name: RequestUsername
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request the users' usernames

fields.name: RequestPhoto
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request the users' photos

types.name: KeyboardButtonRequestChat
types.doc: This object defines the criteria used to request a suitable chat. Information about the selected chat will be shared with the bot when the corresponding button is pressed. The bot will be granted requested rights in the chat if appropriate. More about requesting chats ».

fields.name: RequestID
fields.doc: Signed 32-bit identifier of the request, which will be received back in the ChatShared object. Must be unique within the message.

fields.name: ChatIsChannel
fields.doc: Pass True to request a channel chat, pass False to request a group or a supergroup chat

fields.name: ChatIsForum
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request a forum supergroup, pass False to request a non-forum chat. If not specified, no additional restrictions are applied.

fields.name: ChatHasUsername
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request a supergroup or a channel with a username, pass False to request a chat without a username. If not specified, no additional restrictions are applied.

fields.name: ChatIsCreated
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request a chat owned by the user. Otherwise, no additional restrictions are applied.

fields.name: UserAdministratorRights
fields.doc: Optional. A JSON-serialized object listing the required administrator rights of the user in the chat. The rights must be a superset of bot_administrator_rights. If not specified, no additional restrictions are applied.

fields.name: BotAdministratorRights
fields.doc: Optional. A JSON-serialized object listing the required administrator rights of the bot in the chat. The rights must be a subset of user_administrator_rights. If not specified, no additional restrictions are applied.

fields.name: BotIsMember
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request a chat with the bot as a member. Otherwise, no additional restrictions are applied.

fields.name: RequestTitle
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request the chat's title

fields.name: RequestUsername
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request the chat's username

fields.name: RequestPhoto
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request the chat's photo

types.name: KeyboardButtonRequestManagedBot
types.doc: This object defines the parameters for the creation of a managed bot. Information about the created bot will be shared with the bot using the update managed_bot and a Message with the field managed_bot_created.

fields.name: RequestID
fields.doc: Signed 32-bit identifier of the request. Must be unique within the message.

fields.name: SuggestedName
fields.doc: Optional. Suggested name for the bot

fields.name: SuggestedUsername
fields.doc: Optional. Suggested username for the bot

types.name: KeyboardButtonPollType
types.doc: This object represents type of a poll, which is allowed to be created and sent when the corresponding button is pressed.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Optional. If quiz is passed, the user will be allowed to create only polls in the quiz mode. If regular is passed, only regular polls will be allowed. Otherwise, the user will be allowed to create a poll of any type.

types.name: ReplyKeyboardRemove
types.doc: Upon receiving a message with this object, Telegram clients will remove the current custom keyboard and display the default letter-keyboard. By default, custom keyboards are displayed until a new keyboard is sent by a bot. An exception is made for one-time keyboards that are hidden immediately after the user presses a button (see ReplyKeyboardMarkup). Not supported in channels and for messages sent on behalf of a business account.

fields.name: RemoveKeyboard
fields.doc: Requests clients to remove the custom keyboard (user will not be able to summon this keyboard; if you want to hide the keyboard from sight but keep it accessible, use one_time_keyboard in ReplyKeyboardMarkup)

fields.name: Selective
fields.doc: Optional. Use this parameter if you want to remove the keyboard for specific users only. Targets: 1) users that are @mentioned in the text of the Message object; 2) if the bot's message is a reply to a message in the same chat and forum topic, sender of the original message.Example: A user votes in a poll, bot returns confirmation message in reply to the vote and removes the keyboard for that user, while still showing the keyboard with poll options to users who haven't voted yet.

types.name: InlineKeyboardMarkup
types.doc: This object represents an inline keyboard that appears right next to the message it belongs to.

fields.name: InlineKeyboard
fields.doc: Array of button rows, each represented by an Array of InlineKeyboardButton objects

types.name: InlineKeyboardButton
types.doc: This object represents one button of an inline keyboard. Exactly one of the fields other than text, icon_custom_emoji_id, and style must be used to specify the type of the button.

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Label text on the button

fields.name: IconCustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier of the custom emoji shown before the text of the button. Can only be used by bots that purchased additional usernames on Fragment or in the messages directly sent by the bot to private, group and supergroup chats if the owner of the bot has a Telegram Premium subscription.

fields.name: Style
fields.doc: Optional. Style of the button. Must be one of “danger” (red), “success” (green) or “primary” (blue). If omitted, then an app-specific style is used.

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: Optional. HTTP or tg:// URL to be opened when the button is pressed. Links tg://user?id=<user_id> can be used to mention a user by their identifier without using a username, if this is allowed by their privacy settings.

fields.name: CallbackData
fields.doc: Optional. Data to be sent in a callback query to the bot when the button is pressed, 1-64 bytes

fields.name: WebApp
fields.doc: Optional. Description of the Web App that will be launched when the user presses the button. The Web App will be able to send an arbitrary message on behalf of the user using the method answerWebAppQuery. Available only in private chats between a user and the bot. Not supported for messages sent on behalf of a business account.

fields.name: LoginURL
fields.doc: Optional. An HTTPS URL used to automatically authorize the user. Can be used as a replacement for the Telegram Login Widget.

fields.name: SwitchInlineQuery
fields.doc: Optional. If set, pressing the button will prompt the user to select one of their chats, open that chat and insert the bot's username and the specified inline query in the input field. May be empty, in which case just the bot's username will be inserted. Not supported for messages sent in channel direct messages chats and on behalf of a business account.

fields.name: SwitchInlineQueryCurrentChat
fields.doc: Optional. If set, pressing the button will insert the bot's username and the specified inline query in the current chat's input field. May be empty, in which case only the bot's username will be inserted.This offers a quick way for the user to open your bot in inline mode in the same chat - good for selecting something from multiple options. Not supported in channels and for messages sent in channel direct messages chats and on behalf of a business account.

fields.name: SwitchInlineQueryChosenChat
fields.doc: Optional. If set, pressing the button will prompt the user to select one of their chats of the specified type, open that chat and insert the bot's username and the specified inline query in the input field. Not supported for messages sent in channel direct messages chats and on behalf of a business account.

fields.name: CopyText
fields.doc: Optional. Description of the button that copies the specified text to the clipboard

fields.name: CallbackGame
fields.doc: Optional. Description of the game that will be launched when the user presses the button.NOTE: This type of button must always be the first button in the first row.

fields.name: Pay
fields.doc: Optional. Specify True, to send a Pay button. Substrings “” and “XTR” in the buttons's text will be replaced with a Telegram Star icon.NOTE: This type of button must always be the first button in the first row and can only be used in invoice messages.

types.name: LoginUrl
types.doc:
This object represents a parameter of the inline keyboard button used to automatically authorize a user. Serves as a great replacement for the Telegram Login Widget when the user is coming from Telegram. All the user needs to do is tap/click a button and confirm that they want to log in:
Telegram apps support these buttons as of version 5.7.
Sample bot: @discussbot

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: An HTTPS URL to be opened with user authorization data added to the query string when the button is pressed. If the user refuses to provide authorization data, the original URL without information about the user will be opened. The data added is the same as described in Receiving authorization data.NOTE: You must always check the hash of the received data to verify the authentication and the integrity of the data as described in Checking authorization.

fields.name: ForwardText
fields.doc: Optional. New text of the button in forwarded messages

fields.name: BotUsername
fields.doc: Optional. Username of a bot, which will be used for user authorization. See Setting up a bot for more details. If not specified, the current bot's username will be assumed. The url's domain must be the same as the domain linked with the bot. See Linking your domain to the bot for more details.

fields.name: RequestWriteAccess
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True to request the permission for your bot to send messages to the user

types.name: SwitchInlineQueryChosenChat
types.doc: This object represents an inline button that switches the current user to inline mode in a chosen chat, with an optional default inline query.

fields.name: Query
fields.doc: Optional. The default inline query to be inserted in the input field. If left empty, only the bot's username will be inserted.

fields.name: AllowUserChats
fields.doc: Optional. True, if private chats with users can be chosen

fields.name: AllowBotChats
fields.doc: Optional. True, if private chats with bots can be chosen

fields.name: AllowGroupChats
fields.doc: Optional. True, if group and supergroup chats can be chosen

fields.name: AllowChannelChats
fields.doc: Optional. True, if channel chats can be chosen

types.name: CopyTextButton
types.doc: This object represents an inline keyboard button that copies specified text to the clipboard.

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: The text to be copied to the clipboard; 1-256 characters

types.name: CallbackQuery
types.doc:
This object represents an incoming callback query from a callback button in an inline keyboard. If the button that originated the query was attached to a message sent by the bot, the field message will be present. If the button was attached to a message sent via the bot (in inline mode), the field inline_message_id will be present. Exactly one of the fields data or game_short_name will be present.
NOTE: After the user presses a callback button, Telegram clients will display a progress bar until you call answerCallbackQuery. It is, therefore, necessary to react by calling answerCallbackQuery even if no notification to the user is needed (e.g., without specifying any of the optional parameters).

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this query

fields.name: From
fields.doc: Sender

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Optional. Message sent by the bot with the callback button that originated the query

fields.name: InlineMessageID
fields.doc: Optional. Identifier of the message sent via the bot in inline mode, that originated the query

fields.name: ChatInstance
fields.doc: Global identifier, uniquely corresponding to the chat to which the message with the callback button was sent. Useful for high scores in games.

fields.name: Data
fields.doc: Optional. Data associated with the callback button. Be aware that the message originated the query can contain no callback buttons with this data.

fields.name: GameShortName
fields.doc: Optional. Short name of a Game to be returned, serves as the unique identifier for the game

types.name: ForceReply
types.doc:
Upon receiving a message with this object, Telegram clients will display a reply interface to the user (act as if the user has selected the bot's message and tapped 'Reply'). This can be extremely useful if you want to create user-friendly step-by-step interfaces without having to sacrifice privacy mode. Not supported in channels and for messages sent on behalf of a user account.
Example: A poll bot for groups runs in privacy mode (only receives commands, replies to its messages and mentions). There could be two ways to create a new poll:
The last option is definitely more attractive. And if you use ForceReply in your bot's questions, it will receive the user's answers even if it only receives replies, commands and mentions - without any extra work for the user.

fields.name: ForceReply
fields.doc: Shows reply interface to the user, as if they manually selected the bot's message and tapped 'Reply'

fields.name: InputFieldPlaceholder
fields.doc: Optional. The placeholder to be shown in the input field when the reply is active; 1-64 characters

fields.name: Selective
fields.doc: Optional. Use this parameter if you want to force reply from specific users only. Targets: 1) users that are @mentioned in the text of the Message object; 2) if the bot's message is a reply to a message in the same chat and forum topic, sender of the original message.

types.name: ChatPhoto
types.doc: This object represents a chat photo.

fields.name: SmallFileID
fields.doc: File identifier of small (160x160) chat photo. This file_id can be used only for photo download and only for as long as the photo is not changed.

fields.name: SmallFileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique file identifier of small (160x160) chat photo, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: BigFileID
fields.doc: File identifier of big (640x640) chat photo. This file_id can be used only for photo download and only for as long as the photo is not changed.

fields.name: BigFileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique file identifier of big (640x640) chat photo, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

types.name: ChatInviteLink
types.doc: Represents an invite link for a chat.

fields.name: InviteLink
fields.doc: The invite link. If the link was created by another chat administrator, then the second part of the link will be replaced with “…”.

fields.name: Creator
fields.doc: Creator of the link

fields.name: CreatesJoinRequest
fields.doc: True, if users joining the chat via the link need to be approved by chat administrators

fields.name: IsPrimary
fields.doc: True, if the link is primary

fields.name: IsRevoked
fields.doc: True, if the link is revoked

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Optional. Invite link name

fields.name: ExpireDate
fields.doc: Optional. Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the link will expire or has been expired

fields.name: MemberLimit
fields.doc: Optional. The maximum number of users that can be members of the chat simultaneously after joining the chat via this invite link; 1-99999

fields.name: PendingJoinRequestCount
fields.doc: Optional. Number of pending join requests created using this link

fields.name: SubscriptionPeriod
fields.doc: Optional. The number of seconds the subscription will be active for before the next payment

fields.name: SubscriptionPrice
fields.doc: Optional. The amount of Telegram Stars a user must pay initially and after each subsequent subscription period to be a member of the chat using the link

types.name: ChatAdministratorRights
types.doc: Represents the rights of an administrator in a chat.

fields.name: IsAnonymous
fields.doc: True, if the user's presence in the chat is hidden

fields.name: CanManageChat
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can access the chat event log, get boost list, see hidden supergroup and channel members, report spam messages, ignore slow mode, and send messages to the chat without paying Telegram Stars. Implied by any other administrator privilege.

fields.name: CanDeleteMessages
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can delete messages of other users

fields.name: CanManageVideoChats
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can manage video chats

fields.name: CanRestrictMembers
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can restrict, ban or unban chat members, or access supergroup statistics

fields.name: CanPromoteMembers
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can add new administrators with a subset of their own privileges or demote administrators that they have promoted, directly or indirectly (promoted by administrators that were appointed by the user)

fields.name: CanChangeInfo
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to change the chat title, photo and other settings

fields.name: CanInviteUsers
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to invite new users to the chat

fields.name: CanPostStories
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can post stories to the chat

fields.name: CanEditStories
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can edit stories posted by other users, post stories to the chat page, pin chat stories, and access the chat's story archive

fields.name: CanDeleteStories
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can delete stories posted by other users

fields.name: CanPostMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the administrator can post messages in the channel, approve suggested posts, or access channel statistics; for channels only

fields.name: CanEditMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the administrator can edit messages of other users and can pin messages; for channels only

fields.name: CanPinMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to pin messages; for groups and supergroups only

fields.name: CanManageTopics
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to create, rename, close, and reopen forum topics; for supergroups only

fields.name: CanManageDirectMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the administrator can manage direct messages of the channel and decline suggested posts; for channels only

fields.name: CanManageTags
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the administrator can edit the tags of regular members; for groups and supergroups only. If omitted defaults to the value of can_pin_messages.

types.name: ChatMemberUpdated
types.doc: This object represents changes in the status of a chat member.

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: Chat the user belongs to

fields.name: From
fields.doc: Performer of the action, which resulted in the change

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date the change was done in Unix time

fields.name: OldChatMember
fields.doc: Previous information about the chat member

fields.name: NewChatMember
fields.doc: New information about the chat member

fields.name: InviteLink
fields.doc: Optional. Chat invite link, which was used by the user to join the chat; for joining by invite link events only

fields.name: ViaJoinRequest
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user joined the chat after sending a direct join request without using an invite link and being approved by an administrator

fields.name: ViaChatFolderInviteLink
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user joined the chat via a chat folder invite link

types.name: ChatMember
types.doc: This object contains information about one member of a chat. Currently, the following 6 types of chat members are supported:

types.name: ChatMemberOwner
types.doc: Represents a chat member that owns the chat and has all administrator privileges.

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: The member's status in the chat, always “creator”

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Information about the user

fields.name: IsAnonymous
fields.doc: True, if the user's presence in the chat is hidden

fields.name: CustomTitle
fields.doc: Optional. Custom title for this user

types.name: ChatMemberAdministrator
types.doc: Represents a chat member that has some additional privileges.

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: The member's status in the chat, always “administrator”

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Information about the user

fields.name: CanBeEdited
fields.doc: True, if the bot is allowed to edit administrator privileges of that user

fields.name: IsAnonymous
fields.doc: True, if the user's presence in the chat is hidden

fields.name: CanManageChat
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can access the chat event log, get boost list, see hidden supergroup and channel members, report spam messages, ignore slow mode, and send messages to the chat without paying Telegram Stars. Implied by any other administrator privilege.

fields.name: CanDeleteMessages
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can delete messages of other users

fields.name: CanManageVideoChats
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can manage video chats

fields.name: CanRestrictMembers
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can restrict, ban or unban chat members, or access supergroup statistics

fields.name: CanPromoteMembers
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can add new administrators with a subset of their own privileges or demote administrators that they have promoted, directly or indirectly (promoted by administrators that were appointed by the user)

fields.name: CanChangeInfo
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to change the chat title, photo and other settings

fields.name: CanInviteUsers
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to invite new users to the chat

fields.name: CanPostStories
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can post stories to the chat

fields.name: CanEditStories
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can edit stories posted by other users, post stories to the chat page, pin chat stories, and access the chat's story archive

fields.name: CanDeleteStories
fields.doc: True, if the administrator can delete stories posted by other users

fields.name: CanPostMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the administrator can post messages in the channel, approve suggested posts, or access channel statistics; for channels only

fields.name: CanEditMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the administrator can edit messages of other users and can pin messages; for channels only

fields.name: CanPinMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to pin messages; for groups and supergroups only

fields.name: CanManageTopics
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to create, rename, close, and reopen forum topics; for supergroups only

fields.name: CanManageDirectMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the administrator can manage direct messages of the channel and decline suggested posts; for channels only

fields.name: CanManageTags
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the administrator can edit the tags of regular members; for groups and supergroups only. If omitted defaults to the value of can_pin_messages.

fields.name: CustomTitle
fields.doc: Optional. Custom title for this user

types.name: ChatMemberMember
types.doc: Represents a chat member that has no additional privileges or restrictions.

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: The member's status in the chat, always “member”

fields.name: Tag
fields.doc: Optional. Tag of the member

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Information about the user

fields.name: UntilDate
fields.doc: Optional. Date when the user's subscription will expire; Unix time

types.name: ChatMemberRestricted
types.doc: Represents a chat member that is under certain restrictions in the chat. Supergroups only.

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: The member's status in the chat, always “restricted”

fields.name: Tag
fields.doc: Optional. Tag of the member

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Information about the user

fields.name: IsMember
fields.doc: True, if the user is a member of the chat at the moment of the request

fields.name: CanSendMessages
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to send text messages, contacts, giveaways, giveaway winners, invoices, locations and venues

fields.name: CanSendAudios
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to send audios

fields.name: CanSendDocuments
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to send documents

fields.name: CanSendPhotos
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to send photos

fields.name: CanSendVideos
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to send videos

fields.name: CanSendVideoNotes
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to send video notes

fields.name: CanSendVoiceNotes
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to send voice notes

fields.name: CanSendPolls
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to send polls and checklists

fields.name: CanSendOtherMessages
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to send animations, games, stickers and use inline bots

fields.name: CanAddWebPagePreviews
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to add web page previews to their messages

fields.name: CanReactToMessages
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to react to messages

fields.name: CanEditTag
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to edit their own tag

fields.name: CanChangeInfo
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to change the chat title, photo and other settings

fields.name: CanInviteUsers
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to invite new users to the chat

fields.name: CanPinMessages
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to pin messages

fields.name: CanManageTopics
fields.doc: True, if the user is allowed to create forum topics

fields.name: UntilDate
fields.doc: Date when restrictions will be lifted for this user; Unix time. If 0, then the user is restricted forever.

types.name: ChatMemberLeft
types.doc: Represents a chat member that isn't currently a member of the chat, but may join it themselves.

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: The member's status in the chat, always “left”

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Information about the user

types.name: ChatMemberBanned
types.doc: Represents a chat member that was banned in the chat and can't return to the chat or view chat messages.

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: The member's status in the chat, always “kicked”

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Information about the user

fields.name: UntilDate
fields.doc: Date when restrictions will be lifted for this user; Unix time. If 0, then the user is banned forever.

types.name: ChatJoinRequest
types.doc: Represents a join request sent to a chat.

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: Chat to which the request was sent

fields.name: From
fields.doc: User that sent the join request

fields.name: UserChatID
fields.doc: Identifier of a private chat with the user who sent the join request. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier. The bot can use this identifier for 5 minutes to send messages until the join request is processed, assuming no other administrator contacted the user.

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date the request was sent in Unix time

fields.name: Bio
fields.doc: Optional. Bio of the user

fields.name: InviteLink
fields.doc: Optional. Chat invite link that was used by the user to send the join request

types.name: ChatPermissions
types.doc: Describes actions that a non-administrator user is allowed to take in a chat.

fields.name: CanSendMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to send text messages, contacts, giveaways, giveaway winners, invoices, locations and venues

fields.name: CanSendAudios
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to send audios

fields.name: CanSendDocuments
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to send documents

fields.name: CanSendPhotos
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to send photos

fields.name: CanSendVideos
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to send videos

fields.name: CanSendVideoNotes
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to send video notes

fields.name: CanSendVoiceNotes
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to send voice notes

fields.name: CanSendPolls
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to send polls and checklists

fields.name: CanSendOtherMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to send animations, games, stickers and use inline bots

fields.name: CanAddWebPagePreviews
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to add web page previews to their messages

fields.name: CanReactToMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to react to messages. If omitted, defaults to the value of can_send_messages.

fields.name: CanEditTag
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to edit their own tag. If omitted, defaults to the value of can_pin_messages.

fields.name: CanChangeInfo
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to change the chat title, photo and other settings. Ignored in public supergroups.

fields.name: CanInviteUsers
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to invite new users to the chat

fields.name: CanPinMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to pin messages. Ignored in public supergroups.

fields.name: CanManageTopics
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the user is allowed to create forum topics. If omitted defaults to the value of can_pin_messages.

types.name: Birthdate
types.doc: Describes the birthdate of a user.

fields.name: Day
fields.doc: Day of the user's birth; 1-31

fields.name: Month
fields.doc: Month of the user's birth; 1-12

fields.name: Year
fields.doc: Optional. Year of the user's birth

types.name: BusinessIntro
types.doc: Contains information about the start page settings of a Telegram Business account.

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Optional. Title text of the business intro

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Optional. Message text of the business intro

fields.name: Sticker
fields.doc: Optional. Sticker of the business intro

types.name: BusinessLocation
types.doc: Contains information about the location of a Telegram Business account.

fields.name: Address
fields.doc: Address of the business

fields.name: Location
fields.doc: Optional. Location of the business

types.name: BusinessOpeningHoursInterval
types.doc: Describes an interval of time during which a business is open.

fields.name: OpeningMinute
fields.doc: The minute's sequence number in a week, starting on Monday, marking the start of the time interval during which the business is open; 0 - 7 * 24 * 60

fields.name: ClosingMinute
fields.doc: The minute's sequence number in a week, starting on Monday, marking the end of the time interval during which the business is open; 0 - 8 * 24 * 60

types.name: BusinessOpeningHours
types.doc: Describes the opening hours of a business.

fields.name: TimeZoneName
fields.doc: Unique name of the time zone for which the opening hours are defined

fields.name: OpeningHours
fields.doc: List of time intervals describing business opening hours

types.name: UserRating
types.doc: This object describes the rating of a user based on their Telegram Star spendings.

fields.name: Level
fields.doc: Current level of the user, indicating their reliability when purchasing digital goods and services. A higher level suggests a more trustworthy customer; a negative level is likely reason for concern.

fields.name: Rating
fields.doc: Numerical value of the user's rating; the higher the rating, the better

fields.name: CurrentLevelRating
fields.doc: The rating value required to get the current level

fields.name: NextLevelRating
fields.doc: Optional. The rating value required to get to the next level; omitted if the maximum level was reached

types.name: StoryAreaPosition
types.doc: Describes the position of a clickable area within a story.

fields.name: XPercentage
fields.doc: The abscissa of the area's center, as a percentage of the media width

fields.name: YPercentage
fields.doc: The ordinate of the area's center, as a percentage of the media height

fields.name: WidthPercentage
fields.doc: The width of the area's rectangle, as a percentage of the media width

fields.name: HeightPercentage
fields.doc: The height of the area's rectangle, as a percentage of the media height

fields.name: RotationAngle
fields.doc: The clockwise rotation angle of the rectangle, in degrees; 0-360

fields.name: CornerRadiusPercentage
fields.doc: The radius of the rectangle corner rounding, as a percentage of the media width

types.name: LocationAddress
types.doc: Describes the physical address of a location.

fields.name: CountryCode
fields.doc: The two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the country where the location is located

fields.name: State
fields.doc: Optional. State of the location

fields.name: City
fields.doc: Optional. City of the location

fields.name: Street
fields.doc: Optional. Street address of the location

types.name: StoryAreaType
types.doc: Describes the type of a clickable area on a story. Currently, it can be one of

types.name: StoryAreaTypeLocation
types.doc: Describes a story area pointing to a location. Currently, a story can have up to 10 location areas.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the area, always “location”

fields.name: Latitude
fields.doc: Location latitude in degrees

fields.name: Longitude
fields.doc: Location longitude in degrees

fields.name: Address
fields.doc: Optional. Address of the location

types.name: StoryAreaTypeSuggestedReaction
types.doc: Describes a story area pointing to a suggested reaction. Currently, a story can have up to 5 suggested reaction areas.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the area, always “suggested_reaction”

fields.name: ReactionType
fields.doc: Type of the reaction

fields.name: IsDark
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the reaction area has a dark background

fields.name: IsFlipped
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if reaction area corner is flipped

types.name: StoryAreaTypeLink
types.doc: Describes a story area pointing to an HTTP or tg:// link. Currently, a story can have up to 3 link areas.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the area, always “link”

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: HTTP or tg:// URL to be opened when the area is clicked

types.name: StoryAreaTypeWeather
types.doc: Describes a story area containing weather information. Currently, a story can have up to 3 weather areas.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the area, always “weather”

fields.name: Temperature
fields.doc: Temperature, in degree Celsius

fields.name: Emoji
fields.doc: Emoji representing the weather

fields.name: BackgroundColor
fields.doc: A color of the area background in the ARGB format

types.name: StoryAreaTypeUniqueGift
types.doc: Describes a story area pointing to a unique gift. Currently, a story can have at most 1 unique gift area.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the area, always “unique_gift”

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Unique name of the gift

types.name: StoryArea
types.doc: Describes a clickable area on a story media.

fields.name: Position
fields.doc: Position of the area

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the area

types.name: ChatLocation
types.doc: Represents a location to which a chat is connected.

fields.name: Location
fields.doc: The location to which the supergroup is connected. Can't be a live location.

fields.name: Address
fields.doc: Location address; 1-64 characters, as defined by the chat owner

types.name: ReactionType
types.doc: This object describes the type of a reaction. Currently, it can be one of

types.name: ReactionTypeEmoji
types.doc: The reaction is based on an emoji.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the reaction, always “emoji”

types.name: ReactionTypeCustomEmoji
types.doc: The reaction is based on a custom emoji.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the reaction, always “custom_emoji”

fields.name: CustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Custom emoji identifier

types.name: ReactionTypePaid
types.doc: The reaction is paid.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the reaction, always “paid”

types.name: ReactionCount
types.doc: Represents a reaction added to a message along with the number of times it was added.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the reaction

fields.name: TotalCount
fields.doc: Number of times the reaction was added

types.name: MessageReactionUpdated
types.doc: This object represents a change of a reaction on a message performed by a user.

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: The chat containing the message the user reacted to

fields.name: MessageID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the message inside the chat

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Optional. The user that changed the reaction, if the user isn't anonymous

fields.name: ActorChat
fields.doc: Optional. The chat on behalf of which the reaction was changed, if the user is anonymous

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date of the change in Unix time

fields.name: OldReaction
fields.doc: Previous list of reaction types that were set by the user

fields.name: NewReaction
fields.doc: New list of reaction types that have been set by the user

types.name: MessageReactionCountUpdated
types.doc: This object represents reaction changes on a message with anonymous reactions.

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: The chat containing the message

fields.name: MessageID
fields.doc: Unique message identifier inside the chat

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date of the change in Unix time

fields.name: Reactions
fields.doc: List of reactions that are present on the message

types.name: ForumTopic
types.doc: This object represents a forum topic.

fields.name: MessageThreadID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the forum topic

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name of the topic

fields.name: IconColor
fields.doc: Color of the topic icon in RGB format

fields.name: IconCustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier of the custom emoji shown as the topic icon

fields.name: IsNameImplicit
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the name of the topic wasn't specified explicitly by its creator and likely needs to be changed by the bot

types.name: GiftBackground
types.doc: This object describes the background of a gift.

fields.name: CenterColor
fields.doc: Center color of the background in RGB format

fields.name: EdgeColor
fields.doc: Edge color of the background in RGB format

fields.name: TextColor
fields.doc: Text color of the background in RGB format

types.name: Gift
types.doc: This object represents a gift that can be sent by the bot.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the gift

fields.name: Sticker
fields.doc: The sticker that represents the gift

fields.name: StarCount
fields.doc: The number of Telegram Stars that must be paid to send the sticker

fields.name: UpgradeStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of Telegram Stars that must be paid to upgrade the gift to a unique one

fields.name: IsPremium
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift can only be purchased by Telegram Premium subscribers

fields.name: HasColors
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift can be used (after being upgraded) to customize a user's appearance

fields.name: TotalCount
fields.doc: Optional. The total number of gifts of this type that can be sent by all users; for limited gifts only

fields.name: RemainingCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of remaining gifts of this type that can be sent by all users; for limited gifts only

fields.name: PersonalTotalCount
fields.doc: Optional. The total number of gifts of this type that can be sent by the bot; for limited gifts only

fields.name: PersonalRemainingCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of remaining gifts of this type that can be sent by the bot; for limited gifts only

fields.name: Background
fields.doc: Optional. Background of the gift

fields.name: UniqueGiftVariantCount
fields.doc: Optional. The total number of different unique gifts that can be obtained by upgrading the gift

fields.name: PublisherChat
fields.doc: Optional. Information about the chat that published the gift

types.name: Gifts
types.doc: This object represent a list of gifts.

fields.name: Gifts
fields.doc: The list of gifts

types.name: UniqueGiftModel
types.doc: This object describes the model of a unique gift.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name of the model

fields.name: Sticker
fields.doc: The sticker that represents the unique gift

fields.name: RarityPerMille
fields.doc: The number of unique gifts that receive this model for every 1000 gift upgrades. Always 0 for crafted gifts.

fields.name: Rarity
fields.doc: Optional. Rarity of the model if it is a crafted model. Currently, can be “uncommon”, “rare”, “epic”, or “legendary”.

types.name: UniqueGiftSymbol
types.doc: This object describes the symbol shown on the pattern of a unique gift.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name of the symbol

fields.name: Sticker
fields.doc: The sticker that represents the unique gift

fields.name: RarityPerMille
fields.doc: The number of unique gifts that receive this model for every 1000 gifts upgraded

types.name: UniqueGiftBackdropColors
types.doc: This object describes the colors of the backdrop of a unique gift.

fields.name: CenterColor
fields.doc: The color in the center of the backdrop in RGB format

fields.name: EdgeColor
fields.doc: The color on the edges of the backdrop in RGB format

fields.name: SymbolColor
fields.doc: The color to be applied to the symbol in RGB format

fields.name: TextColor
fields.doc: The color for the text on the backdrop in RGB format

types.name: UniqueGiftBackdrop
types.doc: This object describes the backdrop of a unique gift.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name of the backdrop

fields.name: Colors
fields.doc: Colors of the backdrop

fields.name: RarityPerMille
fields.doc: The number of unique gifts that receive this backdrop for every 1000 gifts upgraded

types.name: UniqueGiftColors
types.doc: This object contains information about the color scheme for a user's name, message replies and link previews based on a unique gift.

fields.name: ModelCustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Custom emoji identifier of the unique gift's model

fields.name: SymbolCustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Custom emoji identifier of the unique gift's symbol

fields.name: LightThemeMainColor
fields.doc: Main color used in light themes; RGB format

fields.name: LightThemeOtherColors
fields.doc: List of 1-3 additional colors used in light themes; RGB format

fields.name: DarkThemeMainColor
fields.doc: Main color used in dark themes; RGB format

fields.name: DarkThemeOtherColors
fields.doc: List of 1-3 additional colors used in dark themes; RGB format

types.name: UniqueGift
types.doc: This object describes a unique gift that was upgraded from a regular gift.

fields.name: GiftID
fields.doc: Identifier of the regular gift from which the gift was upgraded

fields.name: BaseName
fields.doc: Human-readable name of the regular gift from which this unique gift was upgraded

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Unique name of the gift. This name can be used in https://t.me/nft/... links and story areas.

fields.name: Number
fields.doc: Unique number of the upgraded gift among gifts upgraded from the same regular gift

fields.name: Model
fields.doc: Model of the gift

fields.name: Symbol
fields.doc: Symbol of the gift

fields.name: Backdrop
fields.doc: Backdrop of the gift

fields.name: IsPremium
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the original regular gift was exclusively purchaseable by Telegram Premium subscribers

fields.name: IsBurned
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift was used to craft another gift and isn't available anymore

fields.name: IsFromBlockchain
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift is assigned from the TON blockchain and can't be resold or transferred in Telegram

fields.name: Colors
fields.doc: Optional. The color scheme that can be used by the gift's owner for the chat's name, replies to messages and link previews; for business account gifts and gifts that are currently on sale only

fields.name: PublisherChat
fields.doc: Optional. Information about the chat that published the gift

types.name: GiftInfo
types.doc: Describes a service message about a regular gift that was sent or received.

fields.name: Gift
fields.doc: Information about the gift

fields.name: OwnedGiftID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier of the received gift for the bot; only present for gifts received on behalf of business accounts

fields.name: ConvertStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. Number of Telegram Stars that can be claimed by the receiver by converting the gift; omitted if conversion to Telegram Stars is impossible

fields.name: PrepaidUpgradeStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. Number of Telegram Stars that were prepaid for the ability to upgrade the gift

fields.name: IsUpgradeSeparate
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift's upgrade was purchased after the gift was sent

fields.name: CanBeUpgraded
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift can be upgraded to a unique gift

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Optional. Text of the message that was added to the gift

fields.name: Entities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities that appear in the text

fields.name: IsPrivate
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the sender and gift text are shown only to the gift receiver; otherwise, everyone will be able to see them

fields.name: UniqueGiftNumber
fields.doc: Optional. Unique number reserved for this gift when upgraded. See the number field in UniqueGift.

types.name: UniqueGiftInfo
types.doc: Describes a service message about a unique gift that was sent or received.

fields.name: Gift
fields.doc: Information about the gift

fields.name: Origin
fields.doc: Origin of the gift. Currently, either “upgrade” for gifts upgraded from regular gifts, “transfer” for gifts transferred from other users or channels, “resale” for gifts bought from other users, “gifted_upgrade” for upgrades purchased after the gift was sent, or “offer” for gifts bought or sold through gift purchase offers.

fields.name: LastResaleCurrency
fields.doc: Optional. For gifts bought from other users, the currency in which the payment for the gift was done. Currently, one of “XTR” for Telegram Stars or “TON” for toncoins.

fields.name: LastResaleAmount
fields.doc: Optional. For gifts bought from other users, the price paid for the gift in either Telegram Stars or nanotoncoins

fields.name: OwnedGiftID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier of the received gift for the bot; only present for gifts received on behalf of business accounts

fields.name: TransferStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. Number of Telegram Stars that must be paid to transfer the gift; omitted if the bot cannot transfer the gift

fields.name: NextTransferDate
fields.doc: Optional. Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the gift can be transferred. If it is in the past, then the gift can be transferred now.

types.name: OwnedGift
types.doc: This object describes a gift received and owned by a user or a chat. Currently, it can be one of

types.name: OwnedGiftRegular
types.doc: Describes a regular gift owned by a user or a chat.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the gift, always “regular”

fields.name: Gift
fields.doc: Information about the regular gift

fields.name: OwnedGiftID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier of the gift for the bot; for gifts received on behalf of business accounts only

fields.name: SenderUser
fields.doc: Optional. Sender of the gift if it is a known user

fields.name: SendDate
fields.doc: Date the gift was sent in Unix time

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Optional. Text of the message that was added to the gift

fields.name: Entities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities that appear in the text

fields.name: IsPrivate
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the sender and gift text are shown only to the gift receiver; otherwise, everyone will be able to see them

fields.name: IsSaved
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift is displayed on the account's profile page; for gifts received on behalf of business accounts only

fields.name: CanBeUpgraded
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift can be upgraded to a unique gift; for gifts received on behalf of business accounts only

fields.name: WasRefunded
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift was refunded and isn't available anymore

fields.name: ConvertStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. Number of Telegram Stars that can be claimed by the receiver instead of the gift; omitted if the gift cannot be converted to Telegram Stars; for gifts received on behalf of business accounts only

fields.name: PrepaidUpgradeStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. Number of Telegram Stars that were paid for the ability to upgrade the gift

fields.name: IsUpgradeSeparate
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift's upgrade was purchased after the gift was sent; for gifts received on behalf of business accounts only

fields.name: UniqueGiftNumber
fields.doc: Optional. Unique number reserved for this gift when upgraded. See the number field in UniqueGift.

types.name: OwnedGiftUnique
types.doc: Describes a unique gift received and owned by a user or a chat.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the gift, always “unique”

fields.name: Gift
fields.doc: Information about the unique gift

fields.name: OwnedGiftID
fields.doc: Optional. Unique identifier of the received gift for the bot; for gifts received on behalf of business accounts only

fields.name: SenderUser
fields.doc: Optional. Sender of the gift if it is a known user

fields.name: SendDate
fields.doc: Date the gift was sent in Unix time

fields.name: IsSaved
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift is displayed on the account's profile page; for gifts received on behalf of business accounts only

fields.name: CanBeTransferred
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the gift can be transferred to another owner; for gifts received on behalf of business accounts only

fields.name: TransferStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. Number of Telegram Stars that must be paid to transfer the gift; omitted if the bot cannot transfer the gift

fields.name: NextTransferDate
fields.doc: Optional. Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the gift can be transferred. If it is in the past, then the gift can be transferred now.

types.name: OwnedGifts
types.doc: Contains the list of gifts received and owned by a user or a chat.

fields.name: TotalCount
fields.doc: The total number of gifts owned by the user or the chat

fields.name: Gifts
fields.doc: The list of gifts

fields.name: NextOffset
fields.doc: Optional. Offset for the next request. If empty, then there are no more results.

types.name: BotAccessSettings
types.doc: This object describes the access settings of a bot.

fields.name: IsAccessRestricted
fields.doc: True, if only selected users can access the bot. The bot's owner can always access it.

fields.name: AddedUsers
fields.doc: Optional. The list of other users who have access to the bot if the access is restricted

types.name: AcceptedGiftTypes
types.doc: This object describes the types of gifts that can be gifted to a user or a chat.

fields.name: UnlimitedGifts
fields.doc: True, if unlimited regular gifts are accepted

fields.name: LimitedGifts
fields.doc: True, if limited regular gifts are accepted

fields.name: UniqueGifts
fields.doc: True, if unique gifts or gifts that can be upgraded to unique for free are accepted

fields.name: PremiumSubscription
fields.doc: True, if a Telegram Premium subscription is accepted

fields.name: GiftsFromChannels
fields.doc: True, if transfers of unique gifts from channels are accepted

types.name: StarAmount
types.doc: Describes an amount of Telegram Stars.

fields.name: Amount
fields.doc: Integer amount of Telegram Stars, rounded to 0; can be negative

fields.name: NanostarAmount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of 1/1000000000 shares of Telegram Stars; from -999999999 to 999999999; can be negative if and only if amount is non-positive

types.name: BotCommand
types.doc: This object represents a bot command.

fields.name: Command
fields.doc: Text of the command; 1-32 characters. Can contain only lowercase English letters, digits and underscores.

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: Description of the command; 1-256 characters

types.name: BotCommandScope
types.doc: This object represents the scope to which bot commands are applied. Currently, the following 7 scopes are supported:

types.name: BotCommandScopeDefault
types.doc: Represents the default scope of bot commands. Default commands are used if no commands with a narrower scope are specified for the user.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Scope type, must be default

types.name: BotCommandScopeAllPrivateChats
types.doc: Represents the scope of bot commands, covering all private chats.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Scope type, must be all_private_chats

types.name: BotCommandScopeAllGroupChats
types.doc: Represents the scope of bot commands, covering all group and supergroup chats.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Scope type, must be all_group_chats

types.name: BotCommandScopeAllChatAdministrators
types.doc: Represents the scope of bot commands, covering all group and supergroup chat administrators.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Scope type, must be all_chat_administrators

types.name: BotCommandScopeChat
types.doc: Represents the scope of bot commands, covering a specific chat.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Scope type, must be chat

fields.name: ChatID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username. Channel direct messages chats and channel chats aren't supported.

types.name: BotCommandScopeChatAdministrators
types.doc: Represents the scope of bot commands, covering all administrators of a specific group or supergroup chat.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Scope type, must be chat_administrators

fields.name: ChatID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username. Channel direct messages chats and channel chats aren't supported.

types.name: BotCommandScopeChatMember
types.doc: Represents the scope of bot commands, covering a specific member of a group or supergroup chat.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Scope type, must be chat_member

fields.name: ChatID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username. Channel direct messages chats and channel chats aren't supported.

fields.name: UserID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

types.name: BotName
types.doc: This object represents the bot's name.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: The bot's name

types.name: BotDescription
types.doc: This object represents the bot's description.

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: The bot's description

types.name: BotShortDescription
types.doc: This object represents the bot's short description.

fields.name: ShortDescription
fields.doc: The bot's short description

types.name: MenuButton
types.doc:
This object describes the bot's menu button in a private chat. It should be one of
If a menu button other than MenuButtonDefault is set for a private chat, then it is applied in the chat. Otherwise the default menu button is applied. By default, the menu button opens the list of bot commands.

types.name: MenuButtonCommands
types.doc: Represents a menu button, which opens the bot's list of commands.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the button, must be commands

types.name: MenuButtonWebApp
types.doc: Represents a menu button, which launches a Web App.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the button, must be web_app

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Text on the button

fields.name: WebApp
fields.doc: Description of the Web App that will be launched when the user presses the button. The Web App will be able to send an arbitrary message on behalf of the user using the method answerWebAppQuery. Alternatively, a t.me link to a Web App of the bot can be specified in the object instead of the Web App's URL, in which case the Web App will be opened as if the user pressed the link.

types.name: MenuButtonDefault
types.doc: Describes that no specific value for the menu button was set.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the button, must be default

types.name: ChatBoostSource
types.doc: This object describes the source of a chat boost. It can be one of

types.name: ChatBoostSourcePremium
types.doc: The boost was obtained by subscribing to Telegram Premium or by gifting a Telegram Premium subscription to another user.

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Source of the boost, always “premium”

fields.name: User
fields.doc: User that boosted the chat

types.name: ChatBoostSourceGiftCode
types.doc: The boost was obtained by the creation of Telegram Premium gift codes to boost a chat. Each such code boosts the chat 4 times for the duration of the corresponding Telegram Premium subscription.

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Source of the boost, always “gift_code”

fields.name: User
fields.doc: User for which the gift code was created

types.name: ChatBoostSourceGiveaway
types.doc: The boost was obtained by the creation of a Telegram Premium or a Telegram Star giveaway. This boosts the chat 4 times for the duration of the corresponding Telegram Premium subscription for Telegram Premium giveaways and prize_star_count / 500 times for one year for Telegram Star giveaways.

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Source of the boost, always “giveaway”

fields.name: GiveawayMessageID
fields.doc: Identifier of a message in the chat with the giveaway; the message could have been deleted already. May be 0 if the message isn't sent yet.

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Optional. User that won the prize in the giveaway if any; for Telegram Premium giveaways only

fields.name: PrizeStarCount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of Telegram Stars to be split between giveaway winners; for Telegram Star giveaways only

fields.name: IsUnclaimed
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the giveaway was completed, but there was no user to win the prize

types.name: ChatBoost
types.doc: This object contains information about a chat boost.

fields.name: BoostID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the boost

fields.name: AddDate
fields.doc: Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the chat was boosted

fields.name: ExpirationDate
fields.doc: Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the boost will automatically expire, unless the booster's Telegram Premium subscription is prolonged

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Source of the added boost

types.name: ChatBoostUpdated
types.doc: This object represents a boost added to a chat or changed.

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: Chat which was boosted

fields.name: Boost
fields.doc: Information about the chat boost

types.name: ChatBoostRemoved
types.doc: This object represents a boost removed from a chat.

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: Chat which was boosted

fields.name: BoostID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the boost

fields.name: RemoveDate
fields.doc: Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the boost was removed

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Source of the removed boost

types.name: ChatOwnerLeft
types.doc: Describes a service message about the chat owner leaving the chat.

fields.name: NewOwner
fields.doc: Optional. The user who will become the new owner of the chat if the previous owner does not return to the chat

types.name: ChatOwnerChanged
types.doc: Describes a service message about an ownership change in the chat.

fields.name: NewOwner
fields.doc: The new owner of the chat

types.name: UserChatBoosts
types.doc: This object represents a list of boosts added to a chat by a user.

fields.name: Boosts
fields.doc: The list of boosts added to the chat by the user

types.name: BusinessBotRights
types.doc: Represents the rights of a business bot.

fields.name: CanReply
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can send and edit messages in the private chats that had incoming messages in the last 24 hours

fields.name: CanReadMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can mark incoming private messages as read

fields.name: CanDeleteSentMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can delete messages sent by the bot

fields.name: CanDeleteAllMessages
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can delete all private messages in managed chats

fields.name: CanEditName
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can edit the first and last name of the business account

fields.name: CanEditBio
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can edit the bio of the business account

fields.name: CanEditProfilePhoto
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can edit the profile photo of the business account

fields.name: CanEditUsername
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can edit the username of the business account

fields.name: CanChangeGiftSettings
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can change the privacy settings pertaining to gifts for the business account

fields.name: CanViewGiftsAndStars
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can view gifts and the amount of Telegram Stars owned by the business account

fields.name: CanConvertGiftsToStars
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can convert regular gifts owned by the business account to Telegram Stars

fields.name: CanTransferAndUpgradeGifts
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can transfer and upgrade gifts owned by the business account

fields.name: CanTransferStars
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can transfer Telegram Stars received by the business account to its own account, or use them to upgrade and transfer gifts

fields.name: CanManageStories
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the bot can post, edit and delete stories on behalf of the business account

types.name: BusinessConnection
types.doc: Describes the connection of the bot with a business account.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Business account user that created the business connection

fields.name: UserChatID
fields.doc: Identifier of a private chat with the user who created the business connection. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier.

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date the connection was established in Unix time

fields.name: Rights
fields.doc: Optional. Rights of the business bot

fields.name: IsEnabled
fields.doc: True, if the connection is active

types.name: BusinessMessagesDeleted
types.doc: This object is received when messages are deleted from a connected business account.

fields.name: BusinessConnectionID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: Information about a chat in the business account. The bot may not have access to the chat or the corresponding user.

fields.name: MessageIds
fields.doc: The list of identifiers of deleted messages in the chat of the business account

types.name: SentWebAppMessage
types.doc: Describes an inline message sent by a Web App on behalf of a user.

fields.name: InlineMessageID
fields.doc: Optional. Identifier of the sent inline message. Available only if there is an inline keyboard attached to the message.

types.name: SentGuestMessage
types.doc: Describes an inline message sent by a guest bot.

fields.name: InlineMessageID
