fields.doc: Identifier of the sent inline message

types.name: PreparedInlineMessage
types.doc: Describes an inline message to be sent by a user of a Mini App.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the prepared message

fields.name: ExpirationDate
fields.doc: Expiration date of the prepared message, in Unix time. Expired prepared messages can no longer be used.

types.name: PreparedKeyboardButton
types.doc: Describes a keyboard button to be used by a user of a Mini App.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the keyboard button

types.name: ResponseParameters
types.doc: Describes why a request was unsuccessful.

fields.name: MigrateToChatID
fields.doc: Optional. The group has been migrated to a supergroup with the specified identifier. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier.

fields.name: RetryAfter
fields.doc: Optional. In case of exceeding flood control, the number of seconds left to wait before the request can be repeated

types.name: InputMedia
types.doc: This object represents the content of a media message to be sent. It should be one of

types.name: InputMediaAnimation
types.doc: Represents an animation file (GIF or H.264/MPEG-4 AVC video without sound) to be sent.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the result, must be animation

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: File to send. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended), pass an HTTP URL for Telegram to get a file from the Internet, or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Thumbnail
fields.doc: Optional. Thumbnail of the file sent; can be ignored if thumbnail generation for the file is supported server-side. The thumbnail should be in JPEG format and less than 200 kB in size. A thumbnail's width and height should not exceed 320. Ignored if the file is not uploaded using multipart/form-data. Thumbnails can't be reused and can be only uploaded as a new file, so you can pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” if the thumbnail was uploaded using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name>. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Caption
fields.doc: Optional. Caption of the animation to be sent, 0-1024 characters after entities parsing

fields.name: ParseMode
fields.doc: Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the animation caption. See formatting options for more details.

fields.name: CaptionEntities
fields.doc: Optional. List of special entities that appear in the caption, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

fields.name: ShowCaptionAboveMedia
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True, if the caption must be shown above the message media

fields.name: Width
fields.doc: Optional. Animation width

fields.name: Height
fields.doc: Optional. Animation height

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Optional. Animation duration in seconds

fields.name: HasSpoiler
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the animation needs to be covered with a spoiler animation

types.name: InputMediaAudio
types.doc: Represents an audio file to be treated as music to be sent.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the result, must be audio

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: File to send. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended), pass an HTTP URL for Telegram to get a file from the Internet, or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Thumbnail
fields.doc: Optional. Thumbnail of the file sent; can be ignored if thumbnail generation for the file is supported server-side. The thumbnail should be in JPEG format and less than 200 kB in size. A thumbnail's width and height should not exceed 320. Ignored if the file is not uploaded using multipart/form-data. Thumbnails can't be reused and can be only uploaded as a new file, so you can pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” if the thumbnail was uploaded using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name>. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Caption
fields.doc: Optional. Caption of the audio to be sent, 0-1024 characters after entities parsing

fields.name: ParseMode
fields.doc: Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the audio caption. See formatting options for more details.

fields.name: CaptionEntities
fields.doc: Optional. List of special entities that appear in the caption, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Optional. Duration of the audio in seconds

fields.name: Performer
fields.doc: Optional. Performer of the audio

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Optional. Title of the audio

types.name: InputMediaDocument
types.doc: Represents a general file to be sent.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the result, must be document

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: File to send. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended), pass an HTTP URL for Telegram to get a file from the Internet, or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Thumbnail
fields.doc: Optional. Thumbnail of the file sent; can be ignored if thumbnail generation for the file is supported server-side. The thumbnail should be in JPEG format and less than 200 kB in size. A thumbnail's width and height should not exceed 320. Ignored if the file is not uploaded using multipart/form-data. Thumbnails can't be reused and can be only uploaded as a new file, so you can pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” if the thumbnail was uploaded using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name>. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Caption
fields.doc: Optional. Caption of the document to be sent, 0-1024 characters after entities parsing

fields.name: ParseMode
fields.doc: Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the document caption. See formatting options for more details.

fields.name: CaptionEntities
fields.doc: Optional. List of special entities that appear in the caption, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

fields.name: DisableContentTypeDetection
fields.doc: Optional. Disables automatic server-side content type detection for files uploaded using multipart/form-data. Always True, if the document is sent as part of an album.

types.name: InputMediaLivePhoto
types.doc: Represents a live photo to be sent.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the result, must be live_photo

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: Video of the live photo to send. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended) or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. More information on Sending Files ». Sending live photos by a URL is currently unsupported.

fields.name: Photo
fields.doc: The static photo to send. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended) or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. More information on Sending Files ». Sending live photos by a URL is currently unsupported.

fields.name: Caption
fields.doc: Optional. Caption of the live photo to be sent, 0-1024 characters after entities parsing

fields.name: ParseMode
fields.doc: Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the live photo caption. See formatting options for more details.

fields.name: CaptionEntities
fields.doc: Optional. List of special entities that appear in the caption, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

fields.name: ShowCaptionAboveMedia
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True, if the caption must be shown above the message media

fields.name: HasSpoiler
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the live photo needs to be covered with a spoiler animation

types.doc: Represents a location to be sent.

fields.name: Longitude
fields.doc: Longitude of the location

fields.name: HorizontalAccuracy
fields.doc: Optional. The radius of uncertainty for the location, measured in meters; 0-1500

types.name: InputMediaPhoto
types.doc: Represents a photo to be sent.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the result, must be photo

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: File to send. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended), pass an HTTP URL for Telegram to get a file from the Internet, or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Caption
fields.doc: Optional. Caption of the photo to be sent, 0-1024 characters after entities parsing

fields.name: ParseMode
fields.doc: Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the photo caption. See formatting options for more details.

fields.name: CaptionEntities
fields.doc: Optional. List of special entities that appear in the caption, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

fields.name: ShowCaptionAboveMedia
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True, if the caption must be shown above the message media

fields.name: HasSpoiler
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the photo needs to be covered with a spoiler animation

types.name: InputMediaSticker
types.doc: Represents a sticker file to be sent.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the result, must be sticker

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: File to send. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended), pass an HTTP URL for Telegram to get a .WEBP sticker from the Internet, or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new .WEBP, .TGS, or .WEBM sticker using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Emoji
fields.doc: Optional. Emoji associated with the sticker; only for just uploaded stickers

types.name: InputMediaVenue
types.doc: Represents a venue to be sent.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the result, must be venue

fields.name: Latitude
fields.doc: Latitude of the location

fields.name: Longitude
fields.doc: Longitude of the location

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Name of the venue

fields.name: Address
fields.doc: Address of the venue

fields.name: FoursquareID
fields.doc: Optional. Foursquare identifier of the venue

fields.name: FoursquareType
fields.doc: Optional. Foursquare type of the venue, if known. (For example, “arts_entertainment/default”, “arts_entertainment/aquarium” or “food/icecream”.)

fields.name: GooglePlaceID
fields.doc: Optional. Google Places identifier of the venue

fields.name: GooglePlaceType
fields.doc: Optional. Google Places type of the venue. (See supported types.)

types.name: InputMediaVideo
types.doc: Represents a video to be sent.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the result, must be video

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: File to send. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended), pass an HTTP URL for Telegram to get a file from the Internet, or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Thumbnail
fields.doc: Optional. Thumbnail of the file sent; can be ignored if thumbnail generation for the file is supported server-side. The thumbnail should be in JPEG format and less than 200 kB in size. A thumbnail's width and height should not exceed 320. Ignored if the file is not uploaded using multipart/form-data. Thumbnails can't be reused and can be only uploaded as a new file, so you can pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” if the thumbnail was uploaded using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name>. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Cover
fields.doc: Optional. Cover for the video in the message. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended), pass an HTTP URL for Telegram to get a file from the Internet, or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: StartTimestamp
fields.doc: Optional. Start timestamp for the video in the message

fields.name: Caption
fields.doc: Optional. Caption of the video to be sent, 0-1024 characters after entities parsing

fields.name: ParseMode
fields.doc: Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the video caption. See formatting options for more details.

fields.name: CaptionEntities
fields.doc: Optional. List of special entities that appear in the caption, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

fields.name: ShowCaptionAboveMedia
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True, if the caption must be shown above the message media

fields.name: Width
fields.doc: Optional. Video width

fields.name: Height
fields.doc: Optional. Video height

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Optional. Video duration in seconds

fields.name: SupportsStreaming
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the uploaded video is suitable for streaming

fields.name: HasSpoiler
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the video needs to be covered with a spoiler animation

types.name: InputFile
types.doc: This object represents the contents of a file to be uploaded. Must be posted using multipart/form-data in the usual way that files are uploaded via the browser.

types.name: InputPaidMedia
types.doc: This object describes the paid media to be sent. Currently, it can be one of

fields.name: Type

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: Video of the live photo to send. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended) or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. More information on Sending Files ». Sending live photos by a URL is currently unsupported.

fields.name: Photo

More information on Sending Files ». Sending live photos by a URL is currently unsupported.

types.name: InputPaidMediaPhoto

fields.name: Type

fields.name: Media
fields.doc: File to send. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended), pass an HTTP URL for Telegram to get a file from the Internet, or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. fields.doc: File to send. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended), pass an HTTP URL for Telegram to get a file from the Internet, or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. A thumbnail's width and height should not exceed 320. Ignored if the file is not uploaded using multipart/form-data. Pass a file_id to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended), pass an HTTP URL for Telegram to get a file from the Internet, or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new one using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: StartTimestamp
fields.doc: Optional. Start timestamp for the video in the message

fields.name: Width
fields.doc: Optional. Video width

fields.name: Height
fields.doc: Optional. Video height

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Optional. Video duration in seconds

fields.name: SupportsStreaming
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the uploaded video is suitable for streaming

types.name: InputProfilePhoto
types.doc: This object describes a profile photo to set. Currently, it can be one of

types.name: InputProfilePhotoStatic
types.doc: A static profile photo in the .JPG format.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the profile photo, must be static

fields.name: Photo
fields.doc: The static profile photo. Profile photos can't be reused and can only be uploaded as a new file, so you can pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” if the photo was uploaded using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name>. More information on Sending Files »

types.name: InputProfilePhotoAnimated
types.doc: An animated profile photo in the MPEG4 format.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the profile photo, must be animated

fields.name: Animation
fields.doc: The animated profile photo. Profile photos can't be reused and can only be uploaded as a new file, so you can pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” if the photo was uploaded using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name>. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: MainFrameTimestamp
fields.doc: Optional. Timestamp in seconds of the frame that will be used as the static profile photo. Defaults to 0.0.

types.name: InputStoryContent
types.doc: This object describes the content of a story to post. Currently, it can be one of

types.name: InputStoryContentPhoto
types.doc: Describes a photo to post as a story.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the content, must be photo

fields.name: Photo
fields.doc: The photo to post as a story. The photo must be of the size 1080x1920 and must not exceed 10 MB. The photo can't be reused and can only be uploaded as a new file, so you can pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” if the photo was uploaded using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name>. More information on Sending Files »

types.name: InputStoryContentVideo
types.doc: Describes a video to post as a story.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the content, must be video

fields.name: Video
fields.doc: The video to post as a story. The video must be of the size 720x1280, streamable, encoded with H.265 codec, with key frames added each second in the MPEG4 format, and must not exceed 30 MB. The video can't be reused and can only be uploaded as a new file, so you can pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” if the video was uploaded using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name>. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Duration
fields.doc: Optional. Precise duration of the video in seconds; 0-60

fields.name: CoverFrameTimestamp
fields.doc: Optional. Timestamp in seconds of the frame that will be used as the static cover for the story. Defaults to 0.0.

fields.name: IsAnimation
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the video has no sound

types.name: Sticker
types.doc: This object represents a sticker.

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the sticker, currently one of “regular”, “mask”, “custom_emoji”. The type of the sticker is independent from its format, which is determined by the fields is_animated and is_video.

fields.name: Width
fields.doc: Sticker width

fields.name: Height
fields.doc: Sticker height

fields.name: IsAnimated
fields.doc: True, if the sticker is animated

fields.name: IsVideo
fields.doc: True, if the sticker is a video sticker

fields.name: Thumbnail
fields.doc: Optional. Sticker thumbnail in the .WEBP or .JPG format

fields.name: Emoji
fields.doc: Optional. Emoji associated with the sticker

fields.name: SetName
fields.doc: Optional. Name of the sticker set to which the sticker belongs

fields.name: PremiumAnimation
fields.doc: Optional. For premium regular stickers, premium animation for the sticker

fields.name: MaskPosition
fields.doc: Optional. For mask stickers, the position where the mask should be placed

fields.name: CustomEmojiID
fields.doc: Optional. For custom emoji stickers, unique identifier of the custom emoji

fields.name: NeedsRepainting
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the sticker must be repainted to a text color in messages, the color of the Telegram Premium badge in emoji status, white color on chat photos, or another appropriate color in other places

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: Optional. File size in bytes

types.name: StickerSet
types.doc: This object represents a sticker set.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Sticker set name

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Sticker set title

fields.name: StickerType
fields.doc: Type of stickers in the set, currently one of “regular”, “mask”, “custom_emoji”

fields.name: Stickers
fields.doc: List of all set stickers

fields.name: Thumbnail
fields.doc: Optional. Sticker set thumbnail in the .WEBP, .TGS, or .WEBM format

types.name: MaskPosition
types.doc: This object describes the position on faces where a mask should be placed by default.

fields.name: Point
fields.doc: The part of the face relative to which the mask should be placed. One of “forehead”, “eyes”, “mouth”, or “chin”.

fields.name: XShift
fields.doc: Shift by X-axis measured in widths of the mask scaled to the face size, from left to right. For example, choosing -1.0 will place mask just to the left of the default mask position.

fields.name: YShift
fields.doc: Shift by Y-axis measured in heights of the mask scaled to the face size, from top to bottom. For example, 1.0 will place the mask just below the default mask position.

fields.name: Scale
fields.doc: Mask scaling coefficient. For example, 2.0 means double size.

types.name: InputSticker
types.doc: This object describes a sticker to be added to a sticker set.

fields.name: Sticker
fields.doc: The added sticker. Pass a file_id as a String to send a file that already exists on the Telegram servers, pass an HTTP URL as a String for Telegram to get a file from the Internet, or pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” to upload a new file using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name> name. Animated and video stickers can't be uploaded via HTTP URL. More information on Sending Files »

fields.name: Format
fields.doc: Format of the added sticker, must be one of “static” for a .WEBP or .PNG image, “animated” for a .TGS animation, “video” for a .WEBM video

fields.name: EmojiList
fields.doc: List of 1-20 emoji associated with the sticker

fields.name: MaskPosition
fields.doc: Optional. Position where the mask should be placed on faces. For “mask” stickers only.

fields.name: Keywords
fields.doc: Optional. List of 0-20 search keywords for the sticker with total length of up to 64 characters. For “regular” and “custom_emoji” stickers only.

types.name: InlineQuery
types.doc: This object represents an incoming inline query. When the user sends an empty query, your bot could return some default or trending results.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this query

fields.name: From
fields.doc: Sender

fields.name: Query
fields.doc: Text of the query (up to 256 characters)

fields.name: Offset
fields.doc: Offset of the results to be returned, can be controlled by the bot

fields.name: ChatType
fields.doc: Optional. Type of the chat from which the inline query was sent. Can be either “sender” for a private chat with the inline query sender, “private”, “group”, “supergroup”, or “channel”. The chat type should be always known for requests sent from official clients and most third-party clients, unless the request was sent from a secret chat.

fields.name: Location
fields.doc: Optional. Sender location, only for bots that request user location

types.name: InlineQueryResultsButton
types.doc: This object represents a button to be shown above inline query results. You must use exactly one of the optional fields.

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Label text on the button

fields.name: WebApp
fields.doc: Optional. Description of the Web App that will be launched when the user presses the button. The Web App will be able to switch back to the inline mode using the method switchInlineQuery inside the Web App.

fields.name: StartParameter
fields.doc: Optional. Deep-linking parameter for the /start message sent to the bot when a user presses the button. 1-64 characters, only A-Z, a-z, 0-9, _ and - are allowed.Example: An inline bot that sends YouTube videos can ask the user to connect the bot to their YouTube account to adapt search results accordingly. To do this, it displays a 'Connect your YouTube account' button above the results, or even before showing any. The user presses the button, switches to a private chat with the bot and, in doing so, passes a start parameter that instructs the bot to return an OAuth link. Once done, the bot can offer a switch_inline button so that the user can easily return to the chat where they wanted to use the bot's inline capabilities.

types.name: InlineQueryResult
types.doc:
This object represents one result of an inline query. Telegram clients currently support results of the following 20 types:
Note: All URLs passed in inline query results will be available to end users and therefore must be assumed to be public.

types.name: InlineQueryResultArticle
types.doc: Represents a link to an article or web page.

fields.doc: Type of the result, must be article

fields.doc: Optional. Short description of the result

fields.doc: Optional. Url of the thumbnail for the result

fields.name: ThumbnailWidth
fields.doc: Optional. Thumbnail width

fields.name: ThumbnailHeight
fields.doc: Optional. Thumbnail height

types.name: InlineQueryResultVideo
types.doc:
Represents a link to a page containing an embedded video player or a video file. By default, this video file will be sent by the user with an optional caption. Alternatively, you can use input_message_content to send a message with the specified content instead of the video.
If an InlineQueryResultVideo message contains an embedded video (e.g., YouTube), you must replace its content using input_message_content.

fields.doc: URL of the thumbnail (JPEG only) for the video

fields.name: Title

fields.doc: Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the video caption. See formatting options for more details.

fields.doc: Optional. Video duration in seconds

fields.name: ReplyMarkup
fields.doc: Optional. Inline keyboard attached to the message

fields.name: InputMessageContent
fields.doc: Optional. Content of the message to be sent instead of the video. This field is required if InlineQueryResultVideo is used to send an HTML-page as a result (e.g., a YouTube video).

types.name: InputMessageContent
types.doc: This object represents the content of a message to be sent as a result of an inline query. Telegram clients currently support the following 5 types:

types.doc: Represents the content of a text message to be sent as the result of an inline query.

fields.name: ParseMode

fields.doc: Optional. For live locations, a direction in which the user is moving, in degrees. Must be between 1 and 360 if specified.

fields.name: ProximityAlertRadius
fields.doc: Optional. For live locations, a maximum distance for proximity alerts about approaching another chat member, in meters. Must be between 1 and 100000 if specified.

fields.doc: Optional. Google Places type of the venue. (See supported types.)

types.name: InputContactMessageContent
types.doc: Represents the content of a contact message to be sent as the result of an inline query.

fields.name: PhoneNumber
fields.doc: Contact's phone number

fields.name: FirstName
fields.doc: Contact's first name

fields.name: LastName
fields.doc: Optional. Contact's last name

fields.name: Vcard
fields.doc: Optional. Additional data about the contact in the form of a vCard, 0-2048 bytes

types.name: InputInvoiceMessageContent
types.doc: Represents the content of an invoice message to be sent as the result of an inline query.

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Product name, 1-32 characters

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: Product description, 1-255 characters

fields.name: Payload
fields.doc: Bot-defined invoice payload, 1-128 bytes. This will not be displayed to the user, use it for your internal processes.

fields.name: ProviderToken
fields.doc: Optional. Payment provider token, obtained via @BotFather. Pass an empty string for payments in Telegram Stars.

fields.name: Currency
fields.doc: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code, see more on currencies. Pass “XTR” for payments in Telegram Stars.

fields.name: Prices
fields.doc: Price breakdown, a JSON-serialized list of components (e.g. product price, tax, discount, delivery cost, delivery tax, bonus, etc.). Must contain exactly one item for payments in Telegram Stars.

fields.name: MaxTipAmount
fields.doc: Optional. The maximum accepted amount for tips in the smallest units of the currency (integer, not float/double). For example, for a maximum tip of US$ 1.45 pass max_tip_amount = 145. See the exp parameter in currencies.json, it shows the number of digits past the decimal point for each currency (2 for the majority of currencies). Defaults to 0. Not supported for payments in Telegram Stars.

fields.name: SuggestedTipAmounts
fields.doc: Optional. A JSON-serialized array of suggested amounts of tip in the smallest units of the currency (integer, not float/double). At most 4 suggested tip amounts can be specified. The suggested tip amounts must be positive, passed in a strictly increased order and must not exceed max_tip_amount.

fields.name: ProviderData
fields.doc: Optional. A JSON-serialized object for data about the invoice, which will be shared with the payment provider. A detailed description of the required fields should be provided by the payment provider.

fields.name: PhotoURL
fields.doc: Optional. URL of the product photo for the invoice. Can be a photo of the goods or a marketing image for a service.

fields.name: PhotoSize
fields.doc: Optional. Photo size in bytes

fields.name: PhotoWidth
fields.doc: Optional. Photo width

fields.name: PhotoHeight
fields.doc: Optional. Photo height

fields.name: NeedName
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if you require the user's full name to complete the order. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

fields.name: NeedPhoneNumber
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if you require the user's phone number to complete the order. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

fields.name: NeedEmail
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if you require the user's email address to complete the order. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

fields.name: NeedShippingAddress
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if you require the user's shipping address to complete the order. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

fields.name: SendPhoneNumberToProvider
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the user's phone number should be sent to the provider. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

fields.name: SendEmailToProvider
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the user's email address should be sent to the provider. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

fields.name: IsFlexible
fields.doc: Optional. Pass True if the final price depends on the shipping method. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

types.name: ChosenInlineResult
types.doc:
Represents a result of an inline query that was chosen by the user and sent to their chat partner.
Note: It is necessary to enable inline feedback via @BotFather in order to receive these objects in updates.

fields.name: ResultID
fields.doc: The unique identifier for the result that was chosen

fields.name: From
fields.doc: The user that chose the result

fields.name: Location
fields.doc: Optional. Sender location, only for bots that require user location

fields.name: InlineMessageID
fields.doc: Optional. Identifier of the sent inline message. Available only if there is an inline keyboard attached to the message. Will be also received in callback queries and can be used to edit the message.

fields.name: Query
fields.doc: The query that was used to obtain the result

types.name: LabeledPrice
types.doc: This object represents a portion of the price for goods or services.

fields.name: Label
fields.doc: Portion label

fields.name: Amount
fields.doc: Price of the product in the smallest units of the currency (integer, not float/double). For example, for a price of US$ 1.45 pass amount = 145. See the exp parameter in currencies.json, it shows the number of digits past the decimal point for each currency (2 for the majority of currencies).

types.name: Invoice
types.doc: This object contains basic information about an invoice.

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Product name

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: Product description

fields.name: StartParameter
fields.doc: Unique bot deep-linking parameter that can be used to generate this invoice

fields.name: Currency
fields.doc: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code, or “XTR” for payments in Telegram Stars

fields.name: TotalAmount
fields.doc: Total price in the smallest units of the currency (integer, not float/double). For example, for a price of US$ 1.45 pass amount = 145. See the exp parameter in currencies.json, it shows the number of digits past the decimal point for each currency (2 for the majority of currencies).

types.name: ShippingAddress
types.doc: This object represents a shipping address.

fields.name: CountryCode
fields.doc: Two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code

fields.name: State
fields.doc: State, if applicable

fields.name: City
fields.doc: City

fields.name: StreetLine1
fields.doc: First line for the address

fields.name: StreetLine2
fields.doc: Second line for the address

fields.name: PostCode
fields.doc: Address post code

types.name: OrderInfo
types.doc: This object represents information about an order.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Optional. User name

fields.name: PhoneNumber
fields.doc: Optional. User's phone number

fields.name: Email
fields.doc: Optional. User email

fields.name: ShippingAddress
fields.doc: Optional. User shipping address

types.name: ShippingOption
types.doc: This object represents one shipping option.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Shipping option identifier

fields.name: Title
fields.doc: Option title

fields.name: Prices
fields.doc: List of price portions

types.name: SuccessfulPayment
types.doc: This object contains basic information about a successful payment. Note that if the buyer initiates a chargeback with the relevant payment provider following this transaction, the funds may be debited from your balance. This is outside of Telegram's control.

fields.name: Currency
fields.doc: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code, or “XTR” for payments in Telegram Stars

fields.name: TotalAmount
fields.doc: Total price in the smallest units of the currency (integer, not float/double). For example, for a price of US$ 1.45 pass amount = 145. See the exp parameter in currencies.json, it shows the number of digits past the decimal point for each currency (2 for the majority of currencies).

fields.name: InvoicePayload
fields.doc: Bot-specified invoice payload

fields.name: SubscriptionExpirationDate
fields.doc: Optional. Expiration date of the subscription, in Unix time; for recurring payments only

fields.name: IsRecurring
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the payment is a recurring payment for a subscription

fields.name: IsFirstRecurring
fields.doc: Optional. True, if the payment is the first payment for a subscription

fields.name: ShippingOptionID
fields.doc: Optional. Identifier of the shipping option chosen by the user

fields.name: OrderInfo
fields.doc: Optional. Order information provided by the user

fields.name: TelegramPaymentChargeID
fields.doc: Telegram payment identifier

fields.name: ProviderPaymentChargeID
fields.doc: Provider payment identifier

types.name: RefundedPayment
types.doc: This object contains basic information about a refunded payment.

fields.name: Currency
fields.doc: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code, or “XTR” for payments in Telegram Stars. Currently, always “XTR”.

fields.name: TotalAmount
fields.doc: Total refunded price in the smallest units of the currency (integer, not float/double). For example, for a price of US$ 1.45, total_amount = 145. See the exp parameter in currencies.json, it shows the number of digits past the decimal point for each currency (2 for the majority of currencies).

fields.name: InvoicePayload
fields.doc: Bot-specified invoice payload

fields.name: TelegramPaymentChargeID
fields.doc: Telegram payment identifier

fields.name: ProviderPaymentChargeID
fields.doc: Optional. Provider payment identifier

types.name: ShippingQuery
types.doc: This object contains information about an incoming shipping query.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique query identifier

fields.name: From
fields.doc: User who sent the query

fields.name: InvoicePayload
fields.doc: Bot-specified invoice payload

fields.name: ShippingAddress
fields.doc: User specified shipping address

types.name: PreCheckoutQuery
types.doc: This object contains information about an incoming pre-checkout query.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique query identifier

fields.name: From
fields.doc: User who sent the query

fields.name: Currency
fields.doc: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code, or “XTR” for payments in Telegram Stars

fields.name: TotalAmount
fields.doc: Total price in the smallest units of the currency (integer, not float/double). For example, for a price of US$ 1.45 pass amount = 145. See the exp parameter in currencies.json, it shows the number of digits past the decimal point for each currency (2 for the majority of currencies).

fields.name: InvoicePayload
fields.doc: Bot-specified invoice payload

fields.name: ShippingOptionID
fields.doc: Optional. Identifier of the shipping option chosen by the user

fields.name: OrderInfo
fields.doc: Optional. Order information provided by the user

types.name: PaidMediaPurchased
types.doc: This object contains information about a paid media purchase.

fields.name: From
fields.doc: User who purchased the media

fields.name: PaidMediaPayload
fields.doc: Bot-specified paid media payload

types.name: RevenueWithdrawalState
types.doc: This object describes the state of a revenue withdrawal operation. Currently, it can be one of

types.name: RevenueWithdrawalStatePending
types.doc: The withdrawal is in progress.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the state, always “pending”

types.name: RevenueWithdrawalStateSucceeded
types.doc: The withdrawal succeeded.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the state, always “succeeded”

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date the withdrawal was completed in Unix time

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: An HTTPS URL that can be used to see transaction details

types.name: RevenueWithdrawalStateFailed
types.doc: The withdrawal failed and the transaction was refunded.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the state, always “failed”

types.name: AffiliateInfo
types.doc: Contains information about the affiliate that received a commission via this transaction.

fields.name: AffiliateUser
fields.doc: Optional. The bot or the user that received an affiliate commission if it was received by a bot or a user

fields.name: AffiliateChat
fields.doc: Optional. The chat that received an affiliate commission if it was received by a chat

fields.name: CommissionPerMille
fields.doc: The number of Telegram Stars received by the affiliate for each 1000 Telegram Stars received by the bot from referred users

fields.name: Amount
fields.doc: Integer amount of Telegram Stars received by the affiliate from the transaction, rounded to 0; can be negative for refunds

fields.name: NanostarAmount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of 1/1000000000 shares of Telegram Stars received by the affiliate; from -999999999 to 999999999; can be negative for refunds

types.name: TransactionPartner
types.doc: This object describes the source of a transaction, or its recipient for outgoing transactions. Currently, it can be one of

types.name: TransactionPartnerUser
types.doc: Describes a transaction with a user.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the transaction partner, always “user”

fields.name: TransactionType
fields.doc: Type of the transaction, currently one of “invoice_payment” for payments via invoices, “paid_media_payment” for payments for paid media, “gift_purchase” for gifts sent by the bot, “premium_purchase” for Telegram Premium subscriptions gifted by the bot, “business_account_transfer” for direct transfers from managed business accounts

fields.name: User
fields.doc: Information about the user

fields.name: Affiliate
fields.doc: Optional. Information about the affiliate that received a commission via this transaction. Can be available only for “invoice_payment” and “paid_media_payment” transactions.

fields.name: InvoicePayload
fields.doc: Optional. Bot-specified invoice payload. Can be available only for “invoice_payment” transactions.

fields.name: SubscriptionPeriod
fields.doc: Optional. The duration of the paid subscription. Can be available only for “invoice_payment” transactions.

fields.name: PaidMedia
fields.doc: Optional. Information about the paid media bought by the user; for “paid_media_payment” transactions only

fields.name: PaidMediaPayload
fields.doc: Optional. Bot-specified paid media payload. Can be available only for “paid_media_payment” transactions.

fields.name: Gift
fields.doc: Optional. The gift sent to the user by the bot; for “gift_purchase” transactions only

fields.name: PremiumSubscriptionDuration
fields.doc: Optional. Number of months the gifted Telegram Premium subscription will be active for; for “premium_purchase” transactions only

types.name: TransactionPartnerChat
types.doc: Describes a transaction with a chat.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the transaction partner, always “chat”

fields.name: Chat
fields.doc: Information about the chat

fields.name: Gift
fields.doc: Optional. The gift sent to the chat by the bot

types.name: TransactionPartnerAffiliateProgram
types.doc: Describes the affiliate program that issued the affiliate commission received via this transaction.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the transaction partner, always “affiliate_program”

fields.name: SponsorUser
fields.doc: Optional. Information about the bot that sponsored the affiliate program

fields.name: CommissionPerMille
fields.doc: The number of Telegram Stars received by the bot for each 1000 Telegram Stars received by the affiliate program sponsor from referred users

types.name: TransactionPartnerFragment
types.doc: Describes a withdrawal transaction with Fragment.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the transaction partner, always “fragment”

fields.name: WithdrawalState
fields.doc: Optional. State of the transaction if the transaction is outgoing

types.name: TransactionPartnerTelegramAds
types.doc: Describes a withdrawal transaction to the Telegram Ads platform.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the transaction partner, always “telegram_ads”

types.name: TransactionPartnerTelegramApi
types.doc: Describes a transaction with payment for paid broadcasting.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the transaction partner, always “telegram_api”

fields.name: RequestCount
fields.doc: The number of successful requests that exceeded regular limits and were therefore billed

types.name: TransactionPartnerOther
types.doc: Describes a transaction with an unknown source or recipient.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of the transaction partner, always “other”

types.name: StarTransaction
types.doc: Describes a Telegram Star transaction. Note that if the buyer initiates a chargeback with the payment provider from whom they acquired Stars (e.g., Apple, Google) following this transaction, the refunded Stars will be deducted from the bot's balance. This is outside of Telegram's control.

fields.name: ID
fields.doc: Unique identifier of the transaction. Coincides with the identifier of the original transaction for refund transactions. Coincides with SuccessfulPayment.telegram_payment_charge_id for successful incoming payments from users.

fields.name: Amount
fields.doc: Integer amount of Telegram Stars transferred by the transaction

fields.name: NanostarAmount
fields.doc: Optional. The number of 1/1000000000 shares of Telegram Stars transferred by the transaction; from 0 to 999999999

fields.name: Date
fields.doc: Date the transaction was created in Unix time

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Optional. Source of an incoming transaction (e.g., a user purchasing goods or services, Fragment refunding a failed withdrawal). Only for incoming transactions.

fields.name: Receiver
fields.doc: Optional. Receiver of an outgoing transaction (e.g., a user for a purchase refund, Fragment for a withdrawal). Only for outgoing transactions.

types.name: StarTransactions
types.doc: Contains a list of Telegram Star transactions.

fields.name: Transactions
fields.doc: The list of transactions

types.name: PassportData
types.doc: Describes Telegram Passport data shared with the bot by the user.

fields.name: Data
fields.doc: Array with information about documents and other Telegram Passport elements that was shared with the bot

fields.name: Credentials
fields.doc: Encrypted credentials required to decrypt the data

types.name: PassportFile
types.doc: This object represents a file uploaded to Telegram Passport. Currently all Telegram Passport files are in JPEG format when decrypted and don't exceed 10MB.

fields.name: FileID
fields.doc: Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the file

fields.name: FileUniqueID
fields.doc: Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the file.

fields.name: FileSize
fields.doc: File size in bytes

fields.name: FileDate
fields.doc: Unix time when the file was uploaded

types.name: EncryptedPassportElement
types.doc: Describes documents or other Telegram Passport elements shared with the bot by the user.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Element type. One of “personal_details”, “passport”, “driver_license”, “identity_card”, “internal_passport”, “address”, “utility_bill”, “bank_statement”, “rental_agreement”, “passport_registration”, “temporary_registration”, “phone_number”, “email”.

fields.name: Data
fields.doc: Optional. Base64-encoded encrypted Telegram Passport element data provided by the user; available only for “personal_details”, “passport”, “driver_license”, “identity_card”, “internal_passport” and “address” types. Can be decrypted and verified using the accompanying EncryptedCredentials.

fields.name: PhoneNumber
fields.doc: Optional. User's verified phone number; available only for “phone_number” type

fields.name: Email
fields.doc: Optional. User's verified email address; available only for “email” type

fields.name: Files
fields.doc: Optional. Array of encrypted files with documents provided by the user; available only for “utility_bill”, “bank_statement”, “rental_agreement”, “passport_registration” and “temporary_registration” types. Files can be decrypted and verified using the accompanying EncryptedCredentials.

fields.name: FrontSide
fields.doc: Optional. Encrypted file with the front side of the document, provided by the user; available only for “passport”, “driver_license”, “identity_card” and “internal_passport”. The file can be decrypted and verified using the accompanying EncryptedCredentials.

fields.name: ReverseSide
fields.doc: Optional. Encrypted file with the reverse side of the document, provided by the user; available only for “driver_license” and “identity_card”. The file can be decrypted and verified using the accompanying EncryptedCredentials.

fields.name: Selfie
fields.doc: Optional. Encrypted file with the selfie of the user holding a document, provided by the user; available if requested for “passport”, “driver_license”, “identity_card” and “internal_passport”. The file can be decrypted and verified using the accompanying EncryptedCredentials.

fields.name: Translation
fields.doc: Optional. Array of encrypted files with translated versions of documents provided by the user; available if requested for “passport”, “driver_license”, “identity_card”, “internal_passport”, “utility_bill”, “bank_statement”, “rental_agreement”, “passport_registration” and “temporary_registration” types. Files can be decrypted and verified using the accompanying EncryptedCredentials.

fields.name: Hash
fields.doc: Base64-encoded element hash for using in PassportElementErrorUnspecified

types.name: EncryptedCredentials
types.doc: Describes data required for decrypting and authenticating EncryptedPassportElement. See the Telegram Passport Documentation for a complete description of the data decryption and authentication processes.

fields.name: Data
fields.doc: Base64-encoded encrypted JSON-serialized data with unique user's payload, data hashes and secrets required for EncryptedPassportElement decryption and authentication

fields.name: Hash
fields.doc: Base64-encoded data hash for data authentication

fields.name: Secret
fields.doc: Base64-encoded secret, encrypted with the bot's public RSA key, required for data decryption

types.name: PassportElementError
types.doc: This object represents an error in the Telegram Passport element which was submitted that should be resolved by the user. It should be one of:

types.name: PassportElementErrorDataField
types.doc: Represents an issue in one of the data fields that was provided by the user. The error is considered resolved when the field's value changes.

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Error source, must be data

fields.name: Type

fields.doc: Name of the data field which has the error

fields.name: DataHash
fields.doc: Base64-encoded data hash

fields.name: Message

types.name: PassportElementErrorFrontSide
types.doc: Represents an issue with the front side of a document. The error is considered resolved when the file with the front side of the document changes.

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Error source, must be front_side

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: The section of the user's Telegram Passport which has the issue, one of “passport”, “driver_license”, “identity_card”, “internal_passport”

fields.name: FileHash
fields.doc: Base64-encoded hash of the file with the front side of the document

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Error message

types.name: PassportElementErrorReverseSide
types.doc: Represents an issue with the reverse side of a document. The error is considered resolved when the file with reverse side of the document changes.

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Error source, must be reverse_side

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: The section of the user's Telegram Passport which has the issue, one of “driver_license”, “identity_card”

fields.name: FileHash
fields.doc: Base64-encoded hash of the file with the reverse side of the document

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Error message

types.name: PassportElementErrorSelfie
types.doc: Represents an issue with the selfie with a document. The error is considered resolved when the file with the selfie changes.

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Error source, must be selfie

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: The section of the user's Telegram Passport which has the issue, one of “passport”, “driver_license”, “identity_card”, “internal_passport”

fields.name: FileHash
fields.doc: Base64-encoded hash of the file with the selfie

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Error message

types.name: PassportElementErrorFile
types.doc: Represents an issue with a document scan. The error is considered resolved when the file with the document scan changes.

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Error source, must be file

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: The section of the user's Telegram Passport which has the issue, one of “utility_bill”, “bank_statement”, “rental_agreement”, “passport_registration”, “temporary_registration”

fields.name: FileHash
fields.doc: Base64-encoded file hash

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Error message

types.name: PassportElementErrorFiles
types.doc: Represents an issue with a list of scans. The error is considered resolved when the list of files containing the scans changes.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: The section of the user's Telegram Passport which has the issue, one of “utility_bill”, “bank_statement”, “rental_agreement”, “passport_registration”, “temporary_registration”

fields.name: FileHashes
fields.doc: List of base64-encoded file hashes

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Error message

types.name: PassportElementErrorTranslationFile
types.doc: Represents an issue with one of the files that constitute the translation of a document. The error is considered resolved when the file changes.

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Error source, must be translation_file

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of element of the user's Telegram Passport which has the issue, one of “passport”, “driver_license”, “identity_card”, “internal_passport”, “utility_bill”, “bank_statement”, “rental_agreement”, “passport_registration”, “temporary_registration”

fields.name: FileHash
fields.doc: Base64-encoded file hash

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Error message

types.name: PassportElementErrorTranslationFiles
types.doc: Represents an issue with the translated version of a document. The error is considered resolved when a file with the document translation change.

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Error source, must be translation_files

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of element of the user's Telegram Passport which has the issue, one of “passport”, “driver_license”, “identity_card”, “internal_passport”, “utility_bill”, “bank_statement”, “rental_agreement”, “passport_registration”, “temporary_registration”

fields.name: FileHashes
fields.doc: List of base64-encoded file hashes

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Error message

types.name: PassportElementErrorUnspecified
types.doc: Represents an issue in an unspecified place. The error is considered resolved when new data is added.

fields.name: Source
fields.doc: Error source, must be unspecified

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of element of the user's Telegram Passport which has the issue

fields.name: ElementHash
fields.doc: Base64-encoded element hash

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Error message

types.name: Game
types.doc: This object represents a game. Use BotFather to create and edit games, their short names will act as unique identifiers.

fields.doc: Title of the game

fields.name: Description
fields.doc: Description of the game

fields.name: Photo

fields.name: Text
fields.doc: Optional. Brief description of the game or high scores included in the game message. Can be automatically edited to include current high scores for the game when the bot calls setGameScore, or manually edited using editMessageText. 0-4096 characters.

fields.name: TextEntities
fields.doc: Optional. Special entities that appear in text, such as usernames, URLs, bot commands, etc.

fields.name: Animation
fields.doc: Optional. Animation that will be displayed in the game message in chats. Upload via BotFather.

types.name: CallbackGame

types.name: GameHighScore
types.doc:
This object represents one row of the high scores table for a game.
And that's about all we've got for now.If you've got any questions, please check out our Bot FAQ »

fields.doc: Position in high score table for the game

fields.name: User
fields.doc: User

fields.name: Score
fields.doc: Score

methods.name: getUpdates
methods.doc:
Use this method to receive incoming updates using long polling (wiki). Returns an Array of Update objects.
Notes1. This method will not work if an outgoing webhook is set up.2. In order to avoid getting duplicate updates, recalculate offset after each server response.

params.name: Offset
params.doc: Identifier of the first update to be returned. Must be greater by one than the highest among the identifiers of previously received updates. By default, updates starting with the earliest unconfirmed update are returned. An update is considered confirmed as soon as getUpdates is called with an offset higher than its update_id. The negative offset can be specified to retrieve updates starting from -offset update from the end of the updates queue. All previous updates will be forgotten.

params.name: Limit
params.doc: Limits the number of updates to be retrieved. Values between 1-100 are accepted. Defaults to 100.

params.name: Timeout
params.doc: Timeout in seconds for long polling. Defaults to 0, i.e. usual short polling. Should be positive, short polling should be used for testing purposes only.

params.name: AllowedUpdates
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of the update types you want your bot to receive. For example, specify ["message", "edited_channel_post", "callback_query"] to only receive updates of these types. See Update for a complete list of available update types. Specify an empty list to receive all update types except chat_member, message_reaction, and message_reaction_count (default). If not specified, the previous setting will be used.Please note that this parameter doesn't affect updates created before the call to getUpdates, so unwanted updates may be received for a short period of time.

methods.name: setWebhook
methods.doc:
Use this method to specify a URL and receive incoming updates via an outgoing webhook. Whenever there is an update for the bot, we will send an HTTPS POST request to the specified URL, containing a JSON-serialized Update. In case of an unsuccessful request (a request with response HTTP status code different from 2XY), we will repeat the request and give up after a reasonable amount of attempts. Returns True on success.
If you'd like to make sure that the webhook was set by you, you can specify secret data in the parameter secret_token. If specified, the request will contain a header “X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token” with the secret token as content.
Notes1. You will not be able to receive updates using getUpdates for as long as an outgoing webhook is set up.2. To use a self-signed certificate, you need to upload your public key certificate using certificate parameter. Please upload as InputFile, sending a String will not work.3. Ports currently supported for webhooks: 443, 80, 88, 8443.
If you're having any trouble setting up webhooks, please check out this amazing guide to webhooks.

params.name: URL
params.doc: HTTPS URL to send updates to. Use an empty string to remove webhook integration.

params.name: Certificate
params.doc: Upload your public key certificate so that the root certificate in use can be checked. See our self-signed guide for details.

params.name: IPAddress
params.doc: The fixed IP address which will be used to send webhook requests instead of the IP address resolved through DNS

params.name: MaxConnections
params.doc: The maximum allowed number of simultaneous HTTPS connections to the webhook for update delivery, 1-100. Defaults to 40. Use lower values to limit the load on your bot's server, and higher values to increase your bot's throughput.

params.name: AllowedUpdates
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of the update types you want your bot to receive. For example, specify ["message", "edited_channel_post", "callback_query"] to only receive updates of these types. See Update for a complete list of available update types. Specify an empty list to receive all update types except chat_member, message_reaction, and message_reaction_count (default). If not specified, the previous setting will be used.Please note that this parameter doesn't affect updates created before the call to the setWebhook, so unwanted updates may be received for a short period of time.

params.name: DropPendingUpdates
params.doc: Pass True to drop all pending updates

params.name: SecretToken
params.doc: A secret token to be sent in a header “X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token” in every webhook request, 1-256 characters. Only characters A-Z, a-z, 0-9, _ and - are allowed. The header is useful to ensure that the request comes from a webhook set by you.

methods.name: deleteWebhook
methods.doc: Use this method to remove webhook integration if you decide to switch back to getUpdates. Returns True on success.

params.name: DropPendingUpdates
params.doc: Pass True to drop all pending updates

methods.name: getWebhookInfo
methods.doc: Use this method to get current webhook status. Requires no parameters. On success, returns a WebhookInfo object. If the bot is using getUpdates, will return an object with the url field empty.

methods.name: getMe
methods.doc: A simple method for testing your bot's authentication token. Requires no parameters. Returns basic information about the bot in form of a User object.

methods.name: logOut
methods.doc: Use this method to log out from the cloud Bot API server before launching the bot locally. You must log out the bot before running it locally, otherwise there is no guarantee that the bot will receive updates. After a successful call, you can immediately log in on a local server, but will not be able to log in back to the cloud Bot API server for 10 minutes. Returns True on success. Requires no parameters.

methods.name: close
methods.doc: Use this method to close the bot instance before moving it from one local server to another. You need to delete the webhook before calling this method to ensure that the bot isn't launched again after server restart. The method will return error 429 in the first 10 minutes after the bot is launched. Returns True on success. Requires no parameters.

methods.name: sendMessage
methods.doc: Use this method to send text messages. On success, the sent Message is returned.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message will be sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread (topic) of a forum; for forum supergroups and private chats of bots with forum topic mode enabled only

params.name: DirectMessagesTopicID
params.doc: Identifier of the direct messages topic to which the message will be sent; required if the message is sent to a direct messages chat

params.name: Text
params.doc: Text of the message to be sent, 1-4096 characters after entities parsing

params.name: ParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the message text. See formatting options for more details.

params.name: Entities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in message text, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

params.name: LinkPreviewOptions
params.doc: Link preview generation options for the message

params.name: DisableNotification
params.doc: Sends the message silently. Users will receive a notification with no sound.

params.name: ProtectContent
params.doc: Protects the contents of the sent message from forwarding and saving

params.name: AllowPaidBroadcast
params.doc: Pass True to allow up to 1000 messages per second, ignoring broadcasting limits for a fee of 0.1 Telegram Stars per message. The relevant Stars will be withdrawn from the bot's balance.

params.name: MessageEffectID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the message effect to be added to the message; for private chats only

params.name: SuggestedPostParameters
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object containing the parameters of the suggested post to send; for direct messages chats only. If the message is sent as a reply to another suggested post, then that suggested post is automatically declined.

params.name: ReplyParameters
params.doc: Description of the message to reply to

params.name: ReplyMarkup
params.doc: Additional interface options. A JSON-serialized object for an inline keyboard, custom reply keyboard, instructions to remove a reply keyboard or to force a reply from the user.

methods.name: forwardMessage
methods.doc: Use this method to forward messages of any kind. Service messages and messages with protected content can't be forwarded. On success, the sent Message is returned.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread (topic) of a forum; for forum supergroups and private chats of bots with forum topic mode enabled only

params.name: DirectMessagesTopicID
params.doc: Identifier of the direct messages topic to which the message will be forwarded; required if the message is forwarded to a direct messages chat

params.name: FromChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the chat where the original message was sent (or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username)

params.name: VideoStartTimestamp
params.doc: New start timestamp for the forwarded video in the message

params.name: DisableNotification
params.doc: Sends the message silently. Users will receive a notification with no sound.

params.name: ProtectContent
params.doc: Protects the contents of the forwarded message from forwarding and saving

params.name: MessageEffectID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the message effect to be added to the message; only available when forwarding to private chats

params.name: SuggestedPostParameters
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object containing the parameters of the suggested post to send; for direct messages chats only

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Message identifier in the chat specified in from_chat_id

methods.name: forwardMessages
methods.doc: Use this method to forward multiple messages of any kind. If some of the specified messages can't be found or forwarded, they are skipped. Service messages and messages with protected content can't be forwarded. Album grouping is kept for forwarded messages. On success, an array of MessageId of the sent messages is returned.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread (topic) of a forum; for forum supergroups and private chats of bots with forum topic mode enabled only

params.name: DirectMessagesTopicID
params.doc: Identifier of the direct messages topic to which the messages will be forwarded; required if the messages are forwarded to a direct messages chat

params.name: FromChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the chat where the original messages were sent (or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username)

params.name: MessageIds
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of 1-100 identifiers of messages in the chat from_chat_id to forward. The identifiers must be specified in a strictly increasing order.

params.name: DisableNotification
params.doc: Sends the messages silently. Users will receive a notification with no sound.

params.name: ProtectContent
params.doc: Protects the contents of the forwarded messages from forwarding and saving

methods.name: copyMessage
methods.doc: Use this method to copy messages of any kind. Service messages, paid media messages, giveaway messages, giveaway winners messages, and invoice messages can't be copied. A quiz poll can be copied only if the value of the field correct_option_id is known to the bot. The method is analogous to the method forwardMessage, but the copied message doesn't have a link to the original message. Returns the MessageId of the sent message on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: MessageID

params.name: VideoStartTimestamp
params.doc: New start timestamp for the copied video in the message

params.name: Caption
params.doc: New caption for media, 0-1024 characters after entities parsing. If not specified, the original caption is kept.

params.name: ParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the new caption. See formatting options for more details.

params.name: CaptionEntities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the new caption, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

params.name: ShowCaptionAboveMedia
params.doc: Pass True, if the caption must be shown above the message media. Ignored if a new caption isn't specified.

params.name: DisableNotification
params.doc: Sends the message silently. Users will receive a notification with no sound.

params.name: ProtectContent
params.doc: Protects the contents of the sent message from forwarding and saving

params.name: AllowPaidBroadcast
params.doc: Pass True to allow up to 1000 messages per second, ignoring broadcasting limits for a fee of 0.1 Telegram Stars per message. The relevant Stars will be withdrawn from the bot's balance.

params.name: MessageEffectID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the message effect to be added to the message; only available when copying to private chats

params.name: SuggestedPostParameters
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object containing the parameters of the suggested post to send; for direct messages chats only. If the message is sent as a reply to another suggested post, then that suggested post is automatically declined.

params.name: ReplyParameters
params.doc: Description of the message to reply to

params.name: ReplyMarkup
params.doc: Additional interface options. A JSON-serialized object for an inline keyboard, custom reply keyboard, instructions to remove a reply keyboard or to force a reply from the user.

methods.doc: Use this method to copy messages of any kind. If some of the specified messages can't be found or copied, they are skipped. A quiz poll can be copied only if the value of the field correct_option_id is known to the bot. methods.name: sendAudio
methods.doc:
Use this method to send audio files, if you want Telegram clients to display them in the music player. Your audio must be in the .MP3 or .M4A format. On success, the sent Message is returned. Bots can currently send audio files of up to 50 MB in size, this limit may be changed in the future.
For sending voice messages, use the sendVoice method instead.

A thumbnail's width and height should not exceed 320. Ignored if the file is not uploaded using multipart/form-data. Thumbnails can't be reused and can be only uploaded as a new file, so you can pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” if the thumbnail was uploaded using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name>. More information on Sending Files »

params.name: Duration
params.doc: Duration of sent animation in seconds

params.name: Width
params.doc: Animation width

params.name: Height
params.doc: Animation height

params.name: Thumbnail
params.doc: Thumbnail of the file sent; can be ignored if thumbnail generation for the file is supported server-side. The thumbnail should be in JPEG format and less than 200 kB in size. A thumbnail's width and height should not exceed 320. Ignored if the file is not uploaded using multipart/form-data. Thumbnails can't be reused and can be only uploaded as a new file, so you can pass “attach://<file_attach_name>” if the thumbnail was uploaded using multipart/form-data under <file_attach_name>. More information on Sending Files »

params.name: CaptionEntities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the caption, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

params.name: ShowCaptionAboveMedia
params.doc: Pass True, if the caption must be shown above the message media

params.name: HasSpoiler

params.doc: Sends the message silently. Users will receive a notification with no sound.

params.doc: Protects the contents of the sent message from forwarding and saving

params.doc: Pass True to allow up to 1000 messages per second, ignoring broadcasting limits for a fee of 0.1 Telegram Stars per message. The relevant Stars will be withdrawn from the bot's balance.

methods.name: sendVoice
methods.doc: Use this method to send audio files, if you want Telegram clients to display the file as a playable voice message. For this to work, your audio must be in an .OGG file encoded with OPUS, or in .MP3 format, or in .M4A format (other formats may be sent as Audio or Document). On success, the sent Message is returned. Bots can currently send voice messages of up to 50 MB in size, this limit may be changed in the future.

methods.name: sendPoll
methods.doc: Use this method to send a native poll. On success, the sent Message is returned.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message will be sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username. Polls can't be sent to channel direct messages chats.

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread (topic) of a forum; for forum supergroups and private chats of bots with forum topic mode enabled only

params.name: Question
params.doc: Poll question, 1-300 characters

params.name: QuestionParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the question. See formatting options for more details. Currently, only custom emoji entities are allowed.

params.name: QuestionEntities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the poll question. It can be specified instead of question_parse_mode.

params.name: Options
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of 1-12 answer options

params.name: IsAnonymous
params.doc: True, if the poll needs to be anonymous, defaults to True

params.name: Type
params.doc: Poll type, “quiz” or “regular”, defaults to “regular”

params.name: AllowsMultipleAnswers
params.doc: Pass True, if the poll allows multiple answers, defaults to False

params.name: AllowsRevoting
params.doc: Pass True, if the poll allows to change chosen answer options, defaults to False for quizzes and to True for regular polls

params.name: ShuffleOptions
params.doc: Pass True, if the poll options must be shown in random order

params.name: AllowAddingOptions
params.doc: Pass True, if answer options can be added to the poll after creation; not supported for anonymous polls and quizzes

params.name: HideResultsUntilCloses
params.doc: Pass True, if poll results must be shown only after the poll closes

params.name: MembersOnly
params.doc: Pass True, if voting is limited to users who have been members of the chat where the poll is being sent for more than 24 hours; for channel chats only

params.name: CountryCodes
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of 0-12 two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes indicating the countries from which users can vote in the poll; for channel chats only. Use “FT” as a country code to allow users with anonymous numbers to vote. If omitted or empty, then users from any country can participate in the poll.

params.name: CorrectOptionIds
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of monotonically increasing 0-based identifiers of the correct answer options, required for polls in quiz mode

params.name: Explanation
params.doc: Text that is shown when a user chooses an incorrect answer or taps on the lamp icon in a quiz-style poll, 0-200 characters with at most 2 line feeds after entities parsing

params.name: ExplanationParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the explanation. See formatting options for more details.

params.name: ExplanationEntities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the poll explanation. It can be specified instead of explanation_parse_mode.

params.name: ExplanationMedia
params.doc: Media added to the quiz explanation

params.name: OpenPeriod
params.doc: Amount of time in seconds the poll will be active after creation, 5-2628000. Can't be used together with close_date.

params.name: CloseDate
params.doc: Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the poll will be automatically closed. Must be at least 5 and no more than 2628000 seconds in the future. Can't be used together with open_period.

params.name: IsClosed
params.doc: Pass True if the poll needs to be immediately closed. This can be useful for poll preview.

params.name: Description
params.doc: Description of the poll to be sent, 0-1024 characters after entities parsing

params.name: DescriptionParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the poll description. See formatting options for more details.

params.name: DescriptionEntities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the poll description, which can be specified instead of description_parse_mode

params.name: Media
params.doc: Media added to the poll description

params.name: DisableNotification
params.doc: Sends the message silently. Users will receive a notification with no sound.

params.name: ProtectContent
params.doc: Protects the contents of the sent message from forwarding and saving

params.name: AllowPaidBroadcast
params.doc: Pass True to allow up to 1000 messages per second, ignoring broadcasting limits for a fee of 0.1 Telegram Stars per message. The relevant Stars will be withdrawn from the bot's balance.

params.name: MessageEffectID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the message effect to be added to the message; for private chats only

params.name: ReplyParameters
params.doc: Description of the message to reply to

params.name: ReplyMarkup
params.doc: Additional interface options. A JSON-serialized object for an inline keyboard, custom reply keyboard, instructions to remove a reply keyboard or to force a reply from the user.

methods.name: sendMessageDraft
methods.doc: Use this method to stream a partial message to a user while the message is being generated. Note that the streamed draft is ephemeral and acts as a temporary 30-second preview - once the output is finalized, you must call sendMessage with the complete message to persist it in the user's chat. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target private chat

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread

params.name: DraftID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the message draft; must be non-zero. Changes of drafts with the same identifier are animated.

params.name: Text
params.doc: Text of the message to be sent, 0-4096 characters after entities parsing. Pass an empty text to show a “Thinking…” placeholder.

params.name: ParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the message text. See formatting options for more details.

params.name: Entities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in message text, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

methods.name: sendChatAction
methods.doc:
Use this method when you need to tell the user that something is happening on the bot's side. The status is set for 5 seconds or less (when a message arrives from your bot, Telegram clients clear its typing status). Returns True on success.
Example: The ImageBot needs some time to process a request and upload the image. Instead of sending a text message along the lines of “Retrieving image, please wait…”, the bot may use sendChatAction with action = upload_photo. The user will see a “sending photo” status for the bot.
We only recommend using this method when a response from the bot will take a noticeable amount of time to arrive.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the action will be sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot or supergroup in the format @username. Channel chats and channel direct messages chats aren't supported.

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread or topic of a forum; for supergroups and private chats of bots with forum topic mode enabled only

params.name: Action
params.doc: Type of action to broadcast.
