Choose one, depending on what the user is about to receive: typing for text messages, upload_photo for photos, record_video or upload_video for videos, record_voice or upload_voice for voice notes, upload_document for general files, choose_sticker for stickers, find_location for location data, record_video_note or upload_video_note for video notes.

methods.name: setMessageReaction
methods.doc: Use this method to change the chosen reactions on a message. Service messages of some types can't be reacted to. Automatically forwarded messages from a channel to its discussion group have the same available reactions as messages in the channel. Bots can't use paid reactions. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Identifier of the target message. If the message belongs to a media group, the reaction is set to the first non-deleted message in the group instead.

params.name: Reaction
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of reaction types to set on the message. Currently, as non-premium users, bots can set up to one reaction per message. A custom emoji reaction can be used if it is either already present on the message or explicitly allowed by chat administrators. Paid reactions can't be used by bots.

params.name: IsBig
params.doc: Pass True to set the reaction with a big animation

methods.name: getUserProfilePhotos
methods.doc: Use this method to get a list of profile pictures for a user. Returns a UserProfilePhotos object.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

params.name: Offset
params.doc: Sequential number of the first photo to be returned. By default, all photos are returned.

params.name: Limit
params.doc: Limits the number of photos to be retrieved. Values between 1-100 are accepted. Defaults to 100.

methods.name: getUserProfileAudios
methods.doc: Use this method to get a list of profile audios for a user. Returns a UserProfileAudios object.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

params.name: Offset
params.doc: Sequential number of the first audio to be returned. By default, all audios are returned.

params.name: Limit
params.doc: Limits the number of audios to be retrieved. Values between 1-100 are accepted. Defaults to 100.

methods.name: setUserEmojiStatus
methods.doc: Changes the emoji status for a given user that previously allowed the bot to manage their emoji status via the Mini App method requestEmojiStatusAccess. Returns True on success.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

params.name: EmojiStatusCustomEmojiID
params.doc: Custom emoji identifier of the emoji status to set. Pass an empty string to remove the status.

params.name: EmojiStatusExpirationDate
params.doc: Expiration date of the emoji status, if any

methods.name: getFile
methods.doc:
Use this method to get basic information about a file and prepare it for downloading. For the moment, bots can download files of up to 20MB in size. On success, a File object is returned. The file can then be downloaded via the link https://api.telegram.org/file/bot<token>/<file_path>, where <file_path> is taken from the response. It is guaranteed that the link will be valid for at least 1 hour. When the link expires, a new one can be requested by calling getFile again.
Note: This function may not preserve the original file name and MIME type. You should save the file's MIME type and name (if available) when the File object is received.

params.name: FileID
params.doc: File identifier to get information about

methods.name: banChatMember
methods.doc: Use this method to ban a user in a group, a supergroup or a channel. In the case of supergroups and channels, the user will not be able to return to the chat on their own using invite links, etc., unless unbanned first. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target group or username of the target supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

params.name: UntilDate
params.doc: Date when the user will be unbanned; Unix time. If user is banned for more than 366 days or less than 30 seconds from the current time they are considered to be banned forever. Applied for supergroups and channels only.

params.name: RevokeMessages
params.doc: Pass True to delete all messages from the chat for the user that is being removed. If False, the user will be able to see messages in the group that were sent before the user was removed. Always True for supergroups and channels.

methods.name: unbanChatMember
methods.doc: Use this method to unban a previously banned user in a supergroup or channel. The user will not return to the group or channel automatically, but will be able to join via link, etc. The bot must be an administrator for this to work. By default, this method guarantees that after the call the user is not a member of the chat, but will be able to join it. So if the user is a member of the chat they will also be removed from the chat. If you don't want this, use the parameter only_if_banned. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target group or username of the target supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

params.name: OnlyIfBanned
params.doc: Do nothing if the user is not banned

methods.name: restrictChatMember
methods.doc: Use this method to restrict a user in a supergroup. The bot must be an administrator in the supergroup for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Pass True for all permissions to lift restrictions from a user. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

params.name: Permissions
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for new user permissions

params.name: UseIndependentChatPermissions
params.doc: Pass True if chat permissions are set independently. Otherwise, the can_send_other_messages and can_add_web_page_previews permissions will imply the can_send_messages, can_send_audios, can_send_documents, can_send_photos, can_send_videos, can_send_video_notes, and can_send_voice_notes permissions; the can_send_polls permission will imply the can_send_messages permission.

params.name: UntilDate
params.doc: Date when restrictions will be lifted for the user; Unix time. If user is restricted for more than 366 days or less than 30 seconds from the current time, they are considered to be restricted forever.

methods.name: promoteChatMember
methods.doc: Use this method to promote or demote a user in a supergroup or a channel. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Pass False for all boolean parameters to demote a user. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

params.name: IsAnonymous
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator's presence in the chat is hidden

params.name: CanManageChat
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can access the chat event log, get boost list, see hidden supergroup and channel members, report spam messages, ignore slow mode, and send messages to the chat without paying Telegram Stars. Implied by any other administrator privilege.

params.name: CanDeleteMessages
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can delete messages of other users

params.name: CanManageVideoChats
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can manage video chats

params.name: CanRestrictMembers
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can restrict, ban or unban chat members, or access supergroup statistics. For backward compatibility, defaults to True for promotions of channel administrators.

params.name: CanPromoteMembers
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can add new administrators with a subset of their own privileges or demote administrators that they have promoted, directly or indirectly (promoted by administrators that were appointed by him)

params.name: CanChangeInfo
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can change chat title, photo and other settings

params.name: CanInviteUsers
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can invite new users to the chat

params.name: CanPostStories
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can post stories to the chat

params.name: CanEditStories
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can edit stories posted by other users, post stories to the chat page, pin chat stories, and access the chat's story archive

params.name: CanDeleteStories
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can delete stories posted by other users

params.name: CanPostMessages
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can post messages in the channel, approve suggested posts, or access channel statistics; for channels only

params.name: CanEditMessages
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can edit messages of other users and can pin messages; for channels only

params.name: CanPinMessages
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can pin messages; for supergroups only

params.name: CanManageTopics
params.doc: Pass True if the user is allowed to create, rename, close, and reopen forum topics; for supergroups only

params.name: CanManageDirectMessages
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can manage direct messages within the channel and decline suggested posts; for channels only

params.name: CanManageTags
params.doc: Pass True if the administrator can edit the tags of regular members; for groups and supergroups only

methods.name: setChatAdministratorCustomTitle
methods.doc: Use this method to set a custom title for an administrator in a supergroup promoted by the bot. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

params.name: CustomTitle
params.doc: New custom title for the administrator; 0-16 characters, emoji are not allowed

methods.name: setChatMemberTag
methods.doc: Use this method to set a tag for a regular member in a group or a supergroup. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_manage_tags administrator right. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

params.name: Tag
params.doc: New tag for the member; 0-16 characters, emoji are not allowed

methods.name: banChatSenderChat
methods.doc: Use this method to ban a channel chat in a supergroup or a channel. Until the chat is unbanned, the owner of the banned chat won't be able to send messages on behalf of any of their channels. The bot must be an administrator in the supergroup or channel for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: SenderChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target sender chat

methods.name: unbanChatSenderChat
methods.doc: Use this method to unban a previously banned channel chat in a supergroup or channel. The bot must be an administrator for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: SenderChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target sender chat

methods.name: setChatPermissions
methods.doc: Use this method to set default chat permissions for all members. The bot must be an administrator in the group or a supergroup for this to work and must have the can_restrict_members administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: Permissions
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for new default chat permissions

params.name: UseIndependentChatPermissions
params.doc: Pass True if chat permissions are set independently. Otherwise, the can_send_other_messages and can_add_web_page_previews permissions will imply the can_send_messages, can_send_audios, can_send_documents, can_send_photos, can_send_videos, can_send_video_notes, and can_send_voice_notes permissions; the can_send_polls permission will imply the can_send_messages permission.

methods.name: exportChatInviteLink
methods.doc:
Use this method to generate a new primary invite link for a chat; any previously generated primary link is revoked. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns the new invite link as String on success.
Note: Each administrator in a chat generates their own invite links. Bots can't use invite links generated by other administrators. If you want your bot to work with invite links, it will need to generate its own link using exportChatInviteLink or by calling the getChat method. If your bot needs to generate a new primary invite link replacing its previous one, use exportChatInviteLink again.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

methods.name: createChatInviteLink
methods.doc: Use this method to create an additional invite link for a chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. The link can be revoked using the method revokeChatInviteLink. Returns the new invite link as ChatInviteLink object.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: Name
params.doc: Invite link name; 0-32 characters

params.name: ExpireDate
params.doc: Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the link will expire

params.name: MemberLimit
params.doc: The maximum number of users that can be members of the chat simultaneously after joining the chat via this invite link; 1-99999

params.name: CreatesJoinRequest
params.doc: True, if users joining the chat via the link need to be approved by chat administrators. If True, member_limit can't be specified.

methods.name: editChatInviteLink
methods.doc: Use this method to edit a non-primary invite link created by the bot. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns the edited invite link as a ChatInviteLink object.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: InviteLink
params.doc: The invite link to edit

params.name: Name
params.doc: Invite link name; 0-32 characters

params.name: ExpireDate
params.doc: Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the link will expire

params.name: MemberLimit
params.doc: The maximum number of users that can be members of the chat simultaneously after joining the chat via this invite link; 1-99999

params.name: CreatesJoinRequest
params.doc: True, if users joining the chat via the link need to be approved by chat administrators. If True, member_limit can't be specified.

methods.name: createChatSubscriptionInviteLink
methods.doc: Use this method to create a subscription invite link for a channel chat. The bot must have the can_invite_users administrator rights. The link can be edited using the method editChatSubscriptionInviteLink or revoked using the method revokeChatInviteLink. Returns the new invite link as a ChatInviteLink object.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target channel chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: Name
params.doc: Invite link name; 0-32 characters

params.name: SubscriptionPeriod
params.doc: The number of seconds the subscription will be active for before the next payment. Currently, it must always be 2592000 (30 days).

params.name: SubscriptionPrice
params.doc: The amount of Telegram Stars a user must pay initially and after each subsequent subscription period to be a member of the chat; 1-10000

methods.name: editChatSubscriptionInviteLink
methods.doc: Use this method to edit a subscription invite link created by the bot. The bot must have the can_invite_users administrator rights. Returns the edited invite link as a ChatInviteLink object.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: InviteLink
params.doc: The invite link to edit

params.name: Name
params.doc: Invite link name; 0-32 characters

methods.name: revokeChatInviteLink
methods.doc: Use this method to revoke an invite link created by the bot. If the primary link is revoked, a new link is automatically generated. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns the revoked invite link as ChatInviteLink object.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: InviteLink
params.doc: The invite link to revoke

methods.name: approveChatJoinRequest
methods.doc: Use this method to approve a chat join request. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_invite_users administrator right. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

methods.name: declineChatJoinRequest
methods.doc: Use this method to decline a chat join request. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_invite_users administrator right. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

methods.name: setChatPhoto
methods.doc: Use this method to set a new profile photo for the chat. Photos can't be changed for private chats. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: Photo
params.doc: New chat photo, uploaded using multipart/form-data

methods.name: deleteChatPhoto
methods.doc: Use this method to delete a chat photo. Photos can't be changed for private chats. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

methods.name: setChatTitle
methods.doc: Use this method to change the title of a chat. Titles can't be changed for private chats. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: Title
params.doc: New chat title, 1-128 characters

methods.name: setChatDescription
methods.doc: Use this method to change the description of a group, a supergroup or a channel. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: Description
params.doc: New chat description, 0-255 characters

methods.name: pinChatMessage
methods.doc: Use this method to add a message to the list of pinned messages in a chat. In private chats and channel direct messages chats, all non-service messages can be pinned. Conversely, the bot must be an administrator with the 'can_pin_messages' right or the 'can_edit_messages' right to pin messages in groups and channels respectively. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message will be pinned

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Identifier of a message to pin

params.name: DisableNotification
params.doc: Pass True if it is not necessary to send a notification to all chat members about the new pinned message. Notifications are always disabled in channels and private chats.

methods.name: unpinChatMessage
methods.doc: Use this method to remove a message from the list of pinned messages in a chat. In private chats and channel direct messages chats, all messages can be unpinned. Conversely, the bot must be an administrator with the 'can_pin_messages' right or the 'can_edit_messages' right to unpin messages in groups and channels respectively. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message will be unpinned

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Identifier of the message to unpin. Required if business_connection_id is specified. If not specified, the most recent pinned message (by sending date) will be unpinned.

methods.name: unpinAllChatMessages
methods.doc: Use this method to clear the list of pinned messages in a chat. In private chats and channel direct messages chats, no additional rights are required to unpin all pinned messages. Conversely, the bot must be an administrator with the 'can_pin_messages' right or the 'can_edit_messages' right to unpin all pinned messages in groups and channels respectively. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

methods.name: leaveChat
methods.doc: Use this method for your bot to leave a group, supergroup or channel. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup or channel in the format @username. Channel direct messages chats aren't supported; leave the corresponding channel instead.

methods.name: getChat
methods.doc: Use this method to get up-to-date information about the chat. Returns a ChatFullInfo object on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup or channel in the format @username

methods.name: getChatAdministrators
methods.doc: Use this method to get a list of administrators in a chat. Returns an Array of ChatMember objects.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: ReturnBots
params.doc: Pass True to additionally receive all bots that are administrators of the chat. By default, bots other than the current bot are omitted.

methods.name: getChatMemberCount
methods.doc: Use this method to get the number of members in a chat. Returns Int on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup or channel in the format @username

methods.name: getChatMember
methods.doc: Use this method to get information about a member of a chat. The method is only guaranteed to work for other users if the bot is an administrator in the chat. Returns a ChatMember object on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

methods.name: getUserPersonalChatMessages
methods.doc: Use this method to get the last messages from the personal chat (i.e., the chat currently added to their profile) of a given user. On success, an array of Message objects is returned.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target user

params.name: Limit
params.doc: The maximum number of messages to return; 1-20

methods.name: setChatStickerSet
methods.doc: Use this method to set a new group sticker set for a supergroup. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Use the field can_set_sticker_set optionally returned in getChat requests to check if the bot can use this method. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: StickerSetName
params.doc: Name of the sticker set to be set as the group sticker set

methods.name: deleteChatStickerSet
methods.doc: Use this method to delete a group sticker set from a supergroup. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Use the field can_set_sticker_set optionally returned in getChat requests to check if the bot can use this method. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

methods.name: getForumTopicIconStickers
methods.doc: Use this method to get custom emoji stickers, which can be used as a forum topic icon by any user. Requires no parameters. Returns an Array of Sticker objects.

methods.name: createForumTopic
methods.doc: Use this method to create a topic in a forum supergroup chat or a private chat with a user. In the case of a supergroup chat the bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_manage_topics administrator right. Returns information about the created topic as a ForumTopic object.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: Name
params.doc: Topic name, 1-128 characters

params.name: IconColor
params.doc: Color of the topic icon in RGB format. Currently, must be one of 7322096 (0x6FB9F0), 16766590 (0xFFD67E), 13338331 (0xCB86DB), 9367192 (0x8EEE98), 16749490 (0xFF93B2), or 16478047 (0xFB6F5F).

params.name: IconCustomEmojiID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the custom emoji shown as the topic icon. Use getForumTopicIconStickers to get all allowed custom emoji identifiers.

methods.name: editForumTopic
methods.doc: Use this method to edit name and icon of a topic in a forum supergroup chat or a private chat with a user. In the case of a supergroup chat the bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights, unless it is the creator of the topic. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread of the forum topic

params.name: Name
params.doc: New topic name, 0-128 characters. If not specified or empty, the current name of the topic will be kept.

params.name: IconCustomEmojiID
params.doc: New unique identifier of the custom emoji shown as the topic icon. Use getForumTopicIconStickers to get all allowed custom emoji identifiers. Pass an empty string to remove the icon. If not specified, the current icon will be kept.

methods.name: closeForumTopic
methods.doc: Use this method to close an open topic in a forum supergroup chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights, unless it is the creator of the topic. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread of the forum topic

methods.name: reopenForumTopic
methods.doc: Use this method to reopen a closed topic in a forum supergroup chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights, unless it is the creator of the topic. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread of the forum topic

methods.name: deleteForumTopic
methods.doc: Use this method to delete a forum topic along with all its messages in a forum supergroup chat or a private chat with a user. In the case of a supergroup chat the bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_delete_messages administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread of the forum topic

methods.name: unpinAllForumTopicMessages
methods.doc: Use this method to clear the list of pinned messages in a forum topic in a forum supergroup chat or a private chat with a user. In the case of a supergroup chat the bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_pin_messages administrator right in the supergroup. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread of the forum topic

methods.name: editGeneralForumTopic
methods.doc: Use this method to edit the name of the 'General' topic in a forum supergroup chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: Name
params.doc: New topic name, 1-128 characters

methods.name: closeGeneralForumTopic
methods.doc: Use this method to close an open 'General' topic in a forum supergroup chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

methods.name: reopenGeneralForumTopic
methods.doc: Use this method to reopen a closed 'General' topic in a forum supergroup chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights. The topic will be automatically unhidden if it was hidden. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

methods.name: hideGeneralForumTopic
methods.doc: Use this method to hide the 'General' topic in a forum supergroup chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights. The topic will be automatically closed if it was open. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

methods.name: unhideGeneralForumTopic
methods.doc: Use this method to unhide the 'General' topic in a forum supergroup chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

methods.name: unpinAllGeneralForumTopicMessages
methods.doc: Use this method to clear the list of pinned messages in a General forum topic. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the can_pin_messages administrator right in the supergroup. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

methods.name: answerCallbackQuery
methods.doc:
Use this method to send answers to callback queries sent from inline keyboards. The answer will be displayed to the user as a notification at the top of the chat screen or as an alert. On success, True is returned.
Alternatively, the user can be redirected to the specified Game URL. For this option to work, you must first create a game for your bot via @BotFather and accept the terms. Otherwise, you may use links like t.me/your_bot?start=XXXX that open your bot with a parameter.

params.name: CallbackQueryID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the query to be answered

params.name: Text
params.doc: Text of the notification. If not specified, nothing will be shown to the user, 0-200 characters.

params.name: ShowAlert
params.doc: If True, an alert will be shown by the client instead of a notification at the top of the chat screen. Defaults to false.

params.name: URL
params.doc: URL that will be opened by the user's client. If you have created a Game and accepted the conditions via @BotFather, specify the URL that opens your game - note that this will only work if the query comes from a callback_game button.Otherwise, you may use links like t.me/your_bot?start=XXXX that open your bot with a parameter.

params.name: CacheTime
params.doc: The maximum amount of time in seconds that the result of the callback query may be cached client-side. Telegram apps will support caching starting in version 3.14. Defaults to 0.

methods.name: answerGuestQuery
methods.doc: Use this method to reply to a received guest message. On success, a SentGuestMessage object is returned.

params.name: GuestQueryID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the query to be answered

params.name: Result
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object describing the message to be sent

methods.name: getUserChatBoosts
methods.doc: Use this method to get the list of boosts added to a chat by a user. Requires administrator rights in the chat. Returns a UserChatBoosts object.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the chat or username of the channel in the format @username

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

methods.name: getBusinessConnection
methods.doc: Use this method to get information about the connection of the bot with a business account. Returns a BusinessConnection object on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

methods.name: getManagedBotToken
methods.doc: Use this method to get the token of a managed bot. Returns the token as String on success.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: User identifier of the managed bot whose token will be returned

methods.name: replaceManagedBotToken
methods.doc: Use this method to revoke the current token of a managed bot and generate a new one. Returns the new token as String on success.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: User identifier of the managed bot whose token will be replaced

methods.name: getManagedBotAccessSettings
methods.doc: Use this method to get the access settings of a managed bot. Returns a BotAccessSettings object on success.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: User identifier of the managed bot whose access settings will be returned

methods.name: setManagedBotAccessSettings
methods.doc: Use this method to change the access settings of a managed bot. Returns True on success.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: User identifier of the managed bot whose access settings will be changed

params.name: IsAccessRestricted
params.doc: Pass True, if only selected users can access the bot. The bot's owner can always access it.

params.name: AddedUserIds
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of up to 10 identifiers of users who will have access to the bot in addition to its owner. Ignored if is_access_restricted is false.

methods.name: setMyCommands
methods.doc: Use this method to change the list of the bot's commands. See this manual for more details about bot commands. Returns True on success.

params.name: Commands
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of bot commands to be set as the list of the bot's commands. At most 100 commands can be specified.

params.name: Scope
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object, describing scope of users for which the commands are relevant. Defaults to BotCommandScopeDefault.

params.name: LanguageCode
params.doc: A two-letter ISO 639-1 language code. If empty, commands will be applied to all users from the given scope, for whose language there are no dedicated commands.

methods.name: deleteMyCommands
methods.doc: Use this method to delete the list of the bot's commands for the given scope and user language. After deletion, higher level commands will be shown to affected users. Returns True on success.

params.name: Scope
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object, describing scope of users for which the commands are relevant. Defaults to BotCommandScopeDefault.

params.name: LanguageCode
params.doc: A two-letter ISO 639-1 language code. If empty, commands will be applied to all users from the given scope, for whose language there are no dedicated commands.

methods.name: getMyCommands
methods.doc: Use this method to get the current list of the bot's commands for the given scope and user language. Returns an Array of BotCommand objects. If commands aren't set, an empty list is returned.

params.name: Scope
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object, describing scope of users. Defaults to BotCommandScopeDefault.

params.name: LanguageCode
params.doc: A two-letter ISO 639-1 language code or an empty string

methods.name: setMyName
methods.doc: Use this method to change the bot's name. Returns True on success.

params.name: Name
params.doc: New bot name; 0-64 characters. Pass an empty string to remove the dedicated name for the given language.

params.name: LanguageCode
params.doc: A two-letter ISO 639-1 language code. If empty, the name will be shown to all users for whose language there is no dedicated name.

methods.name: getMyName
methods.doc: Use this method to get the current bot name for the given user language. Returns BotName on success.

params.name: LanguageCode
params.doc: A two-letter ISO 639-1 language code or an empty string

methods.name: setMyDescription
methods.doc: Use this method to change the bot's description, which is shown in the chat with the bot if the chat is empty. Returns True on success.

params.name: Description
params.doc: New bot description; 0-512 characters. Pass an empty string to remove the dedicated description for the given language.

params.name: LanguageCode
params.doc: A two-letter ISO 639-1 language code. If empty, the description will be applied to all users for whose language there is no dedicated description.

methods.name: getMyDescription
methods.doc: Use this method to get the current bot description for the given user language. Returns BotDescription on success.

params.name: LanguageCode
params.doc: A two-letter ISO 639-1 language code or an empty string

methods.name: setMyShortDescription
methods.doc: Use this method to change the bot's short description, which is shown on the bot's profile page and is sent together with the link when users share the bot. Returns True on success.

params.name: ShortDescription
params.doc: New short description for the bot; 0-120 characters. Pass an empty string to remove the dedicated short description for the given language.

params.name: LanguageCode
params.doc: A two-letter ISO 639-1 language code. If empty, the short description will be applied to all users for whose language there is no dedicated short description.

methods.name: getMyShortDescription
methods.doc: Use this method to get the current bot short description for the given user language. Returns BotShortDescription on success.

params.name: LanguageCode
params.doc: A two-letter ISO 639-1 language code or an empty string

methods.name: setMyProfilePhoto
methods.doc: Changes the profile photo of the bot. Returns True on success.

params.name: Photo
params.doc: The new profile photo to set

methods.name: removeMyProfilePhoto
methods.doc: Removes the profile photo of the bot. Requires no parameters. Returns True on success.

methods.name: setChatMenuButton
methods.doc: Use this method to change the bot's menu button in a private chat, or the default menu button. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target private chat. If not specified, the bot's default menu button will be changed.

params.name: MenuButton
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for the bot's new menu button. Defaults to MenuButtonDefault.

methods.name: getChatMenuButton
methods.doc: Use this method to get the current value of the bot's menu button in a private chat, or the default menu button. Returns MenuButton on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target private chat. If not specified, the bot's default menu button will be returned.

methods.name: setMyDefaultAdministratorRights
methods.doc: Use this method to change the default administrator rights requested by the bot when it's added as an administrator to groups or channels. These rights will be suggested to users, but they are free to modify the list before adding the bot. Returns True on success.

params.name: Rights
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object describing new default administrator rights. If not specified, the default administrator rights will be cleared.

params.name: ForChannels
params.doc: Pass True to change the default administrator rights of the bot in channels. Otherwise, the default administrator rights of the bot for groups and supergroups will be changed.

methods.name: getMyDefaultAdministratorRights
methods.doc: Use this method to get the current default administrator rights of the bot. Returns ChatAdministratorRights on success.

params.name: ForChannels
params.doc: Pass True to get default administrator rights of the bot in channels. Otherwise, default administrator rights of the bot for groups and supergroups will be returned.

methods.name: getAvailableGifts
methods.doc: Returns the list of gifts that can be sent by the bot to users and channel chats. Requires no parameters. Returns a Gifts object.

methods.name: sendGift
methods.doc: Sends a gift to the given user or channel chat. The gift can't be converted to Telegram Stars by the receiver. Returns True on success.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Required if chat_id is not specified. Unique identifier of the target user who will receive the gift.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Required if user_id is not specified. Unique identifier for the chat or username of the channel (in the format @username) that will receive the gift.

params.name: GiftID
params.doc: Identifier of the gift; limited gifts can't be sent to channel chats

params.name: PayForUpgrade
params.doc: Pass True to pay for the gift upgrade from the bot's balance, thereby making the upgrade free for the receiver

params.name: Text
params.doc: Text that will be shown along with the gift; 0-128 characters

params.name: TextParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the text. See formatting options for more details. Entities other than “bold”, “italic”, “underline”, “strikethrough”, “spoiler”, “custom_emoji”, and “date_time” are ignored.

params.name: TextEntities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the gift text. It can be specified instead of text_parse_mode. Entities other than “bold”, “italic”, “underline”, “strikethrough”, “spoiler”, “custom_emoji”, and “date_time” are ignored.

methods.name: giftPremiumSubscription
methods.doc: Gifts a Telegram Premium subscription to the given user. Returns True on success.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user who will receive a Telegram Premium subscription

params.name: MonthCount
params.doc: Number of months the Telegram Premium subscription will be active for the user; must be one of 3, 6, or 12

params.name: StarCount
params.doc: Number of Telegram Stars to pay for the Telegram Premium subscription; must be 1000 for 3 months, 1500 for 6 months, and 2500 for 12 months

params.name: Text
params.doc: Text that will be shown along with the service message about the subscription; 0-128 characters

params.name: TextParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the text. See formatting options for more details. Entities other than “bold”, “italic”, “underline”, “strikethrough”, “spoiler”, “custom_emoji”, and “date_time” are ignored.

params.name: TextEntities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the gift text. It can be specified instead of text_parse_mode. Entities other than “bold”, “italic”, “underline”, “strikethrough”, “spoiler”, “custom_emoji”, and “date_time” are ignored.

methods.name: verifyUser
methods.doc: Verifies a user on behalf of the organization which is represented by the bot. Returns True on success.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

params.name: CustomDescription
params.doc: Custom description for the verification; 0-70 characters. Must be empty if the organization isn't allowed to provide a custom verification description.

methods.name: verifyChat
methods.doc: Verifies a chat on behalf of the organization which is represented by the bot. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username. Channel direct messages chats can't be verified.

params.name: CustomDescription
params.doc: Custom description for the verification; 0-70 characters. Must be empty if the organization isn't allowed to provide a custom verification description.

methods.name: removeUserVerification
methods.doc: Removes verification from a user who is currently verified on behalf of the organization represented by the bot. Returns True on success.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user

methods.name: removeChatVerification
methods.doc: Removes verification from a chat that is currently verified on behalf of the organization represented by the bot. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot or channel in the format @username

methods.name: readBusinessMessage
methods.doc: Marks incoming message as read on behalf of a business account. Requires the can_read_messages business bot right. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which to read the message

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the chat in which the message was received. The chat must have been active in the last 24 hours.

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the message to mark as read

methods.name: deleteBusinessMessages
methods.doc: Delete messages on behalf of a business account. Requires the can_delete_sent_messages business bot right to delete messages sent by the bot itself, or the can_delete_all_messages business bot right to delete any message. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which to delete the messages

params.name: MessageIds
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of 1-100 identifiers of messages to delete. All messages must be from the same chat. See deleteMessage for limitations on which messages can be deleted.

methods.name: setBusinessAccountName
methods.doc: Changes the first and last name of a managed business account. Requires the can_change_name business bot right. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: FirstName
params.doc: The new value of the first name for the business account; 1-64 characters

params.name: LastName
params.doc: The new value of the last name for the business account; 0-64 characters

methods.name: setBusinessAccountUsername
methods.doc: Changes the username of a managed business account. Requires the can_change_username business bot right. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: Username
params.doc: The new value of the username for the business account; 0-32 characters

methods.name: setBusinessAccountBio
methods.doc: Changes the bio of a managed business account. Requires the can_change_bio business bot right. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: Bio
params.doc: The new value of the bio for the business account; 0-140 characters

methods.name: setBusinessAccountProfilePhoto
methods.doc: Changes the profile photo of a managed business account. Requires the can_edit_profile_photo business bot right. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: Photo
params.doc: The new profile photo to set

params.name: IsPublic
params.doc: Pass True to set the public photo, which will be visible even if the main photo is hidden by the business account's privacy settings. An account can have only one public photo.

methods.name: removeBusinessAccountProfilePhoto
methods.doc: Removes the current profile photo of a managed business account. Requires the can_edit_profile_photo business bot right. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: IsPublic
params.doc: Pass True to remove the public photo, which is visible even if the main photo is hidden by the business account's privacy settings. After the main photo is removed, the previous profile photo (if present) becomes the main photo.

methods.name: setBusinessAccountGiftSettings
methods.doc: Changes the privacy settings pertaining to incoming gifts in a managed business account. Requires the can_change_gift_settings business bot right. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: ShowGiftButton
params.doc: Pass True, if a button for sending a gift to the user or by the business account must always be shown in the input field

params.name: AcceptedGiftTypes
params.doc: Types of gifts accepted by the business account

methods.name: getBusinessAccountStarBalance
methods.doc: Returns the amount of Telegram Stars owned by a managed business account. Requires the can_view_gifts_and_stars business bot right. Returns StarAmount on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

methods.name: transferBusinessAccountStars
methods.doc: Transfers Telegram Stars from the business account balance to the bot's balance. Requires the can_transfer_stars business bot right. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: StarCount
params.doc: Number of Telegram Stars to transfer; 1-10000

methods.name: getBusinessAccountGifts
methods.doc: Returns the gifts received and owned by a managed business account. Requires the can_view_gifts_and_stars business bot right. Returns OwnedGifts on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: ExcludeUnsaved
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that aren't saved to the account's profile page

params.name: ExcludeSaved
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that are saved to the account's profile page

params.name: ExcludeUnlimited
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that can be purchased an unlimited number of times

params.name: ExcludeLimitedUpgradable
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that can be purchased a limited number of times and can be upgraded to unique

params.name: ExcludeLimitedNonUpgradable
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that can be purchased a limited number of times and can't be upgraded to unique

params.name: ExcludeUnique
params.doc: Pass True to exclude unique gifts

params.name: ExcludeFromBlockchain
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that were assigned from the TON blockchain and can't be resold or transferred in Telegram

params.name: SortByPrice
params.doc: Pass True to sort results by gift price instead of send date. Sorting is applied before pagination.

params.name: Offset
params.doc: Offset of the first entry to return as received from the previous request; use empty string to get the first chunk of results

params.name: Limit
params.doc: The maximum number of gifts to be returned; 1-100. Defaults to 100.

methods.name: getUserGifts
methods.doc: Returns the gifts owned and hosted by a user. Returns OwnedGifts on success.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the user

params.name: ExcludeUnlimited
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that can be purchased an unlimited number of times

params.name: ExcludeLimitedUpgradable
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that can be purchased a limited number of times and can be upgraded to unique

params.name: ExcludeLimitedNonUpgradable
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that can be purchased a limited number of times and can't be upgraded to unique

params.name: ExcludeFromBlockchain
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that were assigned from the TON blockchain and can't be resold or transferred in Telegram

params.name: ExcludeUnique
params.doc: Pass True to exclude unique gifts

params.name: SortByPrice
params.doc: Pass True to sort results by gift price instead of send date. Sorting is applied before pagination.

params.name: Offset
params.doc: Offset of the first entry to return as received from the previous request; use an empty string to get the first chunk of results

params.name: Limit
params.doc: The maximum number of gifts to be returned; 1-100. Defaults to 100.

methods.name: getChatGifts
methods.doc: Returns the gifts owned by a chat. Returns OwnedGifts on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target channel in the format @username

params.name: ExcludeUnsaved
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that aren't saved to the chat's profile page. Always True, unless the bot has the can_post_messages administrator right in the channel.

params.name: ExcludeSaved
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that are saved to the chat's profile page. Always False, unless the bot has the can_post_messages administrator right in the channel.

params.name: ExcludeUnlimited
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that can be purchased an unlimited number of times

params.name: ExcludeLimitedUpgradable
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that can be purchased a limited number of times and can be upgraded to unique

params.name: ExcludeLimitedNonUpgradable
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that can be purchased a limited number of times and can't be upgraded to unique

params.name: ExcludeFromBlockchain
params.doc: Pass True to exclude gifts that were assigned from the TON blockchain and can't be resold or transferred in Telegram

params.name: ExcludeUnique
params.doc: Pass True to exclude unique gifts

params.name: SortByPrice
params.doc: Pass True to sort results by gift price instead of send date. Sorting is applied before pagination.

params.name: Offset
params.doc: Offset of the first entry to return as received from the previous request; use an empty string to get the first chunk of results

params.name: Limit
params.doc: The maximum number of gifts to be returned; 1-100. Defaults to 100.

methods.name: convertGiftToStars
methods.doc: Converts a given regular gift to Telegram Stars. Requires the can_convert_gifts_to_stars business bot right. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: OwnedGiftID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the regular gift that should be converted to Telegram Stars

methods.name: upgradeGift
methods.doc: Upgrades a given regular gift to a unique gift. Requires the can_transfer_and_upgrade_gifts business bot right. Additionally requires the can_transfer_stars business bot right if the upgrade is paid. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: OwnedGiftID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the regular gift that should be upgraded to a unique one

params.name: KeepOriginalDetails
params.doc: Pass True to keep the original gift text, sender and receiver in the upgraded gift

params.name: StarCount
params.doc: The amount of Telegram Stars that will be paid for the upgrade from the business account balance. If gift.prepaid_upgrade_star_count > 0, then pass 0, otherwise, the can_transfer_stars business bot right is required and gift.upgrade_star_count must be passed.

methods.name: transferGift
methods.doc: Transfers an owned unique gift to another user. Requires the can_transfer_and_upgrade_gifts business bot right. Requires can_transfer_stars business bot right if the transfer is paid. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: OwnedGiftID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the regular gift that should be transferred

params.name: NewOwnerChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the chat which will own the gift. The chat must be active in the last 24 hours.

params.name: StarCount
params.doc: The amount of Telegram Stars that will be paid for the transfer from the business account balance. If positive, then the can_transfer_stars business bot right is required.

methods.name: postStory
methods.doc: Posts a story on behalf of a managed business account. Requires the can_manage_stories business bot right. Returns Story on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: Content
params.doc: Content of the story

params.name: ActivePeriod
params.doc: Period after which the story is moved to the archive, in seconds; must be one of 6 * 3600, 12 * 3600, 86400, or 2 * 86400

params.name: Caption
params.doc: Caption of the story, 0-2048 characters after entities parsing

params.name: ParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the story caption. See formatting options for more details.

params.name: CaptionEntities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the caption, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

params.name: Areas
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of clickable areas to be shown on the story

params.name: PostToChatPage
params.doc: Pass True to keep the story accessible after it expires

params.name: ProtectContent
params.doc: Pass True if the content of the story must be protected from forwarding and screenshotting

methods.name: repostStory
methods.doc: Reposts a story on behalf of a business account from another business account. Both business accounts must be managed by the same bot, and the story on the source account must have been posted (or reposted) by the bot. Requires the can_manage_stories business bot right for both business accounts. Returns Story on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: FromChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the chat which posted the story that should be reposted

params.name: FromStoryID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the story that should be reposted

params.name: ActivePeriod
params.doc: Period after which the story is moved to the archive, in seconds; must be one of 6 * 3600, 12 * 3600, 86400, or 2 * 86400

params.name: PostToChatPage
params.doc: Pass True to keep the story accessible after it expires

params.name: ProtectContent
params.doc: Pass True if the content of the story must be protected from forwarding and screenshotting

methods.name: editStory
methods.doc: Edits a story previously posted by the bot on behalf of a managed business account. Requires the can_manage_stories business bot right. Returns Story on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: StoryID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the story to edit

params.name: Content
params.doc: Content of the story

params.name: Caption
params.doc: Caption of the story, 0-2048 characters after entities parsing

params.name: ParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the story caption. See formatting options for more details.

params.name: CaptionEntities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the caption, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

params.name: Areas
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of clickable areas to be shown on the story

methods.name: deleteStory
methods.doc: Deletes a story previously posted by the bot on behalf of a managed business account. Requires the can_manage_stories business bot right. Returns True on success.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection

params.name: StoryID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the story to delete

methods.name: answerWebAppQuery
methods.doc: Use this method to set the result of an interaction with a Web App and send a corresponding message on behalf of the user to the chat from which the query originated. On success, a SentWebAppMessage object is returned.

params.name: WebAppQueryID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the query to be answered

params.name: Result
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object describing the message to be sent

methods.name: savePreparedInlineMessage
methods.doc: Stores a message that can be sent by a user of a Mini App. Returns a PreparedInlineMessage object.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user that can use the prepared message

params.name: Result
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object describing the message to be sent

params.name: AllowUserChats
params.doc: Pass True if the message can be sent to private chats with users

params.name: AllowBotChats
params.doc: Pass True if the message can be sent to private chats with bots

params.name: AllowGroupChats
params.doc: Pass True if the message can be sent to group and supergroup chats

params.name: AllowChannelChats
params.doc: Pass True if the message can be sent to channel chats

methods.name: savePreparedKeyboardButton
methods.doc: Stores a keyboard button that can be used by a user within a Mini App. Returns a PreparedKeyboardButton object.

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the target user that can use the button

params.name: Button
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object describing the button to be saved. The button must be of the type request_users, request_chat, or request_managed_bot.

methods.name: editMessageText
methods.doc: Use this method to edit text and game messages. On success, if the edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that business messages that were not sent by the bot and do not contain an inline keyboard can only be edited within 48 hours from the time they were sent.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message to be edited was sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username.

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Identifier of the message to edit.

params.name: InlineMessageID
params.doc: Required if chat_id and message_id are not specified. Identifier of the inline message.

params.name: Text
params.doc: New text of the message, 1-4096 characters after entities parsing

params.name: ParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the message text. See formatting options for more details.

params.name: Entities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in message text, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

params.name: LinkPreviewOptions
params.doc: Link preview generation options for the message

params.name: ReplyMarkup
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for an inline keyboard

methods.name: editMessageCaption
methods.doc: Use this method to edit captions of messages. On success, if the edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that business messages that were not sent by the bot and do not contain an inline keyboard can only be edited within 48 hours from the time they were sent.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message to be edited was sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username.

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Identifier of the message to edit.

params.name: InlineMessageID
params.doc: Required if chat_id and message_id are not specified. Identifier of the inline message.

params.name: Caption
params.doc: New caption of the message, 0-1024 characters after entities parsing

params.name: ParseMode
params.doc: Mode for parsing entities in the message caption. See formatting options for more details.

params.name: CaptionEntities
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in the caption, which can be specified instead of parse_mode

params.name: ShowCaptionAboveMedia
params.doc: Pass True, if the caption must be shown above the message media. Supported only for animation, photo and video messages.

params.name: ReplyMarkup
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for an inline keyboard

methods.name: editMessageMedia
methods.doc: Use this method to edit animation, audio, document, live photo, photo, or video messages, or to add media to text messages. If a message is part of a message album, then it can be edited only to an audio for audio albums, only to a document for document albums and to a photo, a live photo, or a video otherwise. When an inline message is edited, a new file can't be uploaded; use a previously uploaded file via its file_id or specify a URL. On success, if the edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that business messages that were not sent by the bot and do not contain an inline keyboard can only be edited within 48 hours from the time they were sent.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message to be edited was sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username.

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Identifier of the message to edit.

params.name: InlineMessageID
params.doc: Required if chat_id and message_id are not specified. Identifier of the inline message.

params.name: Media
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for a new media content of the message

params.name: ReplyMarkup
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for a new inline keyboard

methods.name: editMessageLiveLocation
methods.doc: Use this method to edit live location messages. A location can be edited until its live_period expires or editing is explicitly disabled by a call to stopMessageLiveLocation. On success, if the edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message to be edited was sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username.

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Identifier of the message to edit.

params.name: InlineMessageID
params.doc: Required if chat_id and message_id are not specified. Identifier of the inline message.

params.name: Latitude
params.doc: Latitude of new location

params.name: Longitude
params.doc: Longitude of new location

params.name: LivePeriod
params.doc: New period in seconds during which the location can be updated, starting from the message send date. If 0x7FFFFFFF is specified, then the location can be updated forever. Otherwise, the new value must not exceed the current live_period by more than a day, and the live location expiration date must remain within the next 90 days. If not specified, then live_period remains unchanged.

params.name: HorizontalAccuracy
params.doc: The radius of uncertainty for the location, measured in meters; 0-1500

params.name: Heading
params.doc: Direction in which the user is moving, in degrees. Must be between 1 and 360 if specified.

params.name: ProximityAlertRadius
params.doc: The maximum distance for proximity alerts about approaching another chat member, in meters. Must be between 1 and 100000 if specified.

params.name: ReplyMarkup
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for a new inline keyboard

methods.name: stopMessageLiveLocation
methods.doc: Use this method to stop updating a live location message before live_period expires. On success, if the message is not an inline message, the edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message to be edited was sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username.

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Identifier of the message with live location to stop.

params.name: InlineMessageID
params.doc: Required if chat_id and message_id are not specified. Identifier of the inline message.

params.name: ReplyMarkup
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for a new inline keyboard

methods.name: editMessageChecklist
methods.doc: Use this method to edit a checklist on behalf of a connected business account. On success, the edited Message is returned.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message will be sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot in the format @username

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message

params.name: Checklist
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for the new checklist

params.name: ReplyMarkup
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for the new inline keyboard for the message

methods.name: editMessageReplyMarkup
methods.doc: Use this method to edit only the reply markup of messages. On success, if the edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that business messages that were not sent by the bot and do not contain an inline keyboard can only be edited within 48 hours from the time they were sent.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message to be edited was sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username.

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Required if inline_message_id is not specified. Identifier of the message to edit.

params.name: InlineMessageID
params.doc: Required if chat_id and message_id are not specified. Identifier of the inline message.

params.name: ReplyMarkup
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for an inline keyboard

methods.name: stopPoll
methods.doc: Use this method to stop a poll which was sent by the bot. On success, the stopped Poll is returned.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message to be edited was sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Identifier of the original message with the poll

params.name: ReplyMarkup
params.doc: A JSON-serialized object for a new message inline keyboard

methods.name: approveSuggestedPost
methods.doc: Use this method to approve a suggested post in a direct messages chat. The bot must have the 'can_post_messages' administrator right in the corresponding channel chat. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target direct messages chat

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Identifier of a suggested post message to approve

params.name: SendDate
params.doc: Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the post is expected to be published; omit if the date has already been specified when the suggested post was created. If specified, then the date must be not more than 2678400 seconds (30 days) in the future.

methods.name: declineSuggestedPost
methods.doc: Use this method to decline a suggested post in a direct messages chat. The bot must have the 'can_manage_direct_messages' administrator right in the corresponding channel chat. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target direct messages chat

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Identifier of a suggested post message to decline

params.name: Comment
params.doc: Comment for the creator of the suggested post; 0-128 characters

methods.name: deleteMessage
methods.doc: Use this method to delete a message, including service messages, with the following limitations:- A message can only be deleted if it was sent less than 48 hours ago.- Service messages about a supergroup, channel, or forum topic creation can't be deleted.- A dice message in a private chat can only be deleted if it was sent more than 24 hours ago.- Bots can delete outgoing messages in private chats, groups, and supergroups.- Bots can delete incoming messages in private chats.- Bots granted can_post_messages permissions can delete outgoing messages in channels.- If the bot is an administrator of a group, it can delete any message there.- If the bot has can_delete_messages administrator right in a supergroup or a channel, it can delete any message there.- If the bot has can_manage_direct_messages administrator right in a channel, it can delete any message in the corresponding direct messages chat.Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Identifier of the message to delete

methods.name: deleteMessages
methods.doc: Use this method to delete multiple messages simultaneously. If some of the specified messages can't be found, they are skipped. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: MessageIds
params.doc: A JSON-serialized list of 1-100 identifiers of messages to delete. See deleteMessage for limitations on which messages can be deleted.

methods.name: deleteMessageReaction
methods.doc: Use this method to remove a reaction from a message in a group or a supergroup chat. The bot must have the 'can_delete_messages' administrator right in the chat. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: MessageID
params.doc: Identifier of the target message

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Identifier of the user whose reaction will be removed, if the reaction was added by a user

params.name: ActorChatID
params.doc: Identifier of the chat whose reaction will be removed, if the reaction was added by a chat

methods.name: deleteAllMessageReactions
methods.doc: Use this method to remove up to 10000 recent reactions in a group or a supergroup chat added by a given user or chat. The bot must have the 'can_delete_messages' administrator right in the chat. Returns True on success.

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target supergroup in the format @username

params.name: UserID
params.doc: Identifier of the user whose reactions will be removed, if the reactions were added by a user

params.name: ActorChatID
params.doc: Identifier of the chat whose reactions will be removed, if the reactions were added by a chat

methods.name: sendSticker
methods.doc: Use this method to send static .WEBP, animated .TGS, or video .WEBM stickers. On success, the sent Message is returned.

params.name: BusinessConnectionID
params.doc: Unique identifier of the business connection on behalf of which the message will be sent

params.name: ChatID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target chat or username of the target bot, supergroup or channel in the format @username

params.name: MessageThreadID
params.doc: Unique identifier for the target message thread (topic) of a forum; for forum supergroups and private chats of bots with forum topic mode enabled only

params.name: DirectMessagesTopicID
params.doc: Identifier of the direct messages topic to which the message will be sent; required if the message is sent to a direct messages chat

params.name: Sticker
params.doc: Sticker to send. Pass a file_id as String to send a file that exists on the Telegram servers (recommended), pass an HTTP URL as a String for Telegram to get a .WEBP sticker from the Internet, or upload a new .WEBP, .TGS, or .WEBM sticker using multipart/form-data.
